item.title: DAP Reviews Coalition Ties Over MACC Scandal — Unity Government Cracking
item.description:
Said by: DAP / Reuters

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-05

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 5 March 2026, DAP — the largest non-Malay party in Anwar’s ruling coalition — publicly announced it was “reviewing its role” in the Unity Government amid deepening rifts over the MACC scandal. DAP called for an independent inquiry into MACC’s alleged “corporate mafia” operations, saying the government’s task force “does not go far enough.” The announcement came after weeks of Bloomberg exposés about MACC chief Azam Baki’s shareholdings and alleged misconduct.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: DAP is the reformist backbone of Pakatan Harapan. When your most ideologically committed coalition partner starts publicly questioning whether to stay, the coalition is in serious trouble. DAP’s base voted for clean governance and institutional reform — not for defending MACC’s chief while Bloomberg publishes damaging exposés. If DAP leaves or distances itself, Anwar loses both his reform credentials and his parliamentary numbers, forcing deeper dependence on UMNO — the party PH was built to replace.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: DAP has been “reviewing” and “expressing frustration” for three years while voting with the government on every confidence motion and budget. The party accepted weaker cabinet portfolios, stayed silent on the Zahid DNAA, and swallowed the watered-down subsidy reform. “Reviewing ties” is DAP’s version of threatening to leave without actually leaving — it’s pressure politics, not principle. If DAP was serious about reform, it would have drawn red lines two years ago. Instead, it drew them after the Sabah wipeout made the electoral cost of loyalty undeniable.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: DAP “reviewed” its ties with Mahathir during PH 1.0, then watched helplessly as the Sheraton Move collapsed the government. The party has a pattern of expressing frustration through press statements while voting loyally in parliament. In 2023, DAP leaders privately complained about the Zahid DNAA but publicly defended coalition unity. The gap between DAP’s rhetoric and its actions has been consistent throughout PH 2.0.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: If you’re serious about reform, set public conditions and enforce them. If the MACC scandal doesn’t trigger an independent inquiry, walk. If it does, stay. But stop “reviewing ties” in the press while voting loyally in parliament — your voters can see the difference between leverage and theatre. Either be the reform party you claim to be, or stop pretending.

Sources: US News / Reuters | SCMP

item.title: DAPSY Chairman Warns MACC Creating 'Climate of Fear' — PH's Own Youth Wing Breaks Ranks on Enforcement Tactics
item.description:
Said by: Woo Kah Leong, DAPSY National Chairman

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-05

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 5 March 2026, DAPSY National Chairman YB Woo Kah Leong issued a formal seven-point press statement criticising the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) for conducting what he called disproportionate and intimidating investigations.

The key points of the statement:

  • DAPSY is “deeply concerned” about how MACC has conducted recent high-profile investigations
  • The individual being investigated is “neither a fugitive financier like Jho Low nor a powerful businessman linked to corporate mafia allegations”
  • The high-profile nature of the investigation “appears disproportionate and unnecessary”
  • MACC “must not engage in political witch-hunting against an ordinary citizen”
  • If enforcement powers are exercised selectively, “the Unity Government will inevitably bear the political consequences”
  • Anti-corruption efforts must “strengthen democracy and ensure good governance, instead of weakening it by creating the perception that government machinery is used to pressure or silence individuals” The statement is widely understood to refer to MACC’s search for a former aide to ex-Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli, identified in various reports as Chai. The MACC had launched a high-profile search operation that drew public attention and criticism.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This is not an opposition attack. This is DAP’s own youth wing — a key component of the ruling Pakatan Harapan coalition — publicly accusing the government’s anti-corruption agency of creating a “climate of fear” and engaging in “political witch-hunting.”

When your own coalition partner’s youth wing issues a formal press statement warning that MACC is selectively targeting individuals and intimidating ordinary citizens, you have a governance crisis. DAPSY is effectively saying what the opposition, civil society, and Bersih have been saying for weeks: the MACC under Azam Baki is out of control.

The statement also contains an implicit threat: if this continues, “the Unity Government will inevitably bear the political consequences.” DAP grassroots are already restless — a special congress is scheduled for July 12 where delegates will vote on whether party leaders should resign from government posts. This DAPSY statement signals that discontent is real and growing.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Let’s be clear about the absurdity here.

DAP is part of the government. DAP has ministers in Cabinet. The MACC reports to the Prime Minister, who leads the coalition DAP is part of.

If DAPSY genuinely believes MACC is engaging in political witch-hunting and creating a climate of fear, why is DAP still in government? Why hasn’t a single DAP minister demanded Azam Baki’s removal in Cabinet? Why is the statement carefully worded to avoid naming anyone — not Azam, not Anwar, not the individual being investigated?

This is a masterclass in having it both ways:

  • Criticise MACC to maintain reformist credentials with your base
  • But keep it vague enough that you don’t actually challenge the Prime Minister
  • Issue a press statement on Facebook instead of raising it in Parliament
  • Frame it as concern for “civil liberties” while your party continues to sit in a government that has done nothing about Azam Baki for months The Facebook comments under the post are revealing. One commenter nailed it: “If you all are not happy with the PM, divorce from PH and the people will demand for a snap election.” Another: “It’s beginning to feel like TBH 2.0” — a reference to Teoh Beng Hock, a DAP aide who died in MACC custody in 2009.

DAPSY wants credit for speaking up without paying any political price for it.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

  • 2009: Teoh Beng Hock. DAP political aide Teoh Beng Hock was found dead at the MACC headquarters in Shah Alam after being called in for questioning. The Royal Commission of Inquiry found that MACC officers had driven him to suicide through aggressive interrogation. DAP was in opposition then. Now they’re in government and the MACC is doing the same thing — intimidating individuals connected to political figures — and DAP’s response is a politely worded Facebook post.
  • 2022-2026: Azam Baki’s tenure. Azam has survived multiple controversies — the 2021 share ownership scandal, Bloomberg’s exposés on his RM800,000 shareholding and alleged corporate mafia collusion. At every turn, PH leaders who once demanded his removal have gone silent or defended him.
  • March 2026: DAP reviews coalition ties. Reuters reported on March 6 that DAP is reviewing its ties with the coalition over the MACC scandal. DAPSY’s statement preceded this by one day, suggesting the youth wing was the canary in the coal mine.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

DAPSY, you wrote: “The ultimate goals of anti-corruption efforts must be to strengthen democracy and ensure good governance, instead of weakening it.”

Beautiful words. Now do something.

Your party has ministers in Cabinet. Your party chairman Anthony Loke sits at the table where decisions about MACC are made. Your MPs have the numbers to demand accountability.

Issuing a Facebook statement about a “climate of fear” while your party leadership does nothing to stop it is not courage. It’s brand management. It’s performative dissent designed to give DAP voters just enough to hold on to, while the party continues to enable the very thing it claims to oppose.

If MACC is engaging in political witch-hunting — your words, not ours — then your party has a moral obligation to act, not post.

Sources: DAP Malaysia (Facebook) | FocusMalaysia | FMT (Instagram)

item.title: Fahmi Blames 'Opposition Confusion' for PM Tenure Bill Failure — When 8 of His OWN MPs Didn't Show Up
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-05

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On March 5, 2026, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil told reporters in Putrajaya that the constitutional amendment bill to limit the PM’s tenure to 10 years failed because opposition MPs were “confused.” He claimed several opposition lawmakers mistakenly believed the bill was designed to give PM Anwar Ibrahim a 10-year term in office, rather than to cap any future PM’s tenure. Fahmi said: “There were MPs from the opposition who gave feedback to one of the ministers saying they thought the vote that day was meant to give Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim a 10-year term as prime minister.” He did not identify which opposition MPs were allegedly confused. PAS secretary-general Takiyuddin Hassan hit back, calling the claim “irresponsible” and “misplaced.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The bill failed by just 2 votes — 146 in favour, 148 needed. The government commands 154 MPs. Eight government MPs were absent. The opposition had already announced it would abstain unless amendments were agreed upon. This means the bill failed entirely because the government couldn’t get its own people to show up. The math is simple: 154 minus 8 absent = 146. The opposition’s abstention was publicly announced in advance. There was no surprise. The failure is a whipping failure, not an opposition confusion problem. Blaming the opposition for being “confused” when the government had more than enough votes — if only its own MPs had bothered to attend — is a masterclass in deflection.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Let’s be clear about what happened: the government had 154 MPs — six more than the 148 needed. Eight government MPs didn’t show up. The opposition announced in advance it would abstain. The bill failed by exactly the number of absent government MPs. And Fahmi’s response? Blame the opposition for being confused. This is the Communications Minister — the person whose literal job is to communicate clearly — claiming that opposition MPs couldn’t understand what the bill was about. If that were true, it would be an indictment of the government’s own communication. If it’s false, it’s a convenient excuse to avoid accountability for a humiliating coordination failure. Fahmi also couldn’t name a single “confused” MP. Because the story isn’t about confusion — it’s about the government not having enough discipline to get 148 of its 154 MPs into the chamber for a vote it had three years to prepare for.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: This follows a well-established PH pattern of blaming others for self-inflicted failures:

  • When the Sabah election was lost, the blame went to “local factors” — not PH’s weak campaign
  • When manifesto promises went unmet, the blame went to “the mess we inherited” — three years later
  • When the MACC scandal erupted, Anwar blamed “Zionist conspiracies” and “foreign media” — not the agency’s own misconduct
  • When Bersih criticised MACC, Anwar called it “unfair” — not a legitimate concern The government that promised radical transparency can’t even take responsibility for its own attendance sheet.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You had the numbers. You didn’t have the discipline. That’s on you — not the opposition. 154 MPs. 148 needed. 8 absent. The arithmetic writes its own headline. No amount of spin about “confused” opposition MPs changes the fact that your coalition failed to deliver on your own reform agenda because your own MPs didn’t show up. If you can’t whip 148 out of 154 for a constitutional amendment you’ve been promising since 2018, what reform can you actually deliver? And if blaming opposition “confusion” is the best Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil can manage, perhaps the communication problem is on your side of the aisle.

Sources: Malaysiakini | The Rakyat Post | Malay Mail | Reuters | Malaysiakini (PAS response)

item.title: Loke Admits DAP Failed to Get Cabinet to Agree on MACC Royal Inquiry — Reform Party Can't Reform Its Own Government
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister and DAP Secretary-General

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-05

📌 What Happened

On March 5, 2026, DAP’s Central Executive Committee reiterated its call for a Royal Commission of Inquiry into allegations that the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission operated as a “corporate mafia” under chief Azam Baki. DAP Secretary-General and Transport Minister Anthony Loke had first proposed the RCI to Cabinet in late February, calling the government’s response to Bloomberg’s February 11 investigative report “inadequate.” But despite repeated public calls and CEC backing, Loke subsequently admitted that DAP could not persuade Cabinet to establish the RCI. Anwar’s political secretary Kamil Azmil Munawar dismissed the proposal, stating any action “must be grounded in established facts and credible evidence” — as if a Royal Commission was not itself a fact-finding body.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is DAP — the party that built its brand on anti-corruption — publicly admitting it cannot convince its own coalition government to investigate its own anti-corruption agency. The Bloomberg report alleged internal MACC documents showed a “corporate mafia” scheme for forced corporate takeovers. The AG’s task force reportedly found Azam Baki holds RM14 million in shares across nine companies. And DAP’s response — the supposed backbone of PH’s reform agenda — amounted to: we tried, we failed, but investigations are ongoing. That is not reform politics. That is coalition captivity.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Loke called the government’s response “inadequate” — and he IS the government. He sits in Cabinet. He is the secretary-general of the second-largest party in the ruling coalition. If DAP cannot secure an RCI with its 40 parliamentary seats and multiple Cabinet positions, then either DAP’s influence in this government is a fiction, or DAP is choosing not to push hard enough because it values coalition stability over institutional accountability. Either way, the reform mandate is dead. Loke’s consolation — that investigations are underway — is meaningless. The government referred the AG’s findings to the PSD disciplinary board, not to a public inquiry. The difference between a disciplinary board and an RCI is the difference between an HR meeting and a courtroom.

📜 Previous Cases

DAP has form on this. In the first Pakatan Harapan government (2018–2020), DAP repeatedly backed down on manifesto promises — ICERD ratification, UEC recognition, the Rome Statute — citing coalition politics. Now in power again with an even larger majority, the pattern repeats. The party that once marched against MACC’s lack of independence now cannot even get its own Cabinet to scrutinise MACC’s alleged corruption. Kit Siang would have written 17 press statements about this by now. Today’s DAP writes one carefully worded CEC resolution and calls it a day.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised Malaysia that PH would be different. That reform would not be sacrificed on the altar of coalition politics. That MACC would be cleaned up, not covered up. Now Bloomberg has published internal MACC documents alleging a corporate mafia, your own AG’s task force found the chief holding RM14 million in undisclosed shares, and your response is to send it to a disciplinary board and dismiss your own transport minister’s call for an RCI. If DAP cannot secure accountability for the anti-corruption agency, what exactly is DAP for?

Sources: Malaysiakini | The Star | Straits Times | CNA | Reuters

item.title: PKR Accused of Lobbying US Against Najib — The SAME Thing Anwar Calls a 'Plot' When Done to Him
item.description:
Said by: Shahidan Kassim (PN-Arau) / Malaysiakini Parliament Report

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-05

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In parliament, opposition MP Shahidan Kassim confronted Anwar Ibrahim with allegations that PKR had communicated with a US firm a decade ago to exert pressure on then-PM Najib Razak. Shahidan demanded Anwar explain the double standard: PKR lobbied foreign powers against Najib, but when Daim Zainuddin’s family allegedly engaged international PR firms, Anwar calls it a ‘plot to topple the government’ and launches police investigations.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This exposes the fundamental hypocrisy at the core of Anwar’s governance: foreign engagement is legitimate activism when PKR does it, but seditious conspiracy when anyone does it against Anwar. The same tactics, rebranded depending on who benefits.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar has spent weeks in parliament talking about an alleged ‘well-funded, strategically planned’ plot to destabilise his government involving international PR firms. He launched criminal investigations. He made dramatic parliamentary speeches. But when confronted with PKR’s own history of engaging foreign firms against a sitting PM, he deflected. The hypocrisy isn’t subtle — it’s structural.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: During the 1MDB scandal, Anwar and PKR actively courted international media and foreign governments to pressure Najib. That was celebrated as brave democracy activism. Now that Anwar IS the PM, the exact same playbook is relabelled as criminal conspiracy. In 2024, Anwar claimed Bersih protests were ‘illegal’ — the same protests that put him in power.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You can’t write the rulebook when you’re in opposition and then burn it when you’re in government. If lobbying foreign powers was legitimate when PKR did it against Najib, it’s legitimate when others do it against you. If it was wrong then, admit it. If it’s wrong now, explain why. You can’t have it both ways — but that’s exactly what PH keeps trying to do.

Sources: Malaysiakini — Parliament Report | The Star — Support Letters

item.title: [BM] Anwar Dakwa 'Kumpulan Zionis Terkemuka' Dalang Komplot Guling Kerajaan — Disaman Keluarga Daim
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim memberitahu Parlimen pada 3–4 Mac bahawa dakwaan komplot mengulingkan kerajaannya melibatkan “tokoh terkemuka berkaitan Zionis,” jurubank asing, dan agensi media antarabangsa. Beliau tidak menamakan sesiapa secara langsung tetapi berkata suspek telah melantik firma PR berpangkalan di UK untuk melancarkan “serangan terselaras” terhadap institusi negara sebelum pilihan raya seterusnya. Polis telah mengumumkan siasatan Seksyen 124B pada 27 Februari, difahami secara meluas menyasarkan keluarga bekas menteri kewangan Daim Zainuddin — khususnya balunya Na’imah Abdul Khalid dan anak-anak mereka. Pada 6 Mac, keluarga Daim memfailkan saman di Mahkamah Tinggi Kuala Lumpur terhadap Anwar, Ketua Polis Negara Mohd Khalid Ismail, dan kerajaan, menuntut pengisytiharan bahawa siasatan itu “tidak sah, bertentangan perlembagaan dan dicemari niat jahat.”

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini bukan sekadar pertikaian undang-undang — ia adalah ujian tekanan untuk kelayakan reformis Anwar. Orang yang menghabiskan berdekad-dekad mengecam tindakan berlebihan eksekutif, pertuduhan hasutan, dan siasatan bermotif politik kini menggunakan buku panduan yang sama: tuduhan kabur subversi asing, label berbaur seperti “Zionis,” dan siasatan polis di bawah Seksyen 124B yang sama yang digunakan terhadap sekutunya sendiri dalam pentadbiran terdahulu.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Kemunafikan itu gred perindustrian. Peguam Na’imah menyatakan dengan jelas: “Tidak ada satu perkataan pun tentang Zionisme atau Israel atau apa-apa berkaitan dengannya dalam keseluruhan pelan komunikasi.” Dokumen PR itu, kata mereka, adalah mengenai meyakinkan jurubank tentang daya maju kewangan keluarga Daim — komunikasi krisis biasa untuk keluarga yang menghadapi penyitaan aset. Anwar mereka-reka sudut Zionis di Parlimen, di mana beliau menikmati kekebalan undang-undang. Sementara itu, partinya sendiri melantik perunding asing untuk melobi terhadap perdana menteri Malaysia yang sedang berkuasa pada 2016.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Anwar mempunyai sejarah panjang menggunakan konspirasi apabila terdesak. Semasa krisis SPRM pada Februari-Mac 2026, beliau menuduh pengkritik sebagai sebahagian komplot oleh “golongan kaya” memanipulasi media. Beliau menolak gesaan Bersih untuk akauntabiliti SPRM sebagai “tidak adil.” Naratif “komplot asing” adalah pengalihan autoritarian klasik — dan ia adalah taktik tepat yang digunakan Najib semasa skandal 1MDB.

🎯 Seruan PH

Anda menjanjikan Malaysia Madani — tamadun dibina atas kepercayaan, akauntabiliti, dan kedaulatan undang-undang. Sebaliknya, anda menggunakan Parlimen sebagai pentas untuk melemparkan perkataan seperti “Zionis” dan “jurubank” tanpa menghasilkan secebis bukti. Jika melantik firma PR asing adalah hasutan, mulakan siasatan dengan projek Washington PKR 2016. Jika bukan, hentikan sandiwara, tarik balik bahasa berbaur, dan biarkan mahkamah melakukan tugasnya tanpa Perdana Menteri bertindak sebagai pendakwa, hakim, dan propagandis dari lantai Dewan Rakyat.

Sumber: Reuters | SCMP | The Star | Malaysiakini | MalaysiaNow | NST

item.title: [BM] Anwar Dakwa 'Kumpulan Zionis Terkemuka' Dalang Komplot Gulingkan Kerajaan — Disaman Keluarga Daim
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim memberitahu Parlimen pada 3–4 Mac bahawa dakwaan komplot menggulingkan kerajaannya melibatkan “tokoh berkait Zionis yang terkemuka,” jurubank asing, dan agensi media antarabangsa. Beliau tidak menamakan sesiapa secara langsung tetapi berkata suspek telah melantik firma perhubungan awam berpangkalan di UK untuk melancarkan “serangan terselaras” terhadap institusi negara sebelum pilihan raya akan datang. Polis telah mengumumkan siasatan Seksyen 124B pada 27 Februari, difahami secara meluas menyasar keluarga bekas menteri kewangan mendiang Daim Zainuddin — khususnya balu beliau Na’imah Abdul Khalid dan anak-anak lelaki mereka. Pada 6 Mac, keluarga Daim memfailkan saman di Mahkamah Tinggi Kuala Lumpur terhadap Anwar, Ketua Polis Negara Mohd Khalid Ismail, dan kerajaan, memohon perisytiharan bahawa siasatan itu “menyalahi undang-undang, bertentangan perlembagaan dan dicemari niat jahat.”

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini bukan sekadar pertikaian undang-undang — ia ujian tekanan terhadap kelayakan reformis Anwar. Orang yang menghabiskan beberapa dekad mengecam penggunaan kuasa berlebihan eksekutif, tuduhan hasutan, dan siasatan bermotif politik kini menggunakan buku panduan yang sama: dakwaan samar tentang pengkhianatan asing, label berbaur seperti “Zionis,” dan siasatan polis di bawah Seksyen 124B yang sama yang pernah digunakan terhadap sekutunya sendiri pada pentadbiran terdahulu. Saman itu memaksa mahkamah menentukan sama ada PM yang sedang memerintah boleh menuduh warganegara secara terbuka atas kesalahan setaraf khianat tanpa bukti dan kemudian menggunakan jentera negara untuk menyiasat mereka. Jika kerajaan Anwar tidak mampu membezakan antara bantahan sah dan pengkhianatan sebenar, ia tiada hak memanggil dirinya reformis.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Kemunafikan ini bertaraf perindustrian. Peguam Na’imah memanggil dakwaan itu “palsu, tidak masuk akal, dan keterlaluan” — dan kemudian MalaysiaNow mendedahkan bom: pada 2016, pemimpin PKR sendiri membincangkan untuk melantik konsultansi AS bagi melobi Washington dan menekan kerajaan Najib bagi pihak Anwar. Jadi apabila parti Anwar sendiri melakukannya, ia memperjuangkan keadilan. Apabila orang lain didakwa mengupah firma perhubungan awam, ia komplot Zionis untuk gulingkan kerajaan. Perkataan “Zionis” memikul beban retorik berat di sini — digunakan bukan sebagai deskriptor fakta tetapi sebagai label bermotif politik yang direka untuk membungkam penelitian di negara majoriti Islam. Merujuk firma perhubungan awam bukan hasutan. Jika ya, separuh Putrajaya akan disiasat.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang kerajaan menjerit “campur tangan asing” untuk menjatuhkan kredibiliti pengkritik dalam negeri. Pentadbiran Mahathir menuduh aktivis reformasi menerima sokongan asing pada akhir 1990-an. Kerajaan Najib menggunakan naratif sama terhadap Bersih, Sarawak Report, dan akhirnya terhadap Anwar sendiri. PH berjanji dalam manifesto 2018 untuk menamatkan siasatan bermotif politik dan memansuhkan undang-undang yang digunakan untuk membungkam suara bantahan — termasuk Akta Hasutan dan Seksyen 124B. Sebaliknya, kerajaan Anwar kini mempersenjatai seksyen yang sama terhadap keluarga Daim, aktivis antirasuah C4, pengulas politik, dan ahli akademik. Generasi reformasi melihat wira mereka sendiri mengulangi kitaran.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Anda janji Malaysia Madani — tamadun yang dibina atas kepercayaan, akauntabiliti, dan kedaulatan undang-undang. Sebaliknya, anda menggunakan Parlimen sebagai pentas untuk melemparkan perkataan seperti “Zionis” dan “jurubank” tanpa mengemukakan sehelai bukti. Anda menyaman warganegara kerana mempersoalkan anda sementara sejarah lobi parti anda sendiri adalah rekod awam. Jika melantik firma perhubungan awam asing adalah hasutan, mulakan siasatan dengan projek Washington PKR 2016. Jika bukan, berhenti berlakon, tarik balik bahasa berbaur itu, dan biarkan mahkamah melakukan tugas mereka tanpa Perdana Menteri bertindak sebagai pendakwa, hakim, dan propagandis dari lantai Dewan Rakyat.

Sumber: Reuters | SCMP | The Star | Malaysiakini | MalaysiaNow | NST

item.title: [BM] Anwar Dakwa 'Kumpulan Zionis Terkemuka' Dalang Komplot Gulingkan Kerajaan — Keluarga Daim Saman Balik
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim memberitahu Parlimen pada 3–4 Mac bahawa komplot yang didakwa untuk menjatuhkan kerajaannya melibatkan seorang “tokoh terkemuka berkait Zionis,” bankir asing, dan sebuah agensi media antarabangsa. Beliau tidak menamakan sesiapa secara langsung tetapi berkata suspek telah melantik firma PR berpangkalan di UK untuk melancarkan “serangan terkoordinasi” terhadap institusi negara sebelum pilihan raya akan datang. Polis telah mengumumkan siasatan di bawah Seksyen 124B pada 27 Februari, yang difahami umum menyasarkan keluarga bekas menteri kewangan arwah Daim Zainuddin — khususnya balu beliau Na’imah Abdul Khalid dan anak-anak lelaki mereka. Pada 6 Mac, keluarga Daim memfailkan saman di Mahkamah Tinggi Kuala Lumpur terhadap Anwar, Ketua Polis Negara Mohd Khalid Ismail, dan kerajaan, memohon pengisytiharan bahawa siasatan itu “menyalahi undang-undang, tidak berperlembagaan dan dicemari niat jahat.”

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Ini bukan sekadar pertikaian undang-undang — ini adalah ujian muktamad untuk kredibiliti reformis Anwar. Orang yang bertahun-tahun mengecam penyalahgunaan kuasa eksekutif, tuduhan hasutan, dan siasatan bermotif politik kini menggunakan playbook yang sama: dakwaan kabur tentang subversif asing, label provokatif seperti “Zionis,” dan siasatan polis di bawah Seksyen 124B yang sama digunakan terhadap sekutu beliau sendiri oleh pentadbiran sebelumnya. Saman ini memaksa mahkamah menentukan sama ada seorang PM yang sedang berkhidmat boleh menuduh rakyat secara terbuka atas kesalahan setaraf pengkhianatan tanpa bukti dan kemudian menggunakan jentera negara untuk menyiasat mereka. Kalau kerajaan Anwar tak boleh bezakan antara perbezaan pendapat dan subversif sebenar, jangan panggil diri reformis.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Hipokrasi ini bertaraf industri. Peguam Na’imah kata dakwaan itu “palsu, tidak masuk akal, dan keterlaluan” — dan kemudian MalaysiaNow dedahkan bom: pada 2016, pemimpin PKR sendiri berbincang untuk melantik firma konsultansi AS bagi melobi Washington dan menekan kerajaan Najib bagi pihak Anwar. Jadi bila parti Anwar sendiri buat, itu perjuangan keadilan. Bila orang lain didakwa lantik firma PR, itu komplot Zionis nak gulingkan kerajaan. Perkataan “Zionis” buat kerja retorik berat di sini — digunakan bukan sebagai penerangan fakta tetapi sebagai label bermotif politik yang direka untuk tutup mulut pengkritik dalam negara majoriti Muslim. Lantik firma PR bukan penderhakaan. Kalau ya, separuh Putrajaya patut disiasat.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Malaysia ada sejarah panjang kerajaan menjerit “campur tangan asing” untuk mendelegitimasi pengkritik domestik. Pentadbiran Mahathir menuduh aktivis reformasi mendapat sokongan asing pada akhir 1990-an. Kerajaan Najib menggunakan naratif yang sama terhadap Bersih, Sarawak Report, dan akhirnya terhadap Anwar sendiri. PH berjanji dalam manifesto 2018 untuk menamatkan siasatan bermotif politik dan memansuhkan undang-undang yang digunakan untuk membungkam perbezaan pendapat — termasuk Akta Hasutan dan Seksyen 124B. Sebaliknya, kerajaan Anwar kini mempersenjatakan seksyen yang sama terhadap keluarga Daim, aktivis antirasuah C4, pengulas politik, dan ahli akademik. Generasi reformasi menyaksikan wira mereka sendiri mengulangi kitaran.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Anda janji Malaysia Madani — tamadun dibina atas kepercayaan, akauntabiliti, dan kedaulatan undang-undang. Sebaliknya, anda gunakan Parlimen sebagai pentas untuk melemparkan perkataan seperti “Zionis” dan “bankir” tanpa satu pun bukti kukuh. Anda saman rakyat kerana mempersoalkan anda sementara sejarah lobi parti anda sendiri adalah rekod awam. Kalau lantik firma PR asing itu penderhakaan, mulakan siasatan dengan projek Washington PKR 2016. Kalau tidak, hentikan sandiwara, tarik balik bahasa provokatif, dan biarkan mahkamah buat kerja mereka tanpa Perdana Menteri bertindak sebagai pendakwa, hakim, dan propagandis dari lantai Dewan Rakyat.

Sumber: Reuters | SCMP | The Star | Malaysiakini | MalaysiaNow | NST

item.title: [BM] Fahmi Kata Rang Undang-Undang Pemisahan PG-PP Perlu 3 Bulan Lagi — Reformasi Utama PH Terus Ditangguhkan
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 4 Mac, Menteri Komunikasi Fahmi Fadzil mengumumkan bahawa rang undang-undang untuk memisahkan peranan Peguam Negara dan Pendakwa Raya memerlukan tiga bulan lagi penambahbaikan sebelum boleh dibentangkan. Jawatankuasa pilihan dipengerusikan Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said akan bermesyuarat pada 16 Mac untuk meneliti maklum balas daripada AP. Ini berlaku hanya sehari selepas kerajaan gagal meluluskan rang undang-undang had tempoh PM — satu lagi reformasi perlembagaan utama — dengan kekurangan dua undi daripada majoriti dua pertiga yang diperlukan. Pemisahan PG-PP adalah antara ikrar reformasi Anwar yang paling awal dan paling menonjol.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Pemisahan PG-PP bukan sekadar bagus untuk ada — ia adalah reformasi struktural yang paling berkait langsung dengan kebebasan pendakwaan dan integriti sistem perundangan Malaysia. Di bawah sistem semasa, Peguam Negara berkhidmat sebagai penasihat undang-undang utama kerajaan dan pendakwa raya tertinggi negara, mewujudkan konflik kepentingan yang dieksploitasi oleh setiap pentadbiran. PH berkempen secara jelas untuk membetulkan ini. Tiga tahun berkuasa, mereka masih menubuhkan jawatankuasa dan meminta lebih banyak masa. Dengan pilihan raya berpotensi beberapa bulan lagi, tetingkap untuk meluluskan pindaan perlembagaan dengan majoriti besar semakin sempit.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH mempunyai majoriti besar parlimen sejak 2022. Mereka mempunyai tiga tahun untuk merangka, memperhalusi, dan meluluskan rang undang-undang ini. Sebaliknya, mereka menubuhkan jawatankuasa pilihan, yang kini akan bermesyuarat untuk “meneliti maklum balas” — setara birokrasi menekan butang tunda pada penggera yang anda sendiri tetapkan. Rang undang-undang tempoh PM sudah gagal kerana kerajaan tidak mampu memastikan AP sendiri hadir mengundi. Kini rang undang-undang PG-PP perlu “tiga bulan lagi” kerja. Sementara itu, Peguam Negara Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar — orang yang peranan dwinya rang undang-undang ini sepatutnya direformasi — sebelum ini menarik balik pertuduhan rasuah terhadap Timbalan Perdana Menteri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi walaupun mahkamah mendapati bukti cukup untuk diteruskan. Konflik kepentingan itu bukan teori. Ia sedang membentuk keadilan Malaysia sekarang.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Ini corak berulang. Rang undang-undang had tempoh PM diumumkan pada Januari 2026 sebagai sebahagian pakej reformasi, dibentangkan pada Mac, dan gagal pada percubaan pertama. Akta Kebebasan Maklumat, juga dijanjikan, kekal dalam draf. Pemansuhan Akta Hasutan — ikrar PH sejak PRU14 — tidak pernah ditunaikan semasa kerajaan PH pertama dan kini telah ditinggalkan secara efektif. Agenda reformasi PH seperti tanah perkuburan niat baik: setiap ikrar diumumkan dengan gembar-gembur, diserahkan kepada jawatankuasa, ditangguhkan oleh “rundingan,” dan akhirnya ditelan oleh kitaran pilihan raya.

🎯 Seruan PH

Anda beritahu rakyat Malaysia bahawa tadbir urus Madani bermakna reformasi institusi, bukan sekadar slogan. Anda berkata pemisahan PG-PP akan menamatkan konflik kepentingan yang membenarkan kerajaan terdahulu mempersenjatai pendakwaan. Tiga tahun dan majoriti besar kemudian, Menteri Komunikasi anda meminta tiga bulan lagi. Tiga bulan akan jadi enam. Enam akan jadi “selepas pilihan raya.” Rakyat Malaysia pernah melihat buku panduan ini sebelum ini. Jika anda tidak mampu menunaikan reformasi yang dijanjikan dengan majoriti parlimen terbesar dalam satu generasi, anda tidak layak mendapat mandat lain untuk membuat janji sama lagi.

Sumber: The Malaysian Reserve | The Star | SCMP

item.title: [BM] Fahmi Kata Rang Undang-Undang Pemisahan PG-PP Perlukan 3 Bulan Lagi — Reformasi Utama PH Terus Ditangguhkan
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 4 Mac, Menteri Komunikasi Fahmi Fadzil mengumumkan bahawa rang undang-undang untuk memisahkan peranan Peguam Negara dan Pendakwa Raya memerlukan tiga bulan lagi penambahbaikan sebelum boleh dibentangkan. Jawatankuasa pilihan khas yang dipengerusikan Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said akan bersidang pada 16 Mac untuk meneliti maklum balas daripada ahli parlimen. Ini berlaku hanya sehari selepas kerajaan gagal meluluskan rang undang-undang had tempoh PM — satu lagi reformasi perlembagaan utama — kekurangan dua undi daripada majoriti dua pertiga yang diperlukan.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Pemisahan PG-PP bukan sesuatu yang bagus untuk ada — ia adalah reformasi struktur yang paling berkait langsung dengan kebebasan pendakwaan dan integriti sistem perundangan Malaysia. Di bawah sistem semasa, Peguam Negara berkhidmat sebagai penasihat undang-undang utama kerajaan dan pendakwa utama negara, mewujudkan konflik kepentingan semula jadi yang telah dieksploitasi oleh setiap pentadbiran. PH berkempen secara jelas untuk memperbaiki ini. Tiga tahun berkuasa, mereka masih membentuk jawatankuasa dan meminta lebih masa.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

PH mempunyai majoriti besar parlimen sejak 2022. Mereka mempunyai tiga tahun untuk merangka, menambah baik dan meluluskan rang undang-undang ini. Sebaliknya, mereka membentuk jawatankuasa pilihan khas, yang kini akan bersidang untuk “meneliti maklum balas” — setara birokrasi dengan menekan butang tidur pada penggera yang kamu sendiri tetapkan. Rang undang-undang had tempoh PM sudah gagal kerana kerajaan tidak dapat mengerahkan AP sendiri untuk datang mengundi. Kini rang undang-undang PG-PP memerlukan “tiga bulan lagi” kerja. Sementara itu, Peguam Negara sebelum ini menarik balik pertuduhan rasuah terhadap Timbalan Perdana Menteri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi walaupun mahkamah mendapati bukti mencukupi untuk meneruskan.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Ini adalah corak berulang. Rang undang-undang had tempoh PM diumumkan pada Januari 2026, dibentangkan pada Mac, dan gagal pada percubaan pertama. Akta Kebebasan Maklumat, juga dijanjikan, kekal dalam draf. Pemansuhan Akta Hasutan — ikrar tanda tangan PH dari PRU14 — tidak pernah disampaikan semasa kerajaan PH pertama dan kini ditinggalkan secara berkesan untuk diganti dengan undang-undang ucapan yang baru dan lebih ketat.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Kamu telah mempunyai lapan tahun dan dua mandat pilihan raya untuk meluluskan rang undang-undang ini. Kamu mempunyai majoriti besar. Kamu tidak perlukan jawatankuasa tiga bulan. Kamu perlukan kemahuan politik. Sama ada luluskannya sebelum pilihan raya seterusnya atau akui bahawa pemisahan PG/PP sentiasa menjadi hiasan manifesto, bukan keutamaan tadbir urus.

Sumber: The Malaysian Reserve | The Star | SCMP

item.title: [BM] MB Negeri Sembilan Akui Pemandunya Langgar Lampu Merah — 'Saya Tiada Pilihan, Tengok Jadual Facebook Saya'
item.description:
Said by: Aminuddin Harun, Menteri Besar of Negri Sembilan (PH-Lenggeng)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 4 Mac 2026, Menteri Besar Negeri Sembilan Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun secara terbuka mengakui bahawa kereta rasminya secara rutin melanggar lampu merah, selepas video tular menunjukkan sebuah kenderaan yang dipercayai miliknya menggunakan lampu strobo biru dan siren untuk melalui lampu merah di Jalan Rasah. Bercakap kepada wartawan selepas mesyuarat majlis mesyuarat kerajaan negeri, Aminuddin berkata: “Saya akui kami terpaksa melepasi lampu kadangkala, tetapi saya tiada pilihan, kerana kadangkala ada beberapa acara rasmi berturut-turut dalam sehari.” Dia menunjukkan laman Facebooknya sebagai bukti jadualnya yang padat.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Ini bukan tentang kesalahan trafik. Ini tentang pemimpin negeri PH secara terbuka mengisytiharkan bahawa peraturan jalan raya tidak terpakai kepadanya — dan merangkanya sebagai akomodasi yang munasabah untuk khidmat awam. Di negara di mana rakyat biasa menerima saman, mata demerit dan denda untuk kesalahan yang sama, seorang Menteri Besar secara santai memberitahu wartawan dia melakukannya secara kerap dan mengharapkan kefahaman. Mesejnya kepada orang ramai jelas: ada satu set peraturan untuk pemimpin dan satu lagi untuk semua orang lain.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Pembelaan Aminuddin luar biasa dalam keberanian. Dia tidak menafikan. Dia tidak berjanji untuk berhenti. Dia memberitahu rakyat Malaysia untuk semak jadual Facebooknya untuk memahami kenapa dia perlu melanggar undang-undang. Justifikasi ‘ada anggota polis dalam kereta’ bukan pembebasan — ia pengakuan bahawa peralatan negara digunakan untuk memudahkan pelanggaran peraturan oleh pihak berkuasa. Permintaan maaf yang berakhir dengan ’tetapi pemimpin negeri perlukan sedikit kelonggaran’ bukan permintaan maaf. Ia pengisytiharan keistimewaan.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang elit politik merawat undang-undang trafik sebagai cadangan. Konvoi VIP, penunggang dan siren telah dinormalisasi sehingga pemandu biasa dijangka mengalah. PH, dalam pembangkang, lantang tentang penyalahgunaan kuasa sedemikian. Kini dalam kerajaan, MB mereka sendiri melakukan perkara yang sama dan meminta simpati.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Kamu berkempen atas kerendahan hati. Kamu beritahu rakyat Malaysia era politik feudal dan budaya VIP sudah berakhir. Kini MB Negeri Sembilan kamu melanggar lampu merah dengan perlindungan polis dan memberitahu rakyat untuk tengok Facebook untuk memahami kenapa dia terlalu penting untuk berhenti di lampu trafik. Patuhi peraturan atau ubahnya melalui proses yang betul. Kamu tidak boleh melanggarnya dan memanggilnya khidmat awam.

Sumber: Malay Mail | The Star | FMT | NST

item.title: [BM] Timbalan Menteri Beritahu Parlimen Endowmen Hindu Mesti Di Peringkat Negeri — Perlembagaan Kata Sebaliknya
item.description:
Said by: R Yuneswaran, Deputy National Unity Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 4 Mac 2026, Timbalan Menteri Perpaduan Negara R Yuneswaran memberitahu Dewan Negara bahawa lembaga endowmen Hindu mesti ditubuhkan di peringkat negeri, mendakwa ini selaras dengan peruntukan perlembagaan di bawah Senarai Negeri Jadual Kesembilan. Beliau menjawab cadangan senator Dr RA Lingeshwaran untuk lembaga endowmen Hindu peringkat kebangsaan bagi mengawal selia isu kuil. Yuneswaran berkata kementerian akan “menyokong sepenuhnya” negeri menubuhkan lembaga mereka sendiri, merujuk model Pulau Pinang. Bekas ahli DUN Pulau Pinang Satees Muniandy dan bekas pengerusi Lembaga Endowmen Hindu Pulau Pinang P Ramasamy segera menegur timbalan menteri itu, menunjuk kepada Perkara 15(c) Senarai Persekutuan, yang secara jelas meletakkan endowmen Hindu di bawah bidang kuasa persekutuan — bukan negeri.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Seorang timbalan menteri memberi maklumat perlembagaan yang salah di Parlimen — dewan atasan badan perundangan negara. Ini bukan soal tafsiran atau undang-undang perlembagaan kelabu. Perkara 15(c) Senarai Persekutuan adalah jelas: endowmen Hindu terletak di bawah bidang kuasa persekutuan. Apabila seorang menteri yang bertanggungjawab untuk perpaduan negara tidak mampu merujuk peruntukan perlembagaan yang mengawal endowmen keagamaan komuniti minoriti dengan tepat, ia menandakan sama ada ketidakcekapan atau percubaan sengaja untuk mengalihkan tanggungjawab persekutuan kepada kerajaan negeri.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH berkempen sebagai gabungan yang akan memulihkan tadbir urus perlembagaan, melindungi komuniti minoriti, dan memastikan kepimpinan cekap dalam Kabinet. Sebaliknya, kita mempunyai timbalan menteri — daripada portfolio perpaduan negara, tidak kurang — yang tidak boleh bersusah payah membaca Senarai Persekutuan sebelum menjawab soalan di Dewan Negara. Perlembagaan tidak kabur mengenai perkara ini. Lembaga Endowmen Hindu Pulau Pinang yang Yuneswaran petik sebagai model peringkat negeri sebenarnya adalah badan persekutuan yang ditadbir negeri yang melapor kepada Parlimen. Yuneswaran bukan sahaja mendapat jawapan salah — beliau mendapat keseluruhan rangka kerja perlembagaan terbalik.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Ini sebahagian daripada corak lebih luas menteri dan timbalan menteri PH membuat kenyataan tidak tepat atau mengelirukan di Parlimen. Kerajaan Madani berulang kali menunjukkan kecenderungan menolak tanggungjawab persekutuan kepada kerajaan negeri apabila melibatkan isu komuniti minoriti, sambil pada masa sama memusatkan kuasa pada perkara yang melayani kepentingan politiknya.

🎯 Seruan PH

Anda beritahu rakyat Malaysia anda akan mentadbir dengan kecekapan, integriti, dan penghormatan terhadap perlembagaan. Timbalan menteri anda baru sahaja beritahu Dewan Negara bahawa endowmen Hindu adalah perkara negeri sedangkan Senarai Persekutuan berkata sebaliknya — hitam putih. Jika menteri anda tidak mampu membaca Jadual Kesembilan sebelum menjawab soalan parlimen, apakah sebenarnya standard tadbir urus yang anda tawarkan? Komuniti Hindu layak mendapat kerajaan yang tahu perlembagaannya sendiri. Betulkan rekod, perbetulkan Hansard, dan beritahu rakyat sama ada anda benar-benar berniat melaksanakan tanggungjawab persekutuan terhadap endowmen Hindu — atau anda hanya menolak tanggungjawab.

Sumber: FMT — Ex-Penang rep accuses deputy minister of misleading Parliament on Hindu boards

⚠️ Notis sumber tunggal: Laporan ini berdasarkan satu outlet yang boleh dipercayai (Free Malaysia Today). Tiada pengesahan bebas daripada outlet kedua ditemui pada masa penerbitan. Rujukan perlembagaan (Perkara 15(c), Senarai Persekutuan, Jadual Kesembilan) boleh disahkan secara bebas.

item.title: [BM] Timbalan Menteri Beritahu Parlimen Wakaf Hindu Mesti Di Peringkat Negeri — Perlembagaan Kata Sebaliknya
item.description:
Said by: R Yuneswaran, Deputy National Unity Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 4 Mac 2026, Timbalan Menteri Perpaduan Negara R Yuneswaran memberitahu Dewan Negara bahawa lembaga wakaf Hindu mesti ditubuhkan di peringkat negeri, mendakwa ini selaras dengan peruntukan perlembagaan di bawah Senarai Negeri Jadual Kesembilan. Beliau menjawab cadangan senator Dr RA Lingeshwaran untuk lembaga wakaf Hindu peringkat kebangsaan. Bekas ahli dewan undangan negeri Pulau Pinang Satees Muniandy dan bekas pengerusi Lembaga Wakaf Hindu Pulau Pinang P Ramasamy segera menegur timbalan menteri, menunjuk kepada Perkara 15(c) Senarai Persekutuan, yang secara jelas meletakkan wakaf Hindu di bawah bidang kuasa persekutuan — bukan negeri.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Seorang timbalan menteri memberikan maklumat yang salah dari segi perlembagaan di Parlimen — dewan atasan badan perundangan negara. Ini bukan soal tafsiran atau undang-undang perlembagaan yang kabur. Perkara 15(c) Senarai Persekutuan adalah jelas: wakaf Hindu terletak di bawah bidang kuasa persekutuan. Apabila menteri yang bertanggungjawab untuk perpaduan negara tidak dapat menyebut peruntukan perlembagaan yang mengawal wakaf agama komuniti minoriti dengan tepat, ia menandakan sama ada ketidakcekapan atau percubaan sengaja mengalihkan tanggungjawab persekutuan kepada kerajaan negeri.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

PH berkempen sebagai gabungan yang akan memulihkan tadbir urus berperlembagaan, melindungi komuniti minoriti dan memastikan kepimpinan yang cekap dalam Kabinet. Sebaliknya, kita mempunyai timbalan menteri — dari portfolio perpaduan negara, tidak kurang — yang tidak ambil peduli membaca Senarai Persekutuan sebelum menjawab soalan di Dewan Negara. Perlembagaan tidak kabur dalam perkara ini. Lembaga Wakaf Hindu Pulau Pinang yang Yuneswaran sebut sebagai model peringkat negeri sebenarnya adalah badan persekutuan yang ditadbir oleh negeri yang melapor kepada Parlimen. Yuneswaran bukan sahaja salah jawapan — dia membalikkan keseluruhan rangka kerja perlembagaan.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Kamu beritahu rakyat Malaysia kamu akan mentadbir dengan kecekapan, integriti dan hormat terhadap perlembagaan. Timbalan menteri kamu baru beritahu Dewan Negara bahawa wakaf Hindu adalah urusan negeri sedangkan Senarai Persekutuan kata sebaliknya — hitam di atas putih. Jika menteri kamu tak boleh baca Jadual Kesembilan sebelum menjawab soalan parlimen, apakah standard tadbir urus yang kamu tawarkan? Komuniti Hindu berhak mendapat kerajaan yang tahu perlembagaannya sendiri. Betulkan rekod dan beritahu rakyat sama ada kamu benar-benar berniat melaksanakan tanggungjawab persekutuan atas wakaf Hindu — atau kamu hanya mengalih tanggungjawab.

Sumber: FMT — Bekas wakil rakyat Pulau Pinang tuduh timbalan menteri mengelirukan Parlimen soal lembaga Hindu

⚠️ Notis sumber tunggal: Laporan ini berdasarkan satu sumber yang sahih (Free Malaysia Today). Tiada pengesahan bebas daripada sumber kedua ditemui semasa penerbitan. Rujukan perlembagaan (Perkara 15(c), Senarai Persekutuan, Jadual Kesembilan) boleh disahkan secara bebas.

item.title: Anwar Claims 'Prominent Zionist Group' Behind Plot to Topple Government — Gets Sued by Daim's Family for It
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 What Happened

Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told Parliament on March 3–4 that an alleged plot to topple his government involved a “prominent Zionist-linked figure,” foreign bankers, and an international media agency. He named no individual directly but said the suspects had engaged a UK-based PR firm to launch a “coordinated attack” on national institutions before the next election. Police had announced a Section 124B investigation on February 27, widely understood to target the family of late former finance minister Daim Zainuddin — specifically his widow Na’imah Abdul Khalid and their sons. On March 6, the Daim family filed a lawsuit at the Kuala Lumpur High Court against Anwar, Inspector-General of Police Mohd Khalid Ismail, and the government, seeking a declaration that the investigation is “unlawful, unconstitutional and tainted by bad faith.”

🔥 Why It Matters

This is not just a legal dispute — it is a stress test for Anwar’s reformist credentials. The man who spent decades denouncing executive overreach, sedition charges, and politically motivated investigations is now deploying the same playbook: vague allegations of foreign subversion, inflammatory labels like “Zionist,” and a police probe under the same Section 124B used against his own allies in previous administrations. The lawsuit forces the courts to adjudicate whether a sitting PM can publicly accuse citizens of treason-level offences without evidence and then use state machinery to investigate them. If Anwar’s government cannot distinguish between legitimate dissent and actual subversion, it has no business calling itself reformist.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The hypocrisy is industrial-grade. Na’imah’s lawyers called the allegations “false, preposterous, and outrageous” — and then MalaysiaNow dropped a bombshell: in 2016, PKR leaders themselves discussed engaging a US consultancy to lobby Washington and pressure the Najib government on Anwar’s behalf. So when Anwar’s own party did it, it was fighting for justice. When someone else allegedly hires a PR firm, it’s a Zionist plot to topple the government. The word “Zionist” is doing heavy rhetorical work here — deployed not as a factual descriptor but as a politically charged label designed to shut down scrutiny in a Muslim-majority country. Consulting a PR firm is not sedition. If it were, half of Putrajaya would be under investigation.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia has a long history of governments crying “foreign interference” to delegitimise domestic critics. Mahathir’s administration accused reformasi activists of foreign backing in the late 1990s. Najib’s government used the same narrative against Bersih, Sarawak Report, and eventually against Anwar himself. PH promised in its 2018 manifesto to end politically motivated investigations and repeal laws used to silence dissent — including the Sedition Act and Section 124B. Instead, Anwar’s government has now weaponised the very same section against the Daim family, C4 anti-corruption activists, political commentators, and academics. The reformasi generation is watching its own heroes repeat the cycle.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised Malaysia Madani — a civilisation built on trust, accountability, and the rule of law. Instead, you are using Parliament as a stage to throw around words like “Zionist” and “banker” without producing a shred of evidence. You are suing citizens for questioning you while your own party’s lobbying history is a matter of public record. If engaging a foreign PR firm is sedition, start the investigation with PKR’s 2016 Washington project. If it isn’t, drop the theatrics, withdraw the inflammatory language, and let the courts do their job without the Prime Minister acting as prosecutor, judge, and propagandist from the Dewan Rakyat floor.

Sources: Reuters | SCMP | The Star | Malaysiakini | MalaysiaNow | NST

item.title: Deputy Minister Tells Parliament Hindu Endowments Must Be at State Level — The Constitution Says Otherwise
item.description:
Said by: R Yuneswaran, Deputy National Unity Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 What Happened

On March 4, 2026, Deputy National Unity Minister R Yuneswaran told the Dewan Negara that Hindu endowments boards must be established at the state level, claiming this was in line with constitutional provisions under the State List of the Ninth Schedule. He was responding to a proposal by senator Dr RA Lingeshwaran for a national-level Hindu endowments board to regulate temple issues. Yuneswaran said the ministry would “fully support” states in setting up their own boards, citing Penang’s model. Former Penang assemblyman Satees Muniandy and former Penang Hindu Endowments Board chairman P Ramasamy immediately called out the deputy minister, pointing to Item 15(c) of the Federal List, which explicitly places Hindu endowments under federal — not state — jurisdiction.

🔥 Why It Matters

A deputy minister gave constitutionally incorrect information in Parliament — the upper house of the country’s legislature. This is not a matter of interpretation or grey-area constitutional law. Item 15(c) of the Federal List is unambiguous: Hindu endowments fall under federal purview. When a minister responsible for national unity cannot accurately cite the constitutional provisions governing a minority community’s religious endowments, it signals either incompetence or a deliberate attempt to deflect federal responsibility onto state governments — either of which erodes trust in PH’s commitment to institutional governance and minority rights.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH campaigned as the coalition that would restore constitutional governance, protect minority communities, and ensure competent leadership in Cabinet. Instead, we have a deputy minister — from the national unity portfolio, no less — who cannot be bothered to read the Federal List before answering a question in the Dewan Negara. The constitution is not ambiguous on this point. The Penang Hindu Endowments Board that Yuneswaran cited as a state-level model is, as Ramasamy pointed out, actually a federal body administered by the state that reports to Parliament. Yuneswaran didn’t just get the answer wrong — he got the entire constitutional framework backwards and then used it to justify inaction at the federal level.

📜 Previous Cases

This is part of a broader pattern of PH ministers and deputy ministers making inaccurate or misleading statements in Parliament. The Madani government has repeatedly shown a tendency to push federal responsibilities down to state governments when it comes to minority community issues, while simultaneously centralising power on matters that serve its political interests. Malaysian parliamentary history is littered with ministerial answers that misstate constitutional provisions — the difference is that PH promised to be better. The national unity ministry, specifically, has been criticised for being more focused on managing optics around racial and religious tensions than on delivering substantive policy outcomes for minority communities.

🎯 PH Callout

You told Malaysians you would govern with competence, integrity, and respect for the constitution. Your deputy minister just told the Dewan Negara that Hindu endowments are a state matter when the Federal List says otherwise — in black and white. If your ministers can’t even read the Ninth Schedule before answering parliamentary questions, what exactly is the standard of governance you’re offering? The Hindu community deserves a government that knows its own constitution. Fix the record, correct the Hansard, and tell the rakyat whether you actually intend to exercise federal responsibility over Hindu endowments — or whether you’re just passing the buck.

Sources: FMT — Ex-Penang rep accuses deputy minister of misleading Parliament on Hindu boards

⚠️ Single-source notice: This report is based on a single credible outlet (Free Malaysia Today). No independent corroboration from a second outlet was found at time of publication. The constitutional reference (Item 15(c), Federal List, Ninth Schedule) is independently verifiable.

item.title: Fahmi Says AG-PP Separation Bill Needs 3 More Months — PH's Flagship Reform Keeps Getting Pushed Back
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 What Happened

On March 4, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil announced that the bill to separate the Attorney General and Public Prosecutor roles requires three more months of refinement before it can be tabled. A select committee chaired by Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said will convene on March 16 to examine feedback from MPs. This came just one day after the government failed to pass the PM tenure limit bill — another flagship constitutional reform — falling two votes short of the required two-thirds majority. The AG-PP separation was one of Anwar’s earliest and most prominent reform pledges, first announced as part of his institutional reform package.

🔥 Why It Matters

The AG-PP separation is not a nice-to-have — it is the structural reform most directly connected to prosecutorial independence and the integrity of Malaysia’s legal system. Under the current system, the Attorney General serves as both the government’s chief legal advisor and the nation’s top prosecutor, creating an inherent conflict of interest that has been exploited by every administration. PH campaigned explicitly on fixing this. Three years into power, they are still forming committees and asking for more time. With elections potentially months away, the window for passing constitutional amendments with a supermajority is closing fast — and the MACC-Azam Baki crisis has made the case for an independent prosecutor more urgent than ever.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH has had a parliamentary supermajority since 2022. They have had three years to draft, refine, and pass this bill. Instead, they formed a select committee, which will now meet to “examine feedback” — the bureaucratic equivalent of hitting the snooze button on the alarm you set yourself. The PM tenure bill already failed because the government couldn’t even whip its own MPs to show up for a vote. Now the AG-PP bill needs “three more months” of work. Meanwhile, Attorney General Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar — the man whose dual role this bill is supposed to reform — previously withdrew corruption charges against Deputy Prime Minister Ahmad Zahid Hamidi despite a court finding enough evidence to proceed. The conflict of interest isn’t theoretical. It is actively shaping Malaysian justice right now, and PH is asking for another quarter to think about it.

📜 Previous Cases

This is a recurring pattern. The PM tenure limit bill was announced in January 2026 as part of a reform package, tabled in March, and failed on its first attempt. The Freedom of Information Act, also promised, remains in draft. The repeal of the Sedition Act — a signature PH pledge from GE14 — was never delivered during the first PH government and has now been effectively abandoned in favour of new restrictive speech laws. The Peaceful Assembly Act reforms are similarly stalled. PH’s reform agenda reads like a graveyard of good intentions: every pledge is announced with fanfare, delegated to a committee, delayed by “consultations,” and eventually overtaken by the election cycle.

🎯 PH Callout

You told Malaysians that Madani governance means institutional reform, not just slogans. You said the AG-PP separation would end the conflict of interest that allowed previous governments to weaponise prosecution. Three years and a supermajority later, your Communications Minister is asking for three more months. Three months will become six. Six will become “after the election.” Malaysians have seen this playbook before — they lived through it under BN for six decades. If you cannot deliver the reforms you promised with the largest parliamentary majority in a generation, you do not deserve another mandate to make the same promises again.

Sources: The Malaysian Reserve | The Star | SCMP

item.title: Negri Sembilan MB Admits His Driver Runs Red Lights — 'I Have No Choice, Look at My Facebook Schedule'
item.description:
Said by: Aminuddin Harun, Menteri Besar of Negri Sembilan (PH-Lenggeng)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 What Happened

On March 4, 2026, Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun publicly admitted that his official car routinely runs red lights, after a viral video showed a vehicle believed to be his using blue strobe lights and a siren to blast through a red light on Jalan Rasah. Speaking to reporters after a state executive council meeting, Aminuddin said: “I admit that we have to beat the light at times, but I have no choice, as there are sometimes several official events lined up in a day.” He pointed to his Facebook page as proof of his hectic schedule. He added that a police officer is always present in the car, and that his office has sought “necessary approvals from the relevant authorities.”

🔥 Why It Matters

This is not about a traffic violation. This is about a PH state leader publicly declaring that the rules of the road do not apply to him — and framing it as a reasonable accommodation for public service. In a country where ordinary Malaysians receive summonses, demerit points, and fines for the same offence, a Menteri Besar is casually telling reporters he does it regularly and expects understanding. The message to the public is unmistakable: there is one set of rules for leaders and another for everyone else. This is the exact culture of elite entitlement PH promised to dismantle.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Aminuddin’s defence is remarkable in its audacity. He didn’t deny it. He didn’t promise to stop. He told Malaysians to check his Facebook schedule to understand why he needs to break the law. The ‘I had a cop in the car’ justification is not exoneration — it’s an admission that the state apparatus is being used to facilitate rule-breaking by the powerful. If the MB’s schedule is so packed that traffic laws become optional, the answer is better scheduling, not blue strobes and sirens. An apology that ends with ‘but state leaders need some leeway’ is not an apology. It is a declaration of privilege.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia has a long history of political elites treating traffic laws as suggestions. VIP motorcades, outriders, and sirens have been normalised to the point where ordinary motorists are expected to yield or face consequences. In 2022, a viral video of a minister’s convoy forcing vehicles off the road drew widespread criticism. PH, in opposition, was vocal about such abuses of power. Now in government, their own MB is doing the same thing and asking for sympathy. The entitlement transcends party lines — it’s baked into how Malaysian political elites understand their own importance.

🎯 PH Callout

You campaigned on humility. You told Malaysians that the era of feudal politics and VIP culture was over. Your own Prime Minister once made a show of flying economy class and driving himself. Now your Negri Sembilan MB is running red lights with police cover and telling the rakyat to look at his Facebook to understand why he’s too important to stop at a traffic light. If you can’t even hold your state leaders to basic traffic laws, what hope is there for institutional reform? Follow the rules or change them through proper process. You don’t get to break them and call it public service.

Sources: Malay Mail | The Star | FMT | NST

item.title: [BM] Anwar Dakwa 'Komplot Zionis' Gulingkan Kerajaan — Keluarga Daim Saman, Panggil Ia 'Salah Guna Kuasa'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 3 Mac 2026, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim memberitahu parlimen bahawa seorang suspek dalam dakwaan komplot menggulingkan kerajaan telah mengupah firma perhubungan awam antarabangsa untuk melancarkan serangan terselaras bertujuan menjejaskan institusi negara sebelum pilihan raya umum seterusnya. Dalam laporan susulan oleh FMT dan Malay Mail, Anwar lebih jauh — mendakwa konspirasi yang didakwa itu melibatkan “Zionis,” kumpulan pelobi asing, bankir, dan agensi media antarabangsa, semuanya didorong oleh apa yang beliau sifatkan sebagai tentangan terhadap desakan antirasuah kerajaannya secara “besar-besaran.” Pada 6 Mac, keluarga bekas Menteri Kewangan mendiang Daim Zainuddin — yang balunya Na’imah Abdul Khalid dikenal pasti secara meluas sebagai sasaran siasatan — memfailkan saman terhadap Anwar, Ketua Polis Negara Mohd Khalid Ismail, dan kerajaan, memanggil siasatan itu “tidak sah” dan “dicemari niat jahat.”

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini bukan sekadar pertikaian undang-undang — ia petanda paling jelas bahawa kerajaan Anwar sanggup menggunakan bahasa ancaman eksistensial dan konspirasi untuk menjatuhkan keabsahan pembangkang politik dan penelitian masyarakat sivil. Dengan menyebut “Zionis” — istilah sarat makna dalam politik Malaysia — Anwar meminjam daripada buku panduan etno-nasionalis yang sama yang pernah dikecamnya di bawah Mahathir dan Najib. Saman balas keluarga Daim membingkai semula naratif: apa yang kerajaan panggil keselamatan negara, sasarannya panggil penganiayaan politik. Ini menetapkan duluan berbahaya di mana sebarang bentuk penglibatan luaran — mengupah firma PR, melibatkan media, berunding dengan pelobi — boleh ditafsir semula sebagai subversif di bawah Seksyen 124B, yang membawa hukuman sehingga 20 tahun penjara.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

Anwar Ibrahim — lelaki yang menghabiskan berdekad-dekad meraih sokongan media asing, pertubuhan hak asasi antarabangsa, dan kerajaan Barat untuk memperjuangkan nasibnya dari penjara — kini memberitahu rakyat Malaysia bahawa mengupah firma PR sama dengan pengkhianatan. Kemunafikan ini berskala perindustrian. Kelangsungan politiknya sendiri dibina atas advokasi asing dan kempen tekanan antarabangsa. Kini setelah berkuasa, alat yang sama menjadi bukti konspirasi “Zionis.” Na’imah Abdul Khalid secara terbuka bertanya soalan yang jelas: sejak bila mengupah firma PR menjadi menggulingkan kerajaan? Anwar tiada jawapan yang baik, kerana jawapannya akan mendedahkan tujuan sebenar siasatan ini — menggertak.

📜 Kes-kes Terdahulu

Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang perdana menteri menyeru komplot asing bayangan untuk membungkam pengkritik. Mahathir Mohamad menyalahkan “pedagang Yahudi” dan George Soros atas krisis kewangan 1997. Sekutu Najib Razak membingkai siasatan 1MDB sebagai percubaan disokong asing untuk menjejaskan kestabilan Malaysia. Anwar sendiri menjadi sasaran pendakwaan bermotif politik di bawah Mahathir dan Najib, dengan Liwat I dan II secara meluas dilihat sebagai alat penganiayaan politik berselindung di sebalik penguatkuasaan undang-undang. Buku panduan “komplot menggulingkan” adalah asas tadbir urus autoritarian Malaysia — dan Anwar, meskipun semua penjenamaan reformisnya, kini menjalankan permainan yang sama dari seberang meja.

🎯 Teguran Kepada PH

Anda janjikan reformasi. Anda janjikan akauntabiliti. Anda janjikan Malaysia tidak akan lagi melihat kerajaan yang menjenayahkan perbezaan pendapat dan mempersenjatakan penguatkuasaan undang-undang terhadap lawan politik. Tiga tahun kemudian, Perdana Menteri anda berdiri di parlimen menyeru “Zionis” untuk menjelaskan mengapa kerajaannya dikritik — dan polis anda menggunakan Seksyen 124B untuk menyiasat seorang balu kerana mengupah firma PR. Ini bukan reformasi. Ini buku panduan Najib dengan PR yang lebih baik. Jika PH tidak mampu membezakan antara pembangkangan politik yang sah dan rampasan kuasa sebenar, maka PH telah menjadi tepat apa yang pernah dilawannya.

Sumber: Reuters (3 Mac) | Reuters (6 Mac) | SCMP (9 Mac) | FMT | CNA | Malay Mail | Straits Times

item.title: [BM] Anwar Dakwa 'Komplot Zionis' Untuk Gulingkan Kerajaan — Keluarga Daim Saman, Kata 'Salah Guna Kuasa'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 3 Mac 2026, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim memberitahu parlimen bahawa suspek dalam dakwaan komplot untuk menjatuhkan kerajaan telah mengupah firma perhubungan awam antarabangsa bagi melancarkan serangan terancang bertujuan menjejaskan institusi negara sebelum pilihan raya umum akan datang. Dalam kenyataan susulan yang dilaporkan FMT dan Malay Mail, Anwar melangkah lebih jauh — mendakwa konspirasi itu melibatkan “Zionis,” kumpulan pelobi asing, bankir, dan agensi media antarabangsa, semuanya didorong oleh rintangan terhadap usaha anti-rasuah “berskala besar” kerajaannya. Pada 6 Mac, keluarga mendiang bekas Menteri Kewangan Daim Zainuddin — yang isterinya Na’imah Abdul Khalid dikenal pasti secara meluas sebagai sasaran siasatan — memfailkan saman terhadap Anwar, Ketua Polis Negara Mohd Khalid Ismail, dan kerajaan, mendakwa siasatan itu “menyalahi undang-undang” dan “berniat jahat.”
