item.title: Anwar Claims 'Prominent Zionist Group' Behind Plot to Topple Government — While His Own Anti-Graft Chief Faces RM14 Million Share Scandal
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 What Happened

On March 3, 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told parliament that police were investigating an alleged plot to topple his government, involving what he described as a “group of Malaysians leveraging the influence of the media and international institutions, including involving a prominent Zionist group.” He claimed the alleged conspirators had engaged an international PR firm and targeted Bloomberg as part of a coordinated campaign to undermine national institutions before GE16. He said the plot was triggered by “large-scale corruption investigations” by the MACC and had been running since August 2024. Documents linked to an international firm allegedly outlined strategies to mobilise foreign media and leverage overseas networks to pressure Malaysian institutions.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is the Prime Minister of a reformist government using parliament — not a party rally — to float a Zionist conspiracy theory as an explanation for why his anti-graft agency is under fire. The MACC scandal is not a foreign plot. It started with a Bloomberg investigative report on February 11 citing internal documents and witnesses, followed by growing public outrage over chief Azam Baki’s alleged RM14 million in undisclosed shareholdings. By redirecting attention to shadowy foreign actors and Zionists, Anwar is borrowing from the same playbook he once condemned under Najib and Mahathir — weaponising the “foreign interference” narrative to shield domestic governance failures.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The “plot” Anwar describes — foreign media reporting on a corruption scandal, civil society demanding accountability, and politicians asking questions in parliament — is called democracy. Bloomberg did not destabilise Malaysia; Azam Baki’s alleged share scandal did. The Zionist angle is particularly cynical: Anwar hosted Donald Trump in Malaysia just weeks after Trump visited Israel to declare “undivided support” for Netanyahu, and Anwar himself dismissed anti-Israel protesters at the time. MalaysiaNow reported that decade-old emails from PKR’s own 2016 “Washington project” showed the party doing exactly what Anwar now accuses others of — using foreign connections to pressure Putrajaya. The hypocrisy is industrial-grade.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysian PMs deploying the “foreign plot” card is a tradition older than Putrajaya itself. Najib blamed “foreign agents” when the 1MDB scandal broke. Mahathir accused “currency speculators” during the 1997 crisis. Anwar himself was the target of such conspiracy-mongering when he was jailed under Mahathir. That he is now the one spinning the same yarn — from the Prime Minister’s chair, no less — shows how quickly the reformist veneer peels off once you are the one being investigated. The Daim family has since sued the government over the Zionist allegations, turning Anwar’s parliamentary gambit into a legal liability.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised Malaysia Madani — integrity, transparency, accountability. Instead, when a credible international outlet published an investigative report on your anti-graft chief’s alleged corruption, your first instinct was to blame Zionists in parliament. You did not call for an RCI. You did not fire Azam Baki. You blamed Bloomberg and foreign conspiracies. This is not reform. This is Najib cosplay with better English.

Sources: Reuters | Bernama | The Star | FMT | MalaysiaNow | SCMP | Anadolu Agency

item.title: Anwar Claims 'Zionist Plot' to Topple Government — Daim Family Sues, Calls It 'Abuse of Power'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 What Happened

On March 3, 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told parliament that a suspect in an alleged plot to topple the government had engaged an international public relations firm to launch a coordinated attack aimed at undermining national institutions before the next general election. In a follow-up reported by FMT and Malay Mail, Anwar went further — claiming the alleged conspiracy involved “Zionists,” foreign lobbyist groups, bankers, and international media agencies, all driven by what he described as resistance to his government’s “mass scale” anti-corruption push. On March 6, the family of the late former Finance Minister Daim Zainuddin — whose widow Na’imah Abdul Khalid was widely identified as the investigation’s target — filed a lawsuit against Anwar, Inspector-General of Police Mohd Khalid Ismail, and the government, calling the probe “unlawful” and “tainted with bad faith.”

🔥 Why It Matters

This is not just a legal spat — it is the clearest sign yet that Anwar’s government is willing to deploy the language of existential threat and conspiracy to delegitimise political opponents and civil society scrutiny. By invoking “Zionists” — a loaded term in Malaysian politics — Anwar is borrowing from the same ethno-nationalist playbook he once condemned under Mahathir and Najib. The Daim family’s counter-lawsuit reframes the narrative: what the government calls national security, the targets call political persecution. This sets a dangerous precedent where any form of external engagement — hiring a PR firm, engaging media, consulting lobbyists — can be recast as subversion under Section 124B, which carries up to 20 years in prison.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar Ibrahim — the man who spent decades courting foreign media, international human rights organisations, and Western governments to champion his cause from prison — is now telling Malaysians that engaging a PR firm is tantamount to treason. The hypocrisy is industrial-grade. His own political survival was built on foreign advocacy and international pressure campaigns. Now that he’s in power, the same tools become evidence of a “Zionist” conspiracy. Na’imah Abdul Khalid publicly asked the obvious question: since when does hiring a PR firm constitute overthrowing a government? Anwar has no good answer, because the answer would expose the real purpose of this probe — intimidation.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia has a long history of prime ministers invoking shadowy foreign plots to silence critics. Mahathir Mohamad blamed “Jewish traders” and George Soros for the 1997 financial crisis. Najib Razak’s allies framed the 1MDB investigations as a foreign-backed attempt to destabilise Malaysia. Anwar himself was the target of politically motivated prosecutions under both Mahathir and Najib, with Sodomy I and II widely seen as tools of political persecution disguised as law enforcement. The “plot to topple” playbook is a staple of Malaysian authoritarian governance — and Anwar, despite all his reformist branding, is now running the same play from the other side of the desk.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised reformasi. You promised accountability. You promised Malaysia would never again see a government that criminalises dissent and weaponises law enforcement against political opponents. Three years in, your Prime Minister is standing in parliament invoking “Zionists” to explain why his government is being criticised — and your police are using Section 124B to investigate a widow for hiring a PR firm. This is not reform. This is the Najib playbook with better PR. If PH cannot tell the difference between legitimate political opposition and an actual coup, then PH has become exactly what it once fought against.

Sources: Reuters (March 3) | Reuters (March 6) | SCMP (March 9) | FMT | CNA | Malay Mail | Straits Times

item.title: Anwar Invokes 'Zionists' and 'Bankers' in Parliament to Smear Daim's Widow — Lawyers Call It an 'Outright Lie'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 What Happened

On March 3, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told the Dewan Rakyat that the alleged plot to destabilise his government — attributed to Na’imah Abdul Khalid, the widow of former Finance Minister Daim Zainuddin — involved “prominent Zionist groups” and international bankers. He claimed a UK-based PR firm had been engaged to launch a coordinated attack on national institutions. This came one day after MalaysiaNow revealed that PKR leaders, including figures close to Anwar, had themselves engaged a US consultant in 2016 to build White House pressure on then-PM Najib Razak. Na’imah’s lawyers, Rajesh Nagarajan and Sachpreetraj Singh, issued a statement the same day calling Anwar’s claims “false, preposterous, outrageous, and a blatant attempt to mislead Parliament.”

🔥 Why It Matters

Anwar used the most incendiary term in Malaysian political vocabulary — “Zionist” — under parliamentary immunity, where he cannot be sued. This is not casual rhetoric. In a Muslim-majority country where anti-Zionism carries enormous emotional weight, invoking Zionists to discredit a political opponent is a deliberate weaponisation of public sentiment. It is the same playbook PH once accused UMNO and PAS of running: using fear and identity to distract from institutional accountability. The timing is critical — Anwar is under pressure over the MACC abuse allegations, Bloomberg exposés, and growing calls from within his own coalition for real reform. The “Zionist plot” narrative reframes domestic accountability demands as foreign sabotage.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Na’imah’s lawyers stated unequivocally: “There is not one word about Zionism or Israel or anything related to it in the entire communications plan.” The PR document, they said, was about reassuring bankers of the Daim family’s financial viability — standard crisis communications for a family facing asset seizure. Anwar fabricated the Zionist angle in Parliament, where he enjoys legal immunity, knowing full well he would not repeat it outside. Meanwhile, his own party engaged foreign consultants to lobby against a sitting Malaysian prime minister in 2016. The hypocrisy is not subtle — it is structural. PKR engaging foreign firms to pressure Putrajaya was political strategy. Na’imah engaging a PR firm to protect her family’s banking relationships is a Zionist plot. The difference? One serves Anwar’s interests. The other threatens them.

📜 Previous Cases

Anwar has a long history of invoking conspiracy when cornered. During the MACC crisis in February-March 2026, he accused critics of being part of a plot by “wealthy elites” manipulating media. He dismissed Bersih’s call for MACC accountability as “unfair.” He told the public not to act as “prosecutor or judge” during the KK Mart firebombing crisis. The “foreign plot” narrative is a classic authoritarian deflection — and it is the exact tactic Najib used during the 1MDB scandal, when he blamed foreign media, George Soros, and opposition sabotage for the exposés. Anwar spent a decade mocking that playbook. Now he is running it.

🎯 PH Callout

You campaigned on transparency, accountability, and institutional reform. You told Malaysians that the days of smearing critics as foreign agents were over. You mocked Najib for blaming Soros. You mocked Mahathir for blaming “foreign speculators.” And now, when a widow’s lawyers publish a PR document that contains zero references to Zionism, you stand in Parliament — protected by immunity — and invoke “Zionists” and “bankers” to discredit her. You are not defending Malaysia from foreign interference. You are using the most loaded term in the country’s political lexicon to shut down legitimate scrutiny of your government’s conduct. That is not reform. That is not even competent spin. That is Najib-era politics with a reformasi sticker on it.

Sources: MalaysiaNow | Reuters | Malay Mail

item.title: PH's Own Term Limit Bill Falls 2 Votes Short — 32 MPs Absent, 44 Abstained, Zero Voted Against
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Pakatan Harapan Coalition

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 What Happened

On March 3, 2026, Malaysia’s parliament voted on the Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2026, which would have instituted a two-term, 10-year limit for prime ministers — one of Anwar Ibrahim’s flagship reform promises since taking office in November 2022. The bill needed a two-thirds supermajority of 148 votes. It got 146. House Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul announced the results: 146 in favor, 32 absent, 44 abstained. Not a single MP voted against the bill. It failed because the government’s own coalition could not get its members into the chamber.

🔥 Why It Matters

This was supposed to be one of the defining reforms of the Madani government — a structural check on executive power that PH campaigned on for years while in opposition. The bill didn’t fail because of principled opposition. It failed because 76 MPs — nearly a third of the house — were either absent or couldn’t be bothered to take a position. In a government that commands a supermajority, that is not bad luck. That is a failure of whipping, leadership, and political will. It signals that PH’s reform agenda is not just stalled — it is being quietly sabotaged from within.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar Ibrahim has spent three years telling Malaysians that reforms take time, that the coalition is complex, that patience is required. He has a two-thirds majority. He had the bill drafted, tabled, and debated. And when the moment came, his own people didn’t show up. This is the same coalition that can mobilize its full bench for confidence votes and budget approvals but apparently cannot do the same for democratic reform. The message is clear: limiting prime ministerial power is not a priority for the people who benefit from unlimited prime ministerial power.

📜 Previous Cases

PH’s record on structural reform is dismal. The promised repeal of the Sedition Act never happened. The plan to separate the Attorney-General’s prosecutorial power from the PM’s appointment authority has gone nowhere. The proposal to liberalize university admissions by recognizing UEC results has been shelved. The Straits Times reported that DAP — PH’s largest party — has brought forward its national congress to July 2026 specifically to evaluate whether the pace of reforms justifies staying in the coalition. Tony Pua, DAP’s disciplinary chief, warned that “if we don’t withdraw, the party will lose all credibility and can expect a punishing outcome from the elections.”

🎯 PH Callout

You had the numbers. You had the bill. You had three years of promises behind you. And you couldn’t get 148 people into a room. Pakatan Harapan told Malaysians in 2022 that a vote for PH was a vote for reform. The term limit was Exhibit A — the proof that Malaysia Madani meant structural change, not just sloganeering. On March 3, 2026, in a chamber where not a single MP voted no, reformasi died of absenteeism. If you cannot pass your own signature reforms with your own supermajority, what exactly is the point of you?

Sources: The Diplomat | Straits Times | Bernama

item.title: [BM] Anwar Kutuk Serangan AS-Israel Ke Atas Iran Di Parlimen — 'Kita Tak Boleh Lagi Telan' Ceramah Hak Asasi Barat, AP PH Seru Boikot Kedutaan AS
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Syahredzan Johan (PH-Bangi)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 2 Mac 2026, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim membentangkan usul tergempar di Dewan Rakyat mengecam serangan AS dan Israel ke atas Iran, yang bermula pada 28 Februari dan membunuh sekurang-kurangnya 201 orang termasuk Pemimpin Tertinggi Iran Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Anwar memanggil serangan itu “pelanggaran terang-terangan undang-undang antarabangsa” dan menggambarkan ceramah Barat mengenai hak asasi manusia dan demokrasi sebagai kemunafikan:

“Dalam perjumpaan saya dengan kepimpinan Amerika dan Eropah, saya tidak selesa dengan ceramah mereka tentang hak asasi manusia dan demokrasi. Kita tidak boleh lagi menelan hujah sedemikian daripada mereka.”

Usul itu diluluskan sebulat suara dengan sokongan dua pihak. Ketua Pembangkang Hamzah Zainudin memuji Anwar atas “kenyataan tegas dan berani.”

Tetapi detik paling bermakna secara politik datang daripada ahli parlimen belakang PH Syahredzan Johan (Bangi), yang menyeru semua AP untuk memboikot acara anjuran kedutaan AS di Kuala Lumpur:

“Jangan pergi. Walaupun kita dijemput ke sambutan Hari Kemerdekaan mereka, jangan pergi. Kita perlu tunjukkan bahawa kita tidak boleh lagi menerima apa yang mereka lakukan.”

Anwar turut mengisyaratkan penempatan semula diplomatik ke arah Iran, menyeru hubungan pendidikan dan perdagangan lebih erat, mendakwa Malaysia adalah “satu-satunya negara yang menunjukkan pendirian tegas dan berani.”

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING

Kecaman itu sendiri tidak luar biasa — Malaysia secara konsisten menyokong kedaulatan Palestin dan Iran. Yang penting ialah peningkatan retorik dan jurang antara kata dan perbuatan.

Pertama, Anwar memanggil ceramah hak asasi Barat “kemunafikan” agak ironis daripada perdana menteri yang kerajaannya sendiri dikecam kerana:

  • Melindungi ketua SPRM Azam Baki walaupun pelbagai dakwaan rasuah
  • Menggunakan siasatan polis untuk menyasar pengkritik atas nama “komplot Zionis”
  • Memotong separuh hukuman Najib sementara hutang 1MDB masih berbilion
  • Gagal meluluskan reformasi institusi asas termasuk rang undang-undang had penggal PM Kedua, seruan Syahredzan memboikot kedutaan AS adalah populisme berperwatakan dengan akibat diplomatik sebenar. Ekonomi Malaysia bergantung kepada perdagangan dan pelaburan AS. AS adalah rakan dagang ketiga terbesar Malaysia. Menyeru boikot diplomatik dari lantai Parlimen bukan pendirian berprinsip — ia teater politik murah yang orang lain terpaksa selesaikan.

Ketiga, berpaling ke Iran secara diplomatik adalah tindakan melulu. Mazhab Syiah Iran secara rasmi dianggap “sesat” di Malaysia yang majoriti Sunni. Anwar mengisyaratkan solidariti dengan negara teokratik yang identiti agamanya dijadikan jenayah oleh kerajaannya sendiri. Percanggahan ini amat ketara.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT

Kira percanggahan:

Soal kemunafikan hak asasi:

  • Anwar kata beliau “tidak boleh lagi menelan” ceramah Barat tentang hak asasi. Ini orang yang sama yang menghabiskan bertahun-tahun melobi kerajaan Barat, NGO, dan media untuk hak asasi manusianya sendiri — semasa pemenjaraan di bawah Mahathir dan Najib. Keseluruhan kebangkitan politiknya dibina atas simpati antarabangsa dan advokasi asing.

  • Kini setelah berkuasa, advokasi asing adalah “komplot Zionis” dan piawaian hak asasi Barat adalah “kemunafikan.” Satu-satunya yang berubah ialah sebelah meja PM mana beliau duduk. Soal boikot kedutaan AS:

  • Syahredzan Johan adalah ahli parlimen belakang PH yang menyeru boikot diplomatik sementara kerajaannya sendiri mengekalkan hubungan diplomatik, perdagangan, dan ketenteraan penuh dengan AS. Malaysia baru menandatangani Perkongsian Strategik Komprehensif dengan Washington pada Oktober 2025.

  • Jika PH serius menentang tindakan AS, mengapa berhenti di koktel kedutaan? Batalkan perjanjian perdagangan. Panggil balik duta. Oh tunggu — itu akan kos wang. Soal solidariti Iran:

  • Malaysia menjenayahkan amalan Syiah. Jentera hal ehwal agama Anwar sendiri melayan umat Islam Syiah sebagai penyeleweng. Mengisytiharkan solidariti dengan Iran sambil menganiaya penganut agama mereka di dalam negeri bukan berprinsip — ia oportunistik.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

  • 2018-2022: Lobi asing Anwar sendiri. Anwar dan sekutu PKR melantik konsultansi Washington, media antarabangsa, dan kerajaan asing untuk menekan pentadbiran Najib dan Muhyiddin. Beliau menerima sokongan ini dengan penuh rasa syukur. Kini penglibatan asing adalah pengkhianatan.
  • Oktober 2025: Perkongsian Strategik Komprehensif Malaysia-AS. Hanya lima bulan sebelum memanggil ceramah hak asasi AS “kemunafikan,” kerajaan Anwar menandatangani perjanjian dua hala yang dinaik taraf dengan Washington meliputi perdagangan, mineral kritikal, dan akses pasaran.
  • Rekod Malaysia mengenai Iran. Malaysia secara tradisional berhati-hati dengan Iran kerana jurang Sunni-Syiah. Berpaling Anwar adalah penyimpangan daripada beberapa dekad lindung nilai diplomatik yang teliti, didorong politik emosi saat itu dan bukan pengiraan strategik.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH

Anwar, anda beritahu Parlimen: “Kita tidak boleh lagi menelan hujah sedemikian daripada mereka.”

Cukup adil. Tetapi rakyat juga tidak boleh lagi menelan hujah anda.

Anda memberikan ceramah kepada Barat tentang kemunafikan sementara ketua SPRM anda bersandar pada RM800,000 saham tidak didedahkan. Anda memanggil advokasi asing “komplot Zionis” selepas menghabiskan beberapa dekad melakukan perkara yang sama. Anda mengisyaratkan solidariti dengan Iran sementara pihak berkuasa agama anda menjenayahkan amalan Syiah di dalam negeri.

Dan ahli parlimen belakang anda menyeru memboikot pesta kedutaan AS — bentuk bantahan paling rendah kos dan paling rendah risiko yang boleh dibayangkan — sementara kerajaan anda mengekalkan perkongsian strategik penuh dengan Washington.

Ini bukan dasar luar berprinsip. Ia populisme musim Ramadan. Anda mempamerkan kemarahan untuk tatapan dalam negeri sambil tidak melakukan apa-apa yang sebenarnya membebankan anda.

Jika anda mahu diambil serius mengenai hak asasi manusia dan kedaulatan, mulakan di dalam negeri. Betulkan institusi anda sendiri. Reformasi agensi penguatkuasaan anda sendiri. Berhenti menggunakan teori konspirasi untuk membungkam pengkritik. Kemudian — dan hanya kemudian — barulah sesiapa akan mengambil serius ceramah anda kepada Barat.

Sumber: Straits Times | Malay Mail | The Star | FMT | FocusMalaysia

item.title: [BM] Anwar Kutuk Serangan AS-Israel Ke Atas Iran Di Parlimen — 'Kita Tak Boleh Telan Lagi' Ceramah Hak Asasi Barat, AP PH Serukan Boikot Kedutaan AS
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Syahredzan Johan (PH-Bangi)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 2 Mac 2026, Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim membentangkan usul tergempar di Dewan Rakyat mengutuk serangan AS dan Israel ke atas Iran, yang bermula pada 28 Februari dan membunuh sekurang-kurangnya 201 orang termasuk Pemimpin Tertinggi Iran Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Anwar memanggil serangan itu “pelanggaran terang-terangan undang-undang antarabangsa” dan menyifatkan ceramah Barat tentang hak asasi manusia dan demokrasi sebagai kemunafikan:

“Dalam pergaulan saya dengan kepimpinan Amerika dan Eropah, saya tidak selesa dengan ceramah mereka tentang hak asasi manusia dan demokrasi. Kita tidak boleh menelan hujah-hujah sedemikian daripada mereka lagi.”

Usul itu diluluskan sebulat suara dengan sokongan kedua-dua pihak. Tetapi saat paling penting secara politik datang daripada ahli parlimen belakang PH Syahredzan Johan (Bangi), yang menyeru semua AP memboikot acara anjuran kedutaan AS di Kuala Lumpur.

Anwar juga memberi isyarat penempatan semula diplomatik ke arah Iran, menyeru hubungan pendidikan dan perdagangan yang lebih rapat.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING

Kutukan itu sendiri tidak luar biasa — Malaysia secara konsisten menyokong kedaulatan Palestin dan Iran. Apa yang penting adalah peningkatan retorik dan jurang antara kata-kata dan tindakan.

Pertama, Anwar memanggil ceramah hak asasi Barat “kemunafikan” adalah kaya datang daripada perdana menteri yang kerajaannya sendiri di bawah serangan kerana: melindungi ketua SPRM Azam Baki, menggunakan siasatan polis untuk menyasarkan pengkritik, menggugurkan separuh hukuman Najib sementara hutang 1MDB masih berbilion.

Kedua, seruan Syahredzan untuk memboikot kedutaan AS adalah populisme pamer dengan akibat diplomatik sebenar. Ekonomi Malaysia bergantung kepada perdagangan dan pelaburan AS. AS adalah rakan dagang ketiga terbesar Malaysia.

Ketiga, beralih ke arah Iran adalah tidak bertanggungjawab dari segi diplomatik. Mazhab Syiah Iran secara rasmi dianggap “menyimpang” di Malaysia yang majoriti Sunni. Anwar memberi isyarat solidariti dengan negara teokratik yang identiti agamanya dijenayahkan oleh kerajaannya sendiri.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT

Mari kira percanggahan:

Soal kemunafikan hak asasi: Anwar mengatakan dia “tidak boleh menelan lagi” ceramah hak asasi Barat. Ini orang yang sama yang menghabiskan bertahun-tahun melobi kerajaan Barat, NGO dan media untuk hak asasinya sendiri — semasa dipenjarakan di bawah Mahathir dan Najib. Kini penglibatan asing adalah “komplot Zionis” dan piawai hak asasi Barat adalah “kemunafikan.” Satu-satunya yang berubah ialah sebelah mana meja PM dia duduk.

Soal boikot kedutaan AS: Syahredzan Johan menyeru boikot diplomatik sementara kerajaannya sendiri mengekalkan hubungan diplomatik, perdagangan dan ketenteraan penuh dengan AS. Malaysia baru menandatangani Perkongsian Strategik Komprehensif dengan Washington pada Oktober 2025.

Soal solidariti Iran: Malaysia menjenayahkan amalan Syiah. Anwar menyatakan solidariti dengan Iran sambil menganiaya penganut semazhab di tanah air — ini bukan berprinsip, ia oportunistik.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH

Anwar, kamu beritahu Parlimen: “Kita tidak boleh menelan hujah-hujah mereka lagi.”

Adil. Tetapi rakyat juga tidak boleh menelan hujah kamu lagi.

Kamu ceramah Barat tentang kemunafikan sementara ketua SPRM kamu duduk atas RM800,000 saham yang tidak didedahkan. Kamu panggil advokasi asing “komplot Zionis” selepas menghabiskan berdekad melakukan perkara yang sama. Ini bukan dasar luar yang berprinsip. Ini populisme musim Ramadan.

Sumber: Straits Times | Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: [BM] Gabungan Anwar Gagal Luluskan Rang Undang-Undang Reformasi Sendiri — Kalah Undi Had Penggal PM Dengan Dua Undi, Janji 'Cuba Lagi Pada Jun'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Pakatan Harapan Coalition

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 2 Mac, kerajaan perpaduan Anwar Ibrahim gagal meluluskan pindaan perlembagaan yang akan mengehadkan penggal Perdana Menteri kepada 10 tahun — janji reformasi utama manifesto PH pada PRU 2022. Rang undang-undang itu mendapat 146 undi, hanya dua kurang daripada 148 yang diperlukan untuk majoriti dua pertiga. Empat puluh empat AP berkecuali dan 32 tidak hadir. Timbalan Speaker Alice Lau sahkan lapan AP kerajaan dan bebas tidak mengundi. Kerajaan kini berjanji membentangkan semula pada Jun, dengan pemimpin gabungan menjanjikan AP mereka akan “benar-benar hadir kali ini.”

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Ini sepatutnya kemenangan mudah. Had penggal PM popular, sarat simbolik, dan selamat dari segi politik — direka untuk buktikan PH masih mampu laksanakan reformasi. Sebaliknya, gabungan Anwar yang memegang supermajoriti dalam Dewan Rakyat 222 kerusi tidak mampu pastikan ahlinya sendiri hadir mengundi. Kegagalan ini dedahkan kerosakan lebih dalam: tiga tahun di Putrajaya, PH tidak mampu terjemahkan angka parlimen kepada hasil perundangan. Kalau gabungan ini gagal luluskan rang undang-undang yang dipromosikan berminggu-minggu, apa harapan untuk reformasi lebih sukar — penguatkuasaan anti-lompat, IPCMC, ketelusan perolehan, atau kebebasan kehakiman?

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Kerajaan dengan supermajoriti yang kalah undi rang undang-undang reformasi sendiri dengan dua undi bukan nasib malang — ini pengurusan teruk. Sama ada whip parti gagal buat kerja, atau sebahagian gabungan diam-diam sabotaj undian. Seorang AP PH tanpa nama beritahu SCMP: “Rang undang-undang itu sepatutnya mudah lulus. Rasa macam usaha tidak cukup untuk pastikan semua hadir.” Itu bukan penjelasan — itu pengakuan. Dan jawapan kerajaan? “Kami akan cuba lagi pada Jun.” Itu reformasi versi tekan butang snooze. Sementara itu, kamera tangkap Anwar ketawa selepas keputusan undi dibaca, berbual dengan timbalannya seolah-olah rang undang-undang utamanya tidak baru sahaja mati di lantai dewan.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Ini satu corak. PH janji IPCMC — ditangguh. Janji kebebasan maklumat — tiada. Janji penguatkuasaan undang-undang anti-lompat — terpilih sahaja. Janji reformasi pembiayaan politik — terperangkap dalam jawatankuasa. Manifesto 2022 setebal 60 muka surat; tiga tahun kemudian, perkara paling sukar masih tidak disentuh dan yang mudah pun tergagap. Had penggal PM secara literal buah paling rendah — popular merentas parti, disokong sendiri oleh Anwar, dan hanya perlu supermajoriti yang PH sudah ada di atas kertas.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Kamu kata kamu berbeza. Kamu lari atas platform “Reformasi.” Kamu ada angka, mandat, dan sokongan rakyat. Dan kamu tak mampu pastikan AP sendiri masuk dewan untuk undi rang undang-undang yang kamu jual kepada negara berminggu-minggu. Kalau kamu tak boleh luluskan reformasi popular dan simbolik dengan supermajoriti, untuk apa sebenarnya majoriti itu? “Kami akan cuba lagi pada Jun” bukan agenda reformasi — itu pengakuan kamu tak ada kawalan ke atas gabungan sendiri.

Sumber: Reuters | Bloomberg | SCMP | Bernama | The Diplomat | CNA

item.title: [BM] Koalisi Anwar Gagal Luluskan Had Penggal PM — Dua Undi Pendek, 32 Ahli Parlimen Tak Hadir, Sifar Akauntabiliti
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Unity Government Leadership

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 2 Mac, kerajaan perpaduan Anwar Ibrahim gagal meluluskan Rang Undang-Undang Pindaan Perlembagaan 2026 yang bertujuan mengehadkan tempoh Perdana Menteri kepada 10 tahun atau dua penggal. Rang undang-undang ini memerlukan majoriti dua pertiga — 148 undi dalam Dewan Rakyat 222 kerusi — tetapi hanya mendapat 146. Tiga puluh dua Ahli Parlimen tidak hadir, 44 berkecuali, dan tiada seorang pun mengundi menentang. Antara yang tidak hadir ialah beberapa wakil daripada rakan gabungan Barisan Nasional, termasuk Ahli Parlimen Sembrong Hishammuddin Hussein dan Ahli Parlimen Tapah Saravanan Murugan. Kerajaan berjanji akan membentangkan semula rang undang-undang ini pada Jun.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Ini sepatutnya kemenangan reformasi paling mudah dalam buku permainan Anwar — cadangan popular, sarat simbolisme, yang PH janji sejak 2018. Anwar memegang majoriti besar dalam Parlimen. Beliau gagal meluluskan rang undang-undang yang tiada siapa pun mengundi menentangnya. Kegagalan ini mendedahkan gabungan yang tidak mampu mendisiplinkan ahlinya sendiri, apatah lagi melaksanakan reformasi institusi yang dijanjikan kepada pengundi Malaysia. Dengan pilihan raya umum dijangka menjelang awal 2028, masa semakin suntuk dan kad laporan reformasi semakin merah.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Kerajaan yang pegang majoriti besar tetapi tidak mampu pastikan ahli parlimennya sendiri hadir untuk undi yang dipromosikan berminggu-minggu bukanlah kerajaan reformasi — ia adalah persembahan. Tiga puluh dua Ahli Parlimen tidak ambil kisah untuk hadir. Empat puluh empat berkecuali. Pembangkang tidak bunuh rang undang-undang ini; kelembapan gabungan sendiri yang buat. Dan jawapannya? “Kami akan cuba lagi pada Jun.” Itu bukan akauntabiliti. Itu kerajaan yang mengaku gagal dalam fungsi paling asas pengurusan gabungan dan minta peluang kedua macam pelajar yang lupa belajar untuk peperiksaan.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Ini bukan kali pertama gabungan Anwar tersadung atas janji reformasi sendiri. Undang-Undang Anti-Lompat Parti, diluluskan pada 2022, dipuji sebagai mercu tanda — sehingga skopnya dikecilkan dan tidak pernah diuji secara serius. Pemisahan peranan Peguam Negara dan Pendakwa Raya yang dijanjikan berulang kali ditangguh. IPCMC (Suruhanjaya Bebas Aduan Polis) dicairkan menjadi IPCC yang tidak bertaring. Setiap kali PH janji reformasi, polanya sama: pengumuman besar, undur senyap, dan janji untuk kaji semula kemudian.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Kamu bertanding atas platform reformasi. Kamu kata 22 tahun Mahathir “rosakkan negara.” Kamu janji had penggal pada 2018. Kamu janji lagi pada 2022. Kamu ada majoriti besar. Kamu ada rang undang-undang. Kamu ada berminggu-minggu untuk bersedia. Dan kamu tak mampu dapat 148 orang untuk hadir. Kalau kamu tak boleh luluskan reformasi paling senang dalam agenda sendiri, untuk apa sebenarnya kerajaan perpaduan ini? Jun bukan peluang kedua — ia adalah ujian sama ada gabungan ini masih ada disiplin. Rakyat sedang memerhatikan, dan jam tidak dimundurkan.

Sumber: Reuters | SCMP | Bloomberg | The Edge Malaysia | CNA | The Diplomat

item.title: [BM] PKR Upah Perunding AS Untuk Lobi Washington Terhadap Najib — Kini Menjerit Salah Bila Orang Lain Buat Sama
item.description:
Said by: Sivarasa Rasiah, Former PKR Vice-President (confirmed); PKR leadership

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada Mac 2026, MalaysiaNow menerbitkan emel bocor yang mendedahkan PKR telah mengupah sebuah firma perundingan politik berpusat di Washington pada 2016 untuk melobi Amerika Syarikat menekan Perdana Menteri ketika itu Najib Razak. Emel tersebut termasuk dokumen 84 muka surat yang membuat tuduhan serius terhadap Najib, dengan matlamat mendapatkan Washington untuk secara terbuka mengecam kerajaan Malaysia.

Bekas naib presiden PKR Sivarasa Rasiah mengesahkan komunikasi tersebut, mengatakan pemimpin parti termasuk beliau sendiri terlibat. Beliau merangkanya sebagai advokasi yang sah untuk membebaskan Anwar Ibrahim, yang ketika itu seorang tahanan politik.

Pendedahan ini datang ketika kerajaan Anwar secara serentak menuduh keluarga bekas menteri kewangan Daim Zainuddin menggunakan ejen asing untuk menggugat kestabilan pentadbiran semasa — memanggilnya ancaman keselamatan negara.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING

Ini mendedahkan piawai berganda yang besar di teras strategi politik PH semasa. Anwar merangka dakwaan lobi asing keluarga Daim sebagai pengkhianatan — komplot untuk menjatuhkan kerajaan menggunakan pengaruh asing. Tetapi partinya sendiri melakukan perkara yang sama apabila mereka dalam pembangkang.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT

Pembelaan Sivarasa — “kami cuba membebaskan tahanan politik” — mudah tetapi tidak tahan penelitian. Dokumen 84 muka surat itu bukan sahaja meminta pembebasan Anwar. Ia membuat tuduhan menyeluruh terhadap Najib dan secara jelas meminta campur tangan AS dalam politik domestik Malaysia. Itu lobi asing. Noktah.

Apabila PKR melakukannya, ia “advokasi.” Apabila keluarga Daim didakwa melakukannya, ia “konspirasi Zionis” dan ancaman keselamatan negara. Piawai berganda begitu terang-terangan sehingga pemerhati biasa pun boleh nampak. Anwar pada dasarnya mengatakan: lobi asing adalah patriotik apabila kami melakukannya, dan pengkhianatan apabila lawan kami melakukannya. Itu bukan prinsip — ia kemudahan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Parti politik Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang lobi antarabangsa. UMNO mengekalkan pelobi Washington selama berdekad. Anwar sendiri memupuk hubungan mendalam dengan pembuat dasar AS, badan pemikir dan media semasa tahun-tahun pembangkangan. Kemarahan terpilih mengikuti corak yang sama seperti layanan PH terhadap kebebasan akhbar (Bloomberg bagus apabila mendedahkan Najib, Bloomberg buruk apabila mendedahkan Azam Baki).

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH

Kamu mengupah firma AS untuk melobi Washington terhadap PM Malaysia yang memerintah, menghasilkan dokumen 84 muka surat tuduhan, dan meminta campur tangan Amerika dalam politik Malaysia. Bekas naib presiden kamu sendiri mengesahkannya. Kini kamu beritahu rakyat Malaysia apabila orang lain melakukan perkara yang sama, ia konspirasi Zionis untuk menjatuhkan kerajaan? Pilih satu laluan. Sama ada lobi asing adalah bahagian sah advokasi politik — maka berhenti menjenayahkan lawan kamu — atau ia ancaman keselamatan negara, maka pemimpin PKR sendiri perlu disiasat juga. Kamu tak boleh ambil kedua-duanya.

Sumber: MalaysiaNow | MalaysiaNow - Dokumen | Malaysiakini

item.title: [BM] PKR Upah Perunding AS Untuk Melobi Washington Menentang Najib — Kini Menangis Bila Orang Lain Buat Sama
item.description:
Said by: Sivarasa Rasiah, Former PKR Vice-President (confirmed); PKR leadership

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada Mac 2026, MalaysiaNow menerbitkan emel bocor yang mendedahkan PKR telah melantik firma perunding politik berpangkalan di Washington pada 2016 untuk melobi Amerika Syarikat agar menekan Perdana Menteri ketika itu Najib Razak. Emel itu termasuk dosier 84 muka surat yang membuat dakwaan serius terhadap Najib, dengan matlamat mendapatkan Washington mengecam kerajaan Malaysia secara terbuka.

Bekas naib presiden PKR Sivarasa Rasiah mengesahkan komunikasi itu, mengatakan pemimpin parti termasuk dirinya terlibat. Beliau membingkainya sebagai advokasi sah untuk membebaskan Anwar Ibrahim, yang ketika itu seorang tahanan politik.

Pendedahan ini berlaku ketika kerajaan Anwar sedang menuduh keluarga bekas menteri kewangan Daim Zainuddin menggunakan ejen asing untuk menggugat kestabilan pentadbiran semasa — memanggilnya ancaman keselamatan negara.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING

Ini mendedahkan standard berganda besar di teras strategi politik PH semasa. Anwar membingkai dakwaan lobi asing keluarga Daim sebagai pengkhianatan — komplot untuk menggulingkan kerajaan menggunakan pengaruh asing. Tetapi partinya sendiri melakukan perkara yang sama ketika dalam pembangkang.

AP pembangkang Shahidan Kassim mendesak Anwar di Parlimen untuk menjelaskan bezanya. Jawapan itu penting kerana kerajaan menggunakan kes Daim untuk membenarkan retorik keselamatan negara yang diperluaskan, mengalih perhatian daripada skandal Azam Baki, dan melukis pengkritik sebagai ejen asing. Jika lobi PKR sendiri adalah sah, maka standard yang sama mesti digunakan untuk orang lain.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT

Pembelaan Sivarasa — “kami hanya cuba membebaskan tahanan politik” — mudah tetapi tidak tahan diteliti. Dosier 84 muka surat itu bukan sekadar meminta pembebasan Anwar. Ia membuat dakwaan menyeluruh terhadap Najib dan secara jelas meminta campur tangan AS dalam politik dalam negeri Malaysia. Itu lobi asing. Titik.

Apabila PKR melakukannya, ia “advokasi.” Apabila keluarga Daim didakwa melakukannya, ia “konspirasi Zionis” dan ancaman keselamatan negara. Standard berganda ini begitu terang-terangan sehingga pemerhati biasa pun boleh nampak.

Kerajaan Anwar pada dasarnya mengatakan: lobi asing adalah patriotik apabila kami melakukannya, dan pengkhianatan apabila lawan kami melakukannya. Itu bukan prinsip — itu kemudahan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Parti politik Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang lobi antarabangsa. UMNO mengekalkan pelobi Washington selama berdekad. Anwar sendiri memupuk hubungan mendalam dengan pembuat dasar AS, pusat pemikir, dan media semasa bertahun-tahun dalam pembangkang. Tiada satu pun dianggap bermasalah apabila PH adalah penerima manfaat.

Kemarahan terpilih ini mengikuti pola sama seperti layanan PH terhadap kebebasan akhbar (Bloomberg baik apabila mendedahkan Najib, Bloomberg buruk apabila mendedahkan Azam Baki) dan akauntabiliti institusi (SPRM dipersenjatakan di bawah BN, SPRM bebas di bawah PH).

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH

Anda mengupah firma AS untuk melobi Washington menentang PM Malaysia yang sedang memerintah, menghasilkan dosier dakwaan 84 muka surat, dan meminta campur tangan Amerika dalam politik Malaysia. Bekas naib presiden anda sendiri mengesahkannya. Kini anda memberitahu rakyat Malaysia bahawa apabila orang lain melakukan perkara sama, ia konspirasi bersandar Zionis untuk menggulingkan kerajaan? Pilih satu laluan. Sama ada lobi asing adalah bahagian sah advokasi politik — dalam hal itu berhenti menjenayahkan lawan anda kerananya — atau ia ancaman keselamatan negara, dalam hal itu pemimpin PKR sendiri patut disiasat juga. Anda tidak boleh ada kedua-duanya.

Sumber: MalaysiaNow | MalaysiaNow - Dosier | The Star | Malaysiakini | Malaysiakini - Sivarasa

item.title: [BM] Rang Undang-Undang Had Penggal PM PH Sendiri Gagal Dua Undi — 8 AP Kerajaan Sendiri Tidak Hadir
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister / Anthony Loke, DAP Secretary-General

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 2 Mac 2026, Dewan Rakyat Malaysia mengundi Rang Undang-Undang Perlembagaan (Pindaan) 2026 — reformasi lama dijanjikan PH untuk mengehadkan penggal perdana menteri kepada 10 tahun (dua penggal). Rang undang-undang memerlukan 148 undi — majoriti besar dua pertiga — untuk diluluskan. Ia mendapat 146. Dua undi kurang. Yang Dipertua Dewan Tan Sri Johari Abdul mengesahkan 44 AP berkecuali (kebanyakan pembangkang), 32 tidak hadir, dan — dalam butiran yang sangat bermakna — tiada seorang AP pun mengundi menentang. Di antara mereka yang tidak hadir ialah 8 AP penyokong kerajaan. Menteri Komunikasi Fahmi Fadzil kemudian mengesahkan hanya 2 daripada 8 yang mengemukakan penjelasan rasmi; 6 tidak ambil kisah pun. Setiausaha Agung DAP Anthony Loke mengeluarkan kenyataan tegas menekankan kesemua 40 AP DAP hadir, dan secara terang-terangan menegur kerusi kosong.

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Ini sepatutnya menjadi reformasi institusi tandatangan PH — tunjang janji manifesto sejak pilihan raya umum 2022, lahir terus daripada trauma pemerintahan 22 tahun Mahathir. Had penggal PM adalah salah satu perlindungan demokrasi paling penting yang boleh digubal oleh mana-mana kerajaan. Dan PH tidak mampu pastikan gabungannya sendiri hadir. Rang undang-undang tidak ditewaskan oleh pembangkang — ia dibunuh oleh sikap tidak peduli, ketidakcekapan, dan ketidakhadiran dari dalam barisan kerajaan sendiri. AP PKR Wong Chen kemudian dedahkan sekurang-kurangnya seorang AP tidak hadir berkata beliau “langsung tidak diberitahu dengan betul untuk hadir” dan tidak menerima panggilan susulan. Ini bukan sekadar kegagalan perundangan — ia kegagalan tadbir urus yang mendedahkan kekosongan jenama reformasi PH.

💩 Mengapa Ia Karut

PH mempunyai tiga tahun untuk bersiap sedia bagi pengundian ini. Tiga tahun untuk mengawal whip AP sendiri, mengira bilangan, mengesahkan kehadiran, dan menyampaikan keperluan paling asas demokrasi berparlimen — hadir. Timbalan PM Fadillah Yusof adalah ketua whip kerajaan. Tugasnya satu-satunya dalam konteks ini adalah memastikan setiap AP kerajaan hadir. Beliau gagal. Dan alasan AP yang tidak hadir — “saya tidak diberitahu dengan betul” — sangat mengejutkan dengan sikap sambil lewanya. Ini pindaan perlembagaan, bukan taklimat bangku belakang tentang dasar saliran. Jika PH tidak mampu menggerakkan APnya sendiri untuk rang undang-undang reformasi utamanya sendiri, maka agenda reformasi bukan keutamaan — ia slogan. Pembangkang berkecuali, seperti dijangka. Tetapi AP kerajaan yang tidak hadir yang membunuh rang undang-undang ini.

📜 Kes-kes Terdahulu

Malaysia tidak pernah mempunyai had penggal berkanun untuk perdana menteri. Mahathir berkhidmat 22 tahun (1981–2003), kemudian kembali untuk 22 bulan lagi (2018–2020). Kerosakan penggal eksekutif tanpa semakan didokumentasikan dengan baik dalam tadbir urus Malaysia — dari pengukuhan rangkaian naungan hingga penghakisan kebebasan institusi. Manifesto PH 2022 secara nyata menjanjikan reformasi ini. Dalam ucapan Belanjawan 2024, Anwar menegaskan semula komitmen kerajaan terhadap reformasi institusi. Namun apabila tiba masanya menyampaikan, disiplin gabungan PH — kelemahan berterusan sejak Langkah Sheraton — sekali lagi terbukti tidak mencukupi. Ini gabungan yang sama yang kehilangan kerajaannya sendiri pada 2020 kerana tidak mampu mengekalkan barisannya.

🎯 Teguran Kepada PH

Anda membuat janji ini. Anda masukkannya dalam manifesto. Anda beritahu pengundi Malaysia bahawa mengehadkan penggal PM penting untuk mencegah pengukuhan kuasa gaya Mahathir. Dan apabila saat tiba — apabila 148 undi sahaja yang anda perlukan dari dewan 222 kerusi di mana anda menguasai majoriti yang selesa — anda tidak mampu membawa orang anda sendiri masuk ke dewan. Enam daripada lapan AP anda yang tidak hadir tidak menjelaskan mengapa mereka tiada. Fahmi kata “tiada tindakan diambil.” Sudah tentu tidak. Kerana dalam PH, janji reformasi adalah untuk kempen, bukan untuk parlimen. Anda ada satu tugas. Anda gagal. Dan setiap rakyat Malaysia yang percaya retorik reformasi anda kini membayar harga untuk ketidakcekapan anda.

Sumber: Reuters | Bloomberg | CNA | CNA Ulasan (13 Mac) | The Diplomat | Malay Mail | Fahmi soal AP tidak hadir (Malay Mail menerusi Yahoo) | The Edge

item.title: [BM] Rang Undang-Undang Had Penggal PM PH Sendiri Gagal Dua Undi — 8 MP Kerajaan Langsung Tak Hadir
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister / Anthony Loke, DAP Secretary-General

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 2 Mac 2026, Dewan Rakyat Malaysia mengundi Rang Undang-Undang Pindaan Perlembagaan 2026 — janji reformasi lama PH untuk menghadkan tempoh perdana menteri kepada 10 tahun (dua penggal). Rang undang-undang ini memerlukan 148 undi — majoriti dua pertiga — untuk diluluskan. Ia dapat 146. Kekurangan dua undi. Yang Dipertua Dewan Rakyat Tan Sri Johari Abdul mengesahkan 44 MP berkecuali (kebanyakan pembangkang), 32 tidak hadir, dan — secara ketara — tiada seorang MP pun mengundi menentang. Antara yang tidak hadir adalah 8 MP penyokong kerajaan. Menteri Komunikasi Fahmi Fadzil kemudian mengesahkan hanya 2 daripada 8 mengemukakan penjelasan rasmi; 6 lagi tidak ambil peduli. Setiausaha Agung DAP Anthony Loke mengeluarkan kenyataan keras menekankan kesemua 40 MP DAP hadir, dan secara tajam menyindir kerusi-kerusi kosong.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Ini sepatutnya menjadi reformasi institusi utama PH — nadi janji manifesto sejak pilihan raya umum 2022, lahir terus daripada trauma pemerintahan 22 tahun Mahathir. Had penggal PM adalah salah satu langkah perlindungan demokrasi paling penting yang boleh digubal mana-mana kerajaan. Dan PH tidak mampu memastikan koalisi sendiri hadir. Rang undang-undang ini tidak dikalahkan oleh pembangkang — ia dibunuh oleh sikap sambil lewa, ketidakcekapan, dan ketidakhadiran dari dalam barisan kerajaan sendiri. MP PKR Wong Chen kemudian mendedahkan sekurang-kurangnya seorang MP yang tidak hadir berkata beliau “langsung tidak dimaklumkan dengan betul untuk datang” dan tidak menerima sebarang panggilan susulan. Ini bukan sekadar kegagalan perundangan — ini kegagalan tadbir urus yang mendedahkan kehampaan jenama reformasi PH.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

PH ada tiga tahun untuk menyediakan undian ini. Tiga tahun untuk mengawal MP sendiri, mengira kepala, mengesahkan kehadiran, dan menyampaikan keperluan paling asas demokrasi berparlimen — hadir. Timbalan PM Fadillah Yusof adalah whip utama kerajaan. Tugasnya dalam konteks ini hanya satu: pastikan setiap MP kerajaan hadir. Dia gagal. Dan alasan MP yang tidak hadir — “saya tidak diberitahu dengan betul” — sungguh mengejutkan dalam kasualnya. Ini pindaan perlembagaan, bukan taklimat bangku belakang mengenai polisi saliran. Jika PH tidak mampu menggerakkan MP sendiri untuk rang undang-undang reformasi utama sendiri, maka agenda reformasi itu bukan keutamaan — ia adalah slogan. Pembangkang berkecuali, seperti dijangka. Tapi MP kerajaan yang tidak hadir itulah yang membunuh rang undang-undang ini.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Malaysia tidak pernah mempunyai had penggal berkanun untuk perdana menteri. Mahathir berkhidmat 22 tahun (1981–2003), kemudian kembali untuk 22 bulan lagi (2018–2020). Kerosakan tempoh eksekutif tanpa kawalan didokumentasikan dengan baik dalam tadbir urus Malaysia — daripada pengukuhan rangkaian naungan hingga hakisan kemerdekaan institusi. Manifesto PH 2022 secara eksplisit menjanjikan reformasi ini. Dalam ucapan Bajet 2024, Anwar menegaskan semula komitmen kerajaan terhadap reformasi institusi. Namun bila tiba masanya untuk menyampaikan, disiplin koalisi PH — kelemahan kekal sejak Langkah Sheraton — sekali lagi terbukti tidak memadai. Ini koalisi sama yang kehilangan kerajaan sendiri pada 2020 kerana gagal mengekalkan barisan.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Kamu buat janji ini. Kamu letak dalam manifesto. Kamu beritahu pengundi Malaysia bahawa menghadkan tempoh PM adalah penting untuk mencegah pengukuhan kuasa ala Mahathir. Dan bila tiba saat itu — bila 148 undi sahaja diperlukan daripada dewan 222 kerusi di mana kamu menguasai majoriti selesa — kamu tak mampu suruh orang kamu sendiri masuk ke dewan. Enam daripada lapan MP yang tidak hadir belum pun jelaskan kenapa mereka tiada. Fahmi kata “tiada tindakan diambil.” Sudah tentu tiada. Kerana dalam PH, janji reformasi untuk kempen, bukan untuk parlimen. Kamu ada satu tugas. Kamu gagal. Dan setiap rakyat Malaysia yang percaya retorik reformasi kamu kini membayar harga untuk ketidakcekapan kamu.

Sumber: Reuters | Bloomberg | CNA | CNA Ulasan (13 Mac) | The Diplomat | Malay Mail | Fahmi mengenai MP tidak hadir (Malay Mail via Yahoo) | The Edge

item.title: Anwar Condemns US-Israel Strikes on Iran in Parliament — 'We Can No Longer Swallow' Western Human Rights Lectures, PH MP Calls for US Embassy Boycott
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Syahredzan Johan (PH-Bangi)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 2 March 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim tabled an emergency motion in the Dewan Rakyat condemning the US and Israel’s strikes on Iran, which began on 28 February and killed at least 201 people including Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Anwar called the strikes a “flagrant violation of international law” and described Western lectures on human rights and democracy as hypocrisy:

“In my engagements with the American and European leadership, I am uneasy about their lectures on human rights and democracy. We can no longer swallow such arguments from them.”

The motion passed unanimously with bipartisan support. Opposition Leader Hamzah Zainudin praised Anwar for a “stern and firm statement.”

But the most politically significant moment came from PH backbencher Syahredzan Johan (Bangi), who called on all MPs to boycott events hosted by the US embassy in Kuala Lumpur:

“Let’s not go. Even though we get invited to their Independence Day celebrations, let’s not go. We need to show that we can no longer accept what they do.”

Anwar also signaled a diplomatic repositioning toward Iran, calling for closer education and trade ties, claiming Malaysia was “the only country to show a firm, brave stance.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

The condemnation itself is unremarkable — Malaysia has consistently supported Palestinian and Iranian sovereignty. What matters is the escalation in rhetoric and the gap between words and actions.

First, Anwar calling Western human rights lectures “hypocrisy” is rich coming from a prime minister whose own government is under fire for:

  • Protecting MACC chief Azam Baki despite multiple corruption allegations
  • Using police investigations to target critics under the guise of a “Zionist plot”
  • Halving Najib’s sentence while the 1MDB debt still runs into billions
  • Failing to pass even basic institutional reforms like the PM tenure bill Anwar is lecturing the West about hypocrisy while running a government that has abandoned virtually every reform promise it made.

Second, Syahredzan’s call to boycott the US embassy is performative populism with real diplomatic consequences. Malaysia’s economy depends on US trade and investment. The US is Malaysia’s third-largest trading partner. Calling for a diplomatic boycott from the floor of Parliament is not a principled stand — it’s cheap political theatre that someone else will have to clean up.

Third, the pivot toward Iran is diplomatically reckless. Iran’s Shia denomination is officially considered “deviant” in Sunni-majority Malaysia. Anwar is signaling solidarity with a theocratic state whose religious identity his own government criminalises. The contradiction is absurd.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Let’s count the contradictions:

On human rights hypocrisy:

  • Anwar says he can “no longer swallow” Western lectures on human rights. This is the same man who spent years lobbying Western governments, NGOs, and media outlets for his own human rights — during his imprisonment under Mahathir and Najib. His entire political comeback was built on international sympathy and foreign advocacy.

  • Now that he’s in power, foreign advocacy is a “Zionist plot” and Western human rights standards are “hypocrisy.” The only thing that changed is which side of the PM’s desk he sits on. On the US embassy boycott:

  • Syahredzan Johan is a PH backbencher calling for a diplomatic boycott while his own government maintains full diplomatic, trade, and military relations with the US. Malaysia just signed a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership with Washington in October 2025.

  • If PH is serious about opposing US actions, why stop at embassy cocktail parties? Cancel the trade deal. Recall the ambassador. Oh wait — that would cost money. On Iran solidarity:

  • Malaysia criminalises Shia practice. Anwar’s own religious affairs apparatus treats Shia Muslims as deviants. Declaring solidarity with Iran while persecuting its co-religionists at home is not principled — it’s opportunistic.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

  • 2018-2022: Anwar’s own foreign lobbying. Anwar and PKR allies engaged Washington consultancies, international media, and foreign governments to pressure the Najib and Muhyiddin administrations. He accepted this support gratefully. Now foreign engagement is subversion.
  • October 2025: Malaysia-US Comprehensive Strategic Partnership. Just five months before calling US human rights lectures “hypocrisy,” Anwar’s government signed an upgraded bilateral deal with Washington covering trade, critical minerals, and market access.
  • Malaysia’s track record on Iran. Malaysia has historically been cautious with Iran due to the Sunni-Shia divide. Anwar’s pivot is a departure from decades of careful diplomatic hedging, driven by the emotional politics of the moment rather than strategic calculation.
  • 2024-2025: Gaza solidarity without substance. Malaysia has been vocal on Palestine but has taken no meaningful economic or diplomatic action against Israel beyond rhetoric. The pattern is clear: loud words, zero follow-through.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

Anwar, you told Parliament: “We can no longer swallow such arguments from them.”

Fair enough. But the rakyat can no longer swallow yours either.

You lecture the West on hypocrisy while your MACC chief sits on RM800,000 in undisclosed shares. You call foreign advocacy a “Zionist plot” after spending decades doing exactly the same thing. You signal solidarity with Iran while your religious authorities criminalise Shia practice at home.

And your backbencher calls for boycotting US embassy parties — the absolute lowest-cost, lowest-risk form of protest imaginable — while your government maintains a full strategic partnership with Washington.

This isn’t principled foreign policy. It’s Ramadan-season populism. You’re performing outrage for domestic consumption while doing nothing that would actually cost you anything.

If you want to be taken seriously on human rights and sovereignty, start at home. Fix your own institutions. Reform your own enforcement agencies. Stop using conspiracy theories to silence critics. Then — and only then — will anyone take your lectures to the West seriously.

Sources: Straits Times | Malay Mail | The Star | FMT | FocusMalaysia

item.title: Anwar's Coalition Can't Pass Its Own Reform Bill — Loses PM Term Limit Vote by Two, Promises to 'Try Again in June'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Pakatan Harapan Coalition

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 What Happened

On March 2, Anwar Ibrahim’s unity government failed to pass a constitutional amendment that would have limited the prime minister’s tenure to 10 years — a flagship reform promise from PH’s 2022 election manifesto. The bill received 146 votes, just two short of the 148 needed for a two-thirds majority. Forty-four MPs abstained and 32 were absent. Deputy Speaker Alice Lau confirmed eight government and independent MPs did not participate. The government has since pledged to reintroduce the bill in June, with coalition leaders promising their MPs will “actually attend this time.”

🔥 Why It Matters

This was supposed to be the easy win. The PM term limit was popular, symbolically loaded, and politically safe — designed to show that PH can still deliver on reform. Instead, Anwar’s coalition, which holds a supermajority in the 222-seat Dewan Rakyat, couldn’t even whip its own members into the chamber. The failure exposes a deeper rot: three years into government, PH cannot translate its parliamentary numbers into legislative outcomes. If the coalition can’t pass a bill it spent weeks championing, what hope is there for harder reforms — anti-hopping enforcement, IPCMC, procurement transparency, or judicial independence?

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

A government with a supermajority losing its own reform bill by two votes is not bad luck — it’s bad management. Either the whips failed to do their jobs, or parts of the coalition quietly sabotaged the vote. An unnamed PH lawmaker told the SCMP: “That bill should have easily passed. It feels like there was not enough effort to make sure everyone turned up.” That’s not an explanation — it’s a confession. And the government’s response? “We’ll try again in June.” That’s the reform equivalent of hitting snooze. Meanwhile, cameras caught Anwar mid-laugh after the vote was read out, chatting with his deputy as if his flagship bill hadn’t just died on the floor.

📜 Previous Cases

This is a pattern. PH promised IPCMC — shelved. Promised freedom of information — nothing. Promised anti-hopping law enforcement — selective at best. Promised political funding reform — in committee limbo. The 2022 manifesto was a 60-page wishlist; three years on, the hardest items remain untouched and even the easy ones are fumbled. The PM term limit was literally the lowest-hanging fruit — popular across party lines, personally backed by Anwar, and requiring only a supermajority PH already had on paper.

🎯 PH Callout

You told Malaysia you were different. You ran on “Reformasi.” You had the numbers, the mandate, and the public goodwill. And you couldn’t get your own MPs into the room to vote for a bill you spent weeks selling to the country. If you can’t pass popular, symbolic reforms with a supermajority, what exactly are you using that majority for? “We’ll try again in June” is not a reform agenda — it’s an admission that you don’t have control of your own coalition.

Sources: Reuters | Bloomberg | SCMP | Bernama | The Diplomat | CNA

item.title: Anwar's Coalition Fails to Pass PM Term-Limit Bill — Two Votes Short, 32 MPs Missing, Zero Accountability
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Unity Government Leadership

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 What Happened

On March 2, Anwar Ibrahim’s unity government failed to pass the Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2026, which would have capped the prime minister’s tenure at 10 years or two terms. The bill needed a two-thirds supermajority — 148 votes in the 222-seat Dewan Rakyat — and got 146. Thirty-two MPs were absent, 44 abstained, and none voted against. Among the absentees were several from coalition partner Barisan Nasional, including Sembrong MP Hishammuddin Hussein and Tapah MP Saravanan Murugan. The government has pledged to reintroduce the bill in June.

🔥 Why It Matters

This was supposed to be the easiest reform win in Anwar’s playbook — a popular, symbolically loaded proposal that PH had promised since 2018. Anwar commands a supermajority in Parliament. He failed to pass a bill that no one even voted against. The failure exposes a coalition that cannot enforce discipline on its own members, let alone deliver the institutional reforms it promised Malaysian voters. With a general election due by early 2028, the clock is ticking and the reform scorecard is bleeding red.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

A government with a supermajority that cannot whip its own MPs to show up for a vote it spent weeks championing is not a reform government — it is a performance. Thirty-two MPs did not bother to attend. Forty-four abstained. The opposition did not kill this bill; the coalition’s own inertia did. And the response? “We’ll try again in June.” That is not accountability. That is a government admitting it failed at the most basic function of coalition management and asking for a do-over like a student who forgot to study for the exam.

📜 Previous Cases

This is not the first time Anwar’s coalition has stumbled on its own reform promises. The Anti-Party Hopping Law, passed in 2022, was hailed as a landmark — until it was narrowed in scope and never seriously tested. The promised separation of the Attorney General and Public Prosecutor roles has been delayed repeatedly. The IPCMC (Independent Police Complaints Commission) was watered down into the toothless IPCC. Every time PH promises reform, the pattern is the same: grand announcement, quiet retreat, and a promise to revisit later.

🎯 PH Callout

You ran on reform. You said Mahathir’s 22 years “damaged the country.” You promised term limits in 2018. You promised them again in 2022. You had a supermajority. You had a bill. You had weeks to prepare. And you could not get 148 people to show up. If you cannot pass the easiest reform on your own agenda, what exactly is the unity government for? June is not a second chance — it is a test of whether this coalition has any discipline left at all. The rakyat is watching, and the clock does not reset.

Sources: Reuters | SCMP | Bloomberg | The Edge Malaysia | CNA | The Diplomat

item.title: PH's Own PM Term Limit Bill Fails by Two Votes — 8 Government MPs Didn't Even Show Up
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister / Anthony Loke, DAP Secretary-General

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 What Happened

On March 2, 2026, Malaysia’s Dewan Rakyat voted on the Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2026 — PH’s long-promised reform to limit the prime minister’s tenure to 10 years (two terms). The bill needed 148 votes — a two-thirds supermajority — to pass. It got 146. Two votes short. Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul confirmed that 44 MPs abstained (mostly opposition), 32 were absent, and — in a telling detail — not a single MP voted against it. Among the absentees were 8 government-supporting MPs. Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil later confirmed that only 2 of the 8 had submitted formal explanations; 6 had not even bothered. DAP Secretary-General Anthony Loke issued a strongly worded statement emphasising that all 40 DAP MPs were present, and pointedly called out the empty seats.

🔥 Why It Matters

This was supposed to be PH’s signature institutional reform — the centrepiece of a manifesto promise dating back to the 2022 general election, born directly from the trauma of Mahathir’s 22-year reign. A PM term limit is one of the most consequential democratic safeguards any government can enact. And PH couldn’t even get its own coalition to show up. The bill wasn’t defeated by opposition — it was killed by indifference, incompetence, and absenteeism from within the government’s own ranks. PKR MP Wong Chen later revealed that at least one absent MP said he “was simply not properly informed to turn up” and received no follow-up calls. This is not just a legislative failure — it is a governance failure that exposes the hollowness of PH’s reform brand.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH had three years to prepare for this vote. Three years to whip their own MPs, count heads, confirm attendance, and deliver the most basic requirement of parliamentary democracy — showing up. Deputy PM Fadillah Yusof is the government chief whip. His one job in this context was to ensure every government MP was present. He failed. And the excuse from absent MPs — “I wasn’t properly informed” — is breathtaking in its casualness. This was a constitutional amendment, not a backbench briefing on drainage policy. If PH cannot mobilise its own MPs for its own flagship reform bill, then the reform agenda is not a priority — it is a slogan. The opposition abstained, as expected. But the government’s absentees are the ones who killed this bill.

📜 Previous Cases

Malaysia has never had a statutory term limit for the prime minister. Mahathir served 22 years (1981–2003), then returned for another 22 months (2018–2020). The damage of unchecked executive tenure is well-documented in Malaysian governance — from the entrenchment of patronage networks to the erosion of institutional independence. PH’s 2022 manifesto explicitly promised this reform. In the 2024 Budget speech, Anwar reaffirmed the government’s commitment to institutional reform. Yet when it came time to deliver, PH’s coalition discipline — a perennial weakness since the Sheraton Move — once again proved inadequate. This is the same coalition that lost its own government in 2020 because it couldn’t hold its ranks together.

🎯 PH Callout

You made this promise. You put it in your manifesto. You told Malaysian voters that limiting PM tenure was essential to prevent another Mahathir-style power entrenchment. And when the moment came — when 148 votes were all you needed from a 222-seat house where you command a comfortable majority — you couldn’t even get your own people to walk into the chamber. Six of your eight absent MPs haven’t even explained why they weren’t there. Fahmi says “no action has been taken.” Of course it hasn’t. Because in PH, reform promises are for campaigns, not for parliament. You had one job. You failed. And every Malaysian who believed your reformasi rhetoric is now paying the price for your incompetence.

Sources: Reuters | Bloomberg | CNA | CNA Commentary (March 13) | The Diplomat | Malay Mail | Fahmi on absent MPs (Malay Mail via Yahoo) | The Edge

item.title: PKR Hired US Consultants to Lobby Washington Against Najib — Now Cries Foul When Others Do the Same
item.description:
Said by: Sivarasa Rasiah, Former PKR Vice-President (confirmed); PKR leadership

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

In March 2026, MalaysiaNow published leaked emails revealing that PKR had engaged a Washington-based political consultancy firm in 2016 to lobby the United States to exert pressure on then-Prime Minister Najib Razak. The emails included an 84-page dossier making serious allegations against Najib, with the goal of getting Washington to publicly rebuke the Malaysian government.

Former PKR vice-president Sivarasa Rasiah confirmed the communications, saying party leaders including himself were involved. He framed it as legitimate advocacy to free Anwar Ibrahim, who was then a political prisoner.

The revelation came as Anwar’s government was simultaneously accusing former finance minister Daim Zainuddin’s family of using foreign agents to destabilise the current administration — calling it a national security threat.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This exposes a massive double standard at the heart of PH’s current political strategy. Anwar is framing Daim’s family’s alleged foreign lobbying as treasonous — a plot to topple the government using foreign influence. But his own party did the exact same thing when they were in opposition.

Opposition MP Shahidan Kassim pressed Anwar in Parliament to explain the difference. The answer matters because the government is using the Daim case to justify expanded national security rhetoric, deflect from the Azam Baki scandal, and paint critics as foreign agents. If PKR’s own lobbying was legitimate, then the same standard must apply to others.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Sivarasa’s defence — “we were trying to free a political prisoner” — is convenient but doesn’t hold up under scrutiny. The 84-page dossier didn’t just ask for Anwar’s release. It made sweeping allegations against Najib and explicitly sought US intervention in Malaysian domestic politics. That’s foreign lobbying. Period.

When PKR does it, it’s “advocacy.” When Daim’s family allegedly does it, it’s a “Zionist conspiracy” and a national security threat. The double standard is so blatant that even a casual observer can see through it.

Anwar’s government is essentially saying: foreign lobbying is patriotic when we do it, and treasonous when our opponents do it. That’s not a principle — it’s a convenience.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Malaysian political parties have a long history of international lobbying. UMNO maintained Washington lobbyists for decades. Anwar himself cultivated deep relationships with US policymakers, think tanks, and media during his years in opposition. None of this was considered problematic when PH was the beneficiary.

The selective outrage follows the same pattern as PH’s treatment of press freedom (Bloomberg good when exposing Najib, Bloomberg bad when exposing Azam Baki) and institutional accountability (MACC is weaponised under BN, MACC is independent under PH).

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You hired a US firm to lobby Washington against a sitting Malaysian PM, produced an 84-page dossier of allegations, and sought American intervention in Malaysian politics. Your own former vice-president confirmed it. Now you’re telling Malaysians that when others do the same thing, it’s a Zionist-backed conspiracy to topple the government? Pick a lane. Either foreign lobbying is a legitimate part of political advocacy — in which case stop criminalising your opponents for it — or it’s a national security threat, in which case PKR’s own leaders should be investigated too. You can’t have it both ways.

Sources: MalaysiaNow | MalaysiaNow - Dossier | The Star | Malaysiakini | Malaysiakini - Sivarasa

item.title: [BM] Anwar Menjalankan Buku Panduan Mahathir 2.0 — Orang Berbeza, Naluri Autokratik Sama
item.description:
Said by: Political Analysts / ISEAS / Toda Peace Institute / CSIS

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pelbagai analisis bebas daripada ISEAS, CSIS, Institut Keamanan Toda dan East Asia Forum berkumpul pada kesimpulan yang sama: gaya pemerintahan Anwar Ibrahim semakin mencerminkan gaya Mahathir Mohamad — orang yang memenjarakannya. Pemusatan kuasa (PM+MK), penggunaan undang-undang represif, tindakan keras terhadap media, pendakwaan terpilih, politik berasaskan kaum, pameran dasar luar sebagai gangguan domestik dan pengurusan gabungan melalui naungan.

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Anwar menghabiskan kehidupan politiknya sebagai ANTI-Mahathir. Seluruh naratifnya adalah: saya menderita di bawah sistem autokratik Mahathir, saya faham kejahatannya, dan saya akan membina sesuatu yang berbeza. Konvergensi antara gaya pemerintahan Anwar dan Mahathir bukan sekadar ironi — ia bukti muktamad bahawa budaya politik Malaysia, bukannya pemimpin individu, menghasilkan pemerintahan autokratik. Menukar wajah di puncak tidak mengubah sistem.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Undang-undang represif? Mahathir ada ISA, Anwar ada SOSMA dan Akta Hasutan. Kawalan media? Mahathir ada PPPA, Anwar ada sekatan SKMM dan kod etika wartawan. Naungan? Mahathir ada kroni, Anwar ada 47.5 pelantikan GLC/tahun. Politik kaum? Mahathir ada DEB, Anwar ada dasar Bumiputera ’tidak boleh dirunding’. Pemusatan kuasa? Mahathir pegang PM+MK, Anwar pegang PM+MK. Pameran dasar luar? Mahathir ada ’nilai Asia,’ Anwar ada ucapan Palestin. Senarai semak itu serupa.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Demokrasi Malaysia mempunyai corak: pemimpin pembangkang menjanjikan reformasi, memenangi kuasa, kemudian mentadbir seperti pendahulu mereka. Mahathir pada 1981 menjanjikan reformasi, kemudian membina sistem yang Anwar lawan. Anwar pada 2022 berjanji merobohkan sistem itu, kemudian menerimanya secara borong. Analisis CSIS menamakannya dengan sempurna: ‘Kematian Reformasi.’ Bukan kerana gerakan itu gagal dari luar — tetapi kerana pemimpinnya membunuhnya dari dalam.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Kamu adalah Mahathir 2.0 dengan bahasa Inggeris yang lebih baik dan kisah latar yang lebih bersimpati. Dasarnya sama. Struktur kuasanya sama. Naungannya sama. Autoritarianismenya sama. Satu-satunya bezanya ialah kamu TAHU ia salah kerana kamu menghabiskan 11 tahun dalam penjara kerananya. Dan kamu pilih untuk melakukannya juga. Itu bukan sekadar kemunafikan — itu pengkhianatan terhadap semua orang yang percaya kamu berbeza.

Sumber: CSIS — Kematian Reformasi | Institut Keamanan Toda — Laporan 184 | East Asia Forum — Aspirasi Reformasi

item.title: [BM] Anwar Menjalankan Buku Panduan Mahathir 2.0 — Orang Lain, Naluri Autoritarian Sama
item.description:
Said by: Political Analysts / ISEAS / Toda Peace Institute / CSIS

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pelbagai analisis bebas daripada ISEAS, CSIS, Toda Peace Institute, dan East Asia Forum bertumpu pada kesimpulan yang sama: gaya pemerintahan Anwar Ibrahim semakin mencerminkan Mahathir Mohamad — orang yang memenjarakannya. Pemusatan kuasa (PM+MK), penggunaan undang-undang menindas, tindakan keras terhadap media, pendakwaan terpilih, politik berasaskan kaum, berdiplomasi dasar luar sebagai pesongan dalam negeri, dan pengurusan gabungan melalui naungan.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Anwar menghabiskan kehidupan politiknya sebagai ANTI-Mahathir. Keseluruhan naratifnya ialah: saya menderita di bawah sistem autoritarian Mahathir, saya faham keburukannya, dan saya akan membina sesuatu yang berbeza. Menukar wajah di puncak tidak mengubah sistem.

Mahathir ada kroni, Anwar ada 47.5 pelantikan GLC/tahun. Politik kaum? Mahathir ada DEB, Anwar ada dasar Bumiputera ’tidak boleh dirunding.’ Pemusatan kuasa? Mahathir pegang PM+MK, Anwar pegang PM+MK. Bergaya dasar luar? Mahathir ada ’nilai Asia,’ Anwar ada ucapan Palestin. Senarai semak sama.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Demokrasi Malaysia mempunyai pola: pemimpin pembangkang janji reformasi, menang kuasa, kemudian memerintah seperti pendahulu mereka. Mahathir pada 1981 janji reformasi, kemudian membina sistem yang Anwar lawan. Anwar pada 2022 janji merungkai sistem itu, kemudian mengamalkannya sepenuhnya. Analisis CSIS memberinya tajuk yang sempurna: ‘Kematian Reformasi.’ Bukan kerana pergerakan gagal dari luar — tetapi kerana pemimpinnya membunuhnya dari dalam.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda adalah Mahathir 2.0 dengan bahasa Inggeris lebih baik dan kisah latar belakang lebih mengundang simpati. Dasarnya sama. Struktur kuasanya sama. Naungannya sama. Autoritarianismenya sama. Satu-satunya beza ialah anda TAHU ia salah kerana anda menghabiskan 11 tahun dalam penjara kerananya. Dan anda memilih untuk melakukannya juga. Itu bukan sekadar kemunafikan — itu pengkhianatan terhadap semua orang yang percaya anda berbeza.

Sumber: CSIS — Kematian Reformasi | Toda Peace Institute — Laporan 184 | East Asia Forum — Aspirasi Reformasi

item.title: Anwar Is Running Mahathir's Playbook 2.0 — Different Man, Same Authoritarian Instincts
item.description:
Said by: Political Analysts / ISEAS / Toda Peace Institute / CSIS

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Multiple independent analyses from ISEAS, CSIS, Toda Peace Institute, and East Asia Forum converge on the same conclusion: Anwar Ibrahim’s governance style increasingly mirrors that of Mahathir Mohamad — the man who imprisoned him. Centralisation of power (PM+FM), use of repressive laws, crackdown on media, selective prosecution, race-based politics, foreign policy grandstanding as domestic distraction, and coalition management through patronage.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Anwar spent his political life as the ANTI-Mahathir. His entire narrative was: I suffered under Mahathir’s authoritarian system, I understand its evils, and I will build something different. The convergence between Anwar’s governing style and Mahathir’s is not just ironic — it’s the final proof that Malaysian political culture, rather than individual leaders, produces authoritarian governance. Changing the face at the top doesn’t change the system.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Repressive laws? Mahathir had ISA, Anwar has SOSMA and the Sedition Act. Media control? Mahathir had PPPA, Anwar has MCMC blocks and journalist codes of ethics. Patronage? Mahathir had cronies, Anwar has 47.5 GLC appointees/year. Race politics? Mahathir had NEP, Anwar has ’non-negotiable’ Bumiputera policies. Concentration of power? Mahathir held PM+FM, Anwar holds PM+FM. Foreign policy preening? Mahathir had ‘Asian values,’ Anwar has Palestine speeches. The checklist is identical.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysian democracy has a pattern: opposition leaders promise reform, win power, then govern like their predecessors. Mahathir in 1981 promised reform, then built the system Anwar fought against. Anwar in 2022 promised to dismantle that system, then adopted it wholesale. The CSIS analysis titled it perfectly: ‘The Death of Reformasi.’ Not because the movement failed from outside — but because its leader killed it from within.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You are Mahathir 2.0 with better English and a more sympathetic backstory. The policies are the same. The power structures are the same. The patronage is the same. The authoritarianism is the same. The only difference is you KNOW it’s wrong because you spent 11 years in prison because of it. And you chose to do it anyway. That’s not just hypocrisy — it’s a betrayal of everyone who believed you were different.

Sources: CSIS — The Death of Reformasi | Toda Peace Institute — Report 184 | East Asia Forum — Reform Aspirations

item.title: [BM] Saman Fitnah RM100J Azam Baki Terhadap Bloomberg — Ketua SPRM Lawan Media Bukan Rasuah
item.description:
Said by: Bloomberg / Azam Baki, MACC Chief Commissioner

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-27

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 27 Februari 2026, dilaporkan bahawa Ketua Pesuruhjaya SPRM Azam Baki memfailkan saman fitnah RM100 juta terhadap Bloomberg. Saman itu datang selepas Bloomberg menerbitkan siasatan yang mendakwa Azam memiliki berjuta-juta saham dalam syarikat tersenarai awam — berpotensi melanggar had pemilikan penjawat awam. Bloomberg seterusnya mendakwa pegawai SPRM telah berpakat dengan ahli perniagaan untuk merancang pengambilalihan bermusuhan firma tersenarai, menggunakan serbuan dan ugutan untuk memaksa eksekutif menjual saham kepada kumpulan yang digelar “mafia korporat.” Respons Anwar ialah pasukan petugas — bukan siasatan bebas, bukan Suruhanjaya Diraja. DAP sendiri kata pasukan petugas “tidak cukup jauh.”

🔥 KENAPA INI PENTING: Ketua agensi antirasuah Malaysia sedang menyaman organisasi berita kewangan paling terkemuka di dunia kerana melaporkan dakwaan salah laku kewangannya. Ini bukan kes rakyat swasta mempertahankan reputasi — ini ketua penguatkuasa undang-undang antirasuah menggunakan sistem perundangan untuk menggeruni organisasi media yang menyiasatnya. Angka RM100J bukan tentang pampasan; ia tentang pencegahan. Agensi antirasuah melawan kewartawanan rasuah dan bukannya rasuah sebenar adalah puncak ironi Madani.

💩 KENAPA INI KARUT: Respons Anwar — pasukan petugas — adalah setara birokrasi dengan doa dan harapan. Pasukan petugas melapor kepada kerajaan. Siasatan bebas melapor kepada orang ramai. Anwar memilih mekanisme yang mengekalkan kawalan di tangannya, bukan yang memaksimumkan ketelusan. CNA melaporkan bahawa Anwar menghadapi “krisis integriti” dengan “perpecahan parti PKR” dan “kegelisahan gabungan PH.” Orang yang membina Reformasi atas janji tadbir urus bersih kini mengetuai kerajaan di mana ketua antirasuah menyaman wartawan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Skandal pemilikan saham Azam Baki pertama kali muncul pada awal 2022, apabila Malaysiakini melaporkan dia memegang saham dalam dua syarikat tersenarai melebihi had pemilikan perkhidmatan awam. Jawatankuasa parlimen khas membersihkannya — mencetuskan kemarahan meluas. Siasatan Bloomberg 2025-2026 melangkah lebih jauh, mendakwa pakatan sulit sistemik antara pegawai SPRM dan perompak korporat.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Kamu menjanjikan kebebasan SPRM. Sebaliknya, ketua SPRM menyaman Bloomberg kerana melakukan kerja SPRM. Siasatan bebas — bukan pasukan petugas — adalah respons minimum yang boleh diterima. Jika Azam tidak bersalah, siasatan membersihkannya. Jika dia bersalah, infrastruktur antirasuah negara perlu dibina semula dari asas. Pasukan petugas kamu adalah mekanisme penangguhan, bukan mekanisme akauntabiliti.
