item.title: [BM] Syed Saddiq Dibebaskan Selepas '5 Tahun Azab' — Adakah Ia Pendakwaan Politik?
item.description:
Said by: Court of Appeal / Prosecution

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-06-25

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Mahkamah Rayuan sebulat suara membebaskan Syed Saddiq daripada kesemua 4 pertuduhan, menegur Mahkamah Tinggi kerana tidak menolak kes itu lebih awal. Beliau telah dijatuhkan hukuman 7 tahun penjara dan sebatan oleh mahkamah bawahan. Pendakwaan kemudian merayu ke Mahkamah Persekutuan, yang memberikan ’tempoh bertenang’ yang jarang berlaku. SCMP memanggilnya ‘5 tahun azab.’

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Seorang ahli politik pembangkang muda telah melalui setengah dekad prosiding undang-undang yang Mahkamah Rayuan kata sepatutnya ditolak jauh lebih awal. Sama ada bermotif politik atau tidak, sistem telah mengecewakan beliau. Sabitan asal begitu lemah sehinggakan mahkamah rayuan jelas kecewa.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

Jika badan kehakiman boleh melakukan ini kepada seorang AP yang sedang berkhidmat, ia boleh melakukannya kepada sesiapa. Kes ini menimbulkan persoalan tentang budi bicara pendakwaan di bawah beberapa Peguam Negara berturut-turut — dan mengapa kerajaan merayu walaupun selepas pembebasan. Pendakwaan terpilih kekal sebagai senjata politik paling berkuasa di Malaysia.

🎯 Seruan PH

Anda mendakwa memperjuangkan kebebasan kehakiman dan proses sewajarnya. Tetapi apabila seorang AP pembangkang dibebaskan selepas lima tahun azab — dan perkhidmatan pendakwaan anda masih merayu — anda bukan mempertahankan keadilan, anda memanjangkan penganiayaan. Tarik balik rayuan atau jelaskan mengapa keputusan sebulat suara Mahkamah Rayuan tidak mencukupi.

Sumber

  • SCMP — Syed Saddiq Dibebaskan
  • FMT — Tempoh Bertenang Mahkamah Persekutuan

item.title: Syed Saddiq Acquitted After '5 Years of Hell' — Was It Political Prosecution?
item.description:
Said by: Court of Appeal / Prosecution

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-06-25

📌 What Happened

Court of Appeal unanimously acquitted Syed Saddiq of all 4 charges, admonishing the High Court for not throwing out the case earlier. He had been sentenced to 7 years jail and caning by the lower court. The prosecution then appealed to the Federal Court, which gave a rare ‘cooling-off period.’ SCMP called it ‘5 years of hell.’

🔥 Why It Matters

A young opposition politician was put through half a decade of legal proceedings that the Court of Appeal said should have been dismissed far earlier. Whether or not it was politically motivated, the system failed him. The original conviction was so weak that the appellate court was visibly frustrated.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

If the judiciary can do this to a sitting MP, it can do it to anyone. The case raises questions about prosecutorial discretion under successive AGs — and why the government appealed even after the acquittal. Selective prosecution remains Malaysia’s most potent political weapon.

🎯 PH Callout

You claim to champion judicial independence and due process. But when an opposition MP is acquitted after five years of hell — and your prosecution service still appeals — you’re not defending justice, you’re prolonging persecution. Drop the appeal or explain why the Court of Appeal’s unanimous verdict isn’t good enough.

Sources

  • SCMP — Syed Saddiq Acquitted
  • FMT — Federal Court Cooling-Off

item.title: [BM] CSIS Isytihar 'Kematian Reformasi' — Gerakan Anwar Sendiri Diisytihar Mati Oleh Badan Pemikir Washington
item.description:
Said by: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS), salah satu badan pemikir dasar paling berpengaruh di Washington, menerbitkan analisis yang memusnahkan bertajuk ‘Kematian Reformasi: Anwar Ibrahim, UMNO, dan Pengkhianatan Sebuah Gerakan.’ Artikel itu berhujah bahawa identiti politik Anwar telah ‘semakin ditakrifkan oleh kompromi yang beliau buat untuk memastikan kedudukannya dalam kerajaan.’

🔥 Mengapa Ia Penting

Reformasi bukan sekadar gerakan politik — ia adalah janji generasi. Berjuta-juta rakyat Malaysia, daripada pelajar universiti hingga keluarga kelas pekerja, melaburkan berdekad harapan, bantahan, dan pengorbanan dalam kepercayaan bahawa Anwar Ibrahim mewakili politik yang berbeza secara asasi. CSIS mengisytiharkannya mati bukan retorik pembangkang — ia adalah keputusan pertubuhan dasar antarabangsa.

💩 Mengapa Ia Mengarut

Anwar menghabiskan 11 tahun dalam penjara, menahan dua perbicaraan liwat, terselamat daripada pengasingan politik, dan membina seluruh gerakan atas satu perkataan: reformasi. Beliau meminta rakyat Malaysia mempercayainya. Mereka percaya. Dan apa yang mereka dapat? Gabungan dengan UMNO, parti yang memenjarakannya. Zahid Hamidi, yang menjadi anjing serang UMNO, kini timbalan beliau. Orang yang melawan sistem ADALAH sistem itu.

📜 Kes-Kes Terdahulu

Pada 2018, PH menang atas mandat reformasi tetapi runtuh dalam 22 bulan akibat pengkhianatan dalaman (Langkah Sheraton). Anwar sepatutnya menjadi pembetulan — pemimpin yang akhirnya akan menyampaikan. Sebaliknya, beliau membentuk gabungan yang lebih berkompromi daripada PH 1.0.

🎯 Seruan PH

Anda membina gerakan atas janji perubahan. Anda meminta rakyat berarak, berisiko ditangkap, untuk percaya. Dan kemudian anda berganding dengan orang yang tepat yang mereka bantah. Itu bukan pragmatisme — itu pengkhianatan muktamad. Reformasi tidak mati kerana ia gagal. Ia mati kerana anda membunuhnya.

Sumber

  • CSIS — Kematian Reformasi

item.title: CSIS Declares 'The Death of Reformasi' — Anwar's Own Movement Pronounced Dead by Washington Think Tank
item.description:
Said by: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-06-01

📌 What Happened

The Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS), one of Washington’s most influential policy think tanks, published a devastating analysis titled ‘The Death of Reformasi: Anwar Ibrahim, UMNO, and the Betrayal of a Movement.’ The piece argues that Anwar’s political identity has been ‘increasingly defined by the compromises he has made to secure his position within the government.’

🔥 Why It Matters

Reformasi wasn’t just a political movement — it was a generational promise. Millions of Malaysians, from university students to working-class families, invested decades of hope, protest, and sacrifice in the belief that Anwar Ibrahim represented a fundamentally different kind of politics. CSIS declaring it dead isn’t opposition rhetoric — it’s the verdict of the international policy establishment.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar spent 11 years in prison, endured two sodomy trials, survived political exile, and built an entire movement around one word: reform. He asked Malaysians to trust him. They did. And what did they get? A coalition with UMNO, the very party that imprisoned him. Zahid Hamidi, who was UMNO’s attack dog, is now his deputy. The man who fought the system IS the system.

📜 Previous Cases

In 2018, PH won on a reform mandate but collapsed within 22 months due to internal betrayal (Sheraton Move). Anwar was supposed to be the corrective — the leader who would finally deliver. Instead, he formed an even more compromised coalition than PH 1.0.

🎯 PH Callout

You built a movement on the promise of change. You asked people to march, to risk arrest, to believe. And then you partnered with the exact people they marched against. That’s not pragmatism — that’s the ultimate betrayal. Reformasi isn’t dead because it failed. It’s dead because you killed it.

Sources

  • CSIS — The Death of Reformasi

item.title: [BM] Nik Nazmi Letak Jawatan Menteri Alam Sekitar Selepas Kalah Pilihan Raya Parti PKR — Dedahkan Politik Feudal PH
item.description:
Said by: Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, Former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-28

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 28 Mei 2025, Menteri Sumber Asli dan Kelestarian Alam Sekitar Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad menyerahkan perletakan jawatan daripada Kabinet, berkuat kuasa 4 Julai. Perletakan jawatannya datang beberapa jam selepas Menteri Ekonomi Rafizi Ramli juga meletak jawatan — kedua-duanya berikutan kekalahan mereka dalam pilihan raya dalaman PKR lima hari sebelumnya.

Nik Nazmi kehilangan jawatan Naib Presiden PKR dan berkata beliau tidak boleh lagi berkhidmat sebagai menteri tanpa mandat parti sepenuhnya. “Menjadi menteri boleh memerangkap anda dalam ‘gelembung’ — dengan keistimewaan, pengiring keselamatan, dan protokol di setiap acara,” katanya. “Saya percaya terlalu melekat dengan gaya hidup sedemikian boleh berbahaya.”

Anwar secara terbuka meminta Nik Nazmi mempertimbangkan semula. Nik Nazmi enggan, berkata keputusannya muktamad dan beliau akan menumpukan kepada berkhidmat kepada pengundi Setiawangsanya.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING

Dua menteri Kabinet meletak jawatan dalam beberapa jam antara satu sama lain atas politik parti dalaman bukanlah tadbir urus biasa. Ia mendedahkan hakikat bahawa pelantikan menteri PH pada asasnya dipacu oleh dinamik puak parti, bukan kecekapan atau kepentingan nasional.

Pilihan raya parti PKR yang mencetuskan perletakan jawatan ini sendiri dicemari dakwaan penipuan undi dan nepotisme — anak perempuan Anwar, Nurul Izzah, menewaskan Rafizi untuk jawatan timbalan presiden. Apabila anak perempuan PM menang pertandingan dalaman dan yang kalah dipaksa keluar daripada Kabinet, itu bukan pembaharuan demokratik. Itu politik feudal berselindung di sebalik retorik reformasi.

Pemergian serentak Menteri Ekonomi dan Menteri Alam Sekitar mewujudkan ketidaktentuan dasar berbulan-bulan dalam dua portfolio kritikal, dengan kekosongan tidak diisi sehingga Disember 2025.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT

Nik Nazmi layak menerima sedikit pujian atas pendiriannya yang berprinsip — beliau antara sedikit ahli politik Malaysia yang rela melepaskan kuasa. Tetapi sistem yang menghasilkan keputusan ini busuk.

Pilihan raya parti PKR sepatutnya menjadi latihan demokratik dalaman. Sebaliknya, ia menjadi perang proksi untuk penyatuan kuasa keluarga Anwar. Kemenangan Nurul Izzah ke atas Rafizi bukan sekadar pertandingan parti — ia adalah anak perempuan PM menewaskan teknokrat ekonomi paling cekap beliau, membawa kepada kekosongan dasar berbulan-bulan di Kementerian Ekonomi.

Dan respons Anwar? Beliau memohon Nik Nazmi untuk kekal — bukan kerana kesinambungan dasar, tetapi kerana dua perletakan jawatan menteri nampak buruk. PM tidak mampu mengawal kesannya daripada pilihan raya partinya sendiri. Ini parti ‘reformis’ yang kononnya berbeza daripada politik feudal UMNO.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Sejarah UMNO dipenuhi pergolakan kuasa dalaman yang melimpah ke dalam tadbir urus — pergaduhan puak antara Mahathir dan Ku Li, antara Mahathir dan Anwar, antara Najib dan Muhyiddin. PKR sepatutnya berbeza. Ia diasaskan atas prinsip reformasi. Sebaliknya, ia mereplikakan politik dalaman berasaskan naungan yang sama, lengkap dengan pertempuran penggantian dinasti.

Pilihan raya PKR 2025 juga menggemakan pilihan raya parti 2018, yang turut dicemari dakwaan penyelewengan serupa. Parti ini tidak pernah berjaya mengadakan pilihan raya dalaman yang bersih dalam seluruh sejarahnya.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Anda membina PKR atas janji reformasi demokratik. Kemudian pilihan raya parti anda dicemari dakwaan penipuan undi, anak perempuan anda menewaskan reformis ekonomi paling terkemuka anda, dan dua menteri meletak jawatan sebagai protes. Hasilnya? Kekosongan dasar berbulan-bulan di kementerian Ekonomi dan Alam Sekitar. Ini bukan reformasi — ini UMNO dengan jenama yang lebih baik. Jika PKR tidak boleh menjalankan pilihan raya dalaman yang bersih, mengapa rakyat Malaysia harus mempercayai anda untuk mereformasi negara?

Sumber: Reuters | Bloomberg | CNA | Malay Mail | ISEAS

item.title: [BM] Rafizi Ramli Letak Jawatan Menteri Ekonomi Selepas Dimalukan PKR — Reformis Yang Disingkirkan
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-28

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 28 Mei 2025, Menteri Ekonomi Rafizi Ramli menyerahkan perletakan jawatannya daripada Kabinet, berkuat kuasa 17 Jun. Sekutunya, Menteri Sumber Asli Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, turut mengumumkan pemergiannya, berkuat kuasa 4 Julai.

Rafizi berkata: “Saya memasuki politik untuk memupuk budaya politik baru yang berasaskan akauntabiliti dan mandat rakyat. Kekalahan saya baru-baru ini dalam pilihan raya PKR menandakan saya tidak lagi memiliki mandat parti.”

Beliau menyatakan bahawa di negara yang berprinsipkan demokrasi, “pemimpin yang kalah dalam pilihan raya parti harus memberi laluan kepada pemenang.” Tindakan terakhir beliau sebagai menteri adalah menyiapkan Rancangan Malaysia Ke-13.

Anwar dilaporkan menggesa Rafizi mempertimbangkan semula. Beliau enggan.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING

Rafizi adalah reformis paling berkredibiliti dalam Kabinet Anwar. Beliau mereka bentuk PADU, mendorong rasionalisasi subsidi, memperjuangkan tadbir urus berasaskan data, dan menjadi enjin intelektual di sebalik agenda reformasi ekonomi PH. Pemergiannya meninggalkan kekosongan pada saat Malaysia memerlukan pengawasan ekonomi yang mantap.

Bloomberg menyebutnya “tamparan kepada kerajaan Anwar.” Straits Times menyatakan pemergiannya “akan meninggalkan jurang pada masa reformasi ekonomi Malaysia terbantut.” CNA melaporkan pilihan raya parti “dicemari dakwaan penipuan undi dan nepotisme.”

Dua menteri meletak jawatan atas pilihan raya parti bukanlah biasa. Ia isyarat bahawa sesuatu amat tidak kena di dalam PKR.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT

Anwar kehilangan menteri terbaiknya kerana beliau membenarkan — atau membolehkan — anak perempuannya mencabar dan menghancurkan penyandang. Rafizi tidak kalah kerana tidak cekap. Beliau kalah kerana jentera parti digerakkan untuk anak perempuan bos.

Hakikat bahawa Anwar kemudian meminta Rafizi untuk kekal adalah bahagian paling sinis. Anda mendalangi penghinaan, kemudian meminta yang dihina untuk terus bekerja untuk anda. Itu bukan kepimpinan — itu manipulasi.

Sejak pemergian Rafizi, beliau menjadi pengkritik yang semakin lantang. Beliau memberi amaran kepada Anwar jangan “memandang rendah sentimen rakyat.” Beliau menggesa penggantungan ketua SPRM Azam Baki. Beliau kini sendiri di bawah siasatan SPRM — pemasaan yang pengkritik panggil sebagai tindakan balas.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Politik Malaysia mempunyai tradisi lama menyingkirkan saingan dalaman. Mahathir meminggirkan Anwar pada 1998. Kini Anwar secara efektif meminggirkan Rafizi pada 2025. Kaedahnya berbeza — pilihan raya parti dan bukannya tuduhan liwat — tetapi hasilnya sama: saingan paling berkebolehan pemimpin disingkirkan.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Anda kehilangan minda ekonomi terbaik anda kerana politik keluarga lebih penting daripada kepentingan nasional. Rafizi membina kredibiliti dasar anda. Beliau sebab pelabur memandang serius PH. Dan anda biarkan beliau pergi — atau menolaknya keluar. Rancangan Malaysia Ke-13 adalah hadiah terakhirnya kepada anda. Apa yang anda lakukan dengannya sejak itu?

Sumber: Straits Times | Bloomberg | FMT | CNA

item.title: Nik Nazmi Resigns as Environment Minister After Losing PKR Party Election — Exposes PH's Feudal Politics
item.description:
Said by: Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, Former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-28

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On May 28, 2025, Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad tendered his resignation from Cabinet, effective July 4. His resignation came hours after Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli also resigned — both following their defeats in PKR’s internal party elections five days earlier.

Nik Nazmi lost his PKR vice-president position and said he could no longer serve as minister without a full party mandate. “Being a minister can trap you in a ‘bubble’ — with privileges, security escorts, and protocols at every event,” he said. “I believe that becoming too attached to such a lifestyle can be dangerous.”

Anwar publicly asked Nik Nazmi to reconsider. Nik Nazmi refused, saying his decision was firm and he would focus on serving his Setiawangsa constituents.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

Two Cabinet ministers resigning within hours of each other over internal party politics is not normal governance. It exposes the fact that PH ministerial appointments are fundamentally driven by party faction dynamics, not competence or national interest.

The PKR party election that triggered these resignations was itself marred by allegations of vote-rigging and nepotism — Anwar’s daughter Nurul Izzah defeated Rafizi for deputy president. When the PM’s daughter wins an internal contest and the losers are forced out of Cabinet, that’s not democratic renewal. That’s feudal politics dressed up in reform rhetoric.

The simultaneous departure of the Economy Minister and Environment Minister created months of policy uncertainty in two critical portfolios, with vacancies unfilled until December 2025.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Nik Nazmi deserves some credit for his principled stance — he’s one of the few Malaysian politicians who voluntarily gave up power. But the system that produced this outcome is rotten.

PKR’s party elections are supposed to be internal democratic exercises. Instead, they became proxy wars for the Anwar family’s consolidation of power. Nurul Izzah’s victory over Rafizi wasn’t just a party contest — it was the PM’s daughter defeating his most capable economic technocrat, leading to months of policy vacuum in the Economy Ministry.

And Anwar’s response? He begged Nik Nazmi to stay — not because of policy continuity, but because two ministerial resignations looked bad. The PM couldn’t even control the fallout from his own party’s elections. This is the “reformist” party that’s supposed to be different from UMNO’s feudal politics.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

UMNO’s history is littered with internal power struggles that spilled into governance — factional fights between Mahathir and Ku Li, between Mahathir and Anwar, between Najib and Muhyiddin. PKR was supposed to be different. It was founded on reformasi principles. Instead, it’s replicated the exact same patronage-driven internal politics, complete with a dynastic succession battle.

The PKR 2025 election also echoed the 2018 party polls, which were marred by similar allegations of irregularities. The party has never managed a clean internal election in its entire history.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You built PKR on the promise of democratic reform. Then your party election was marred by vote-rigging allegations, your daughter defeated your most prominent economic reformer, and two ministers resigned in protest. The result? Months of policy vacuum in the Economy and Environment ministries. This isn’t reform — it’s UMNO with better branding. If PKR can’t even run a clean internal election, why should Malaysians trust you to reform the country?

Sources: Reuters | Bloomberg | CNA | Malay Mail | ISEAS

item.title: Rafizi Ramli Resigns as Economy Minister After PKR Humiliation — The Reformist Who Got Purged
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-28

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 28 May 2025, Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli submitted his resignation from Cabinet, effective 17 June. His ally, Natural Resources Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, also announced his exit, effective 4 July.

Rafizi said: “I entered politics to cultivate a new political culture grounded in accountability and the people’s mandate. My recent defeat in the PKR elections signifies that I no longer possess the party’s mandate.”

He noted that in countries with democratic principles, “leaders who lose in party elections should make way for the victors.” His final act as minister was completing the 13th Malaysia Plan.

Anwar reportedly urged Rafizi to reconsider. He refused.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

Rafizi was the most credible reformist in Anwar’s Cabinet. He designed PADU, pushed subsidy rationalisation, championed data-driven governance, and was the intellectual engine behind PH’s economic reform agenda. His departure left a vacuum at exactly the moment Malaysia needed steady economic stewardship.

Bloomberg called it “a blow to Anwar’s government.” The Straits Times noted his exit “will leave a gap at a time when Malaysia’s economic reforms are stalling.” CNA reported that the party election was “marred by allegations of vote-rigging and nepotism.”

Two ministers quitting over a party election isn’t normal. It’s a signal that something is deeply wrong inside PKR.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Anwar lost his best minister because he allowed — or enabled — his daughter to challenge and destroy the incumbent. Rafizi didn’t lose because he was incompetent. He lost because the party machinery was mobilised for the boss’s daughter.

The fact that Anwar then asked Rafizi to stay is the most cynical part. You orchestrate a humiliation, then ask the humiliated to keep working for you. That’s not leadership — that’s gaslighting.

Since Rafizi’s departure, he has become an increasingly vocal critic. He warned Anwar not to “underestimate public sentiment.” He called for MACC chief Azam Baki’s suspension. He’s now under MACC investigation himself — a timing that critics call retaliatory.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Malaysian politics has a long tradition of purging internal rivals. Mahathir sidelined Anwar in 1998. Now Anwar has effectively sidelined Rafizi in 2025. The method is different — party elections instead of sodomy charges — but the outcome is the same: the leader’s most capable rival is removed.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You lost your best economic mind because family politics mattered more than national interest. Rafizi built your policy credibility. He was the reason investors took PH seriously. And you let him walk — or pushed him out. The 13th Malaysia Plan was his last gift to you. What have you done with it since?

Sources: Straits Times | Bloomberg | FMT | CNA

item.title: [BM] Nurul Izzah Menang Timbalan Presiden PKR — Anwar Kata Demokrasi, Bukan Nepotisme
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister & PKR President

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-24

Daripada Penasihat Kepada Timbalan Presiden: Dinasti Anwar Terbentuk

Pada Mei 2025, Nurul Izzah Anwar dipilih sebagai Timbalan Presiden PKR — jawatan nombor dua dalam parti yang diketuai bapanya, Perdana Menteri yang sedang berkhidmat. Anwar Ibrahim menolak semua kritikan dengan lambaian tangan: dia dipilih oleh ahli. Kes ditutup. Kecuali ia tidak.

Apa Yang Berlaku

Di Kongres Nasional PKR di Johor Bahru pada 24 Mei 2025, Nurul Izzah memenangi pertandingan Timbalan Presiden. Jawatan presiden — yang dipegang Anwar — tidak dipertandingkan. Ini bermakna dua jawatan teratas PKR kini dipegang oleh ayah dan anak perempuan.

Respons Anwar adalah terus:

Orang akan kata isteri dan anak saya dalam parti. Itu mungkin benar, tetapi semasa kita berjuang pada 1998, kongres pun tak ada.

Masalah Dengan “Dipilih Oleh Ahli”

Ya, ahli parti mengundi. Tetapi konteks penting:

  • Anwar adalah presiden parti. Sokongannya — walaupun tidak dinyatakan — membawa pengaruh besar dalam pilihan raya dalaman. Ahli PKR tahu bahawa mengundi menentang anak perempuan presiden membawa risiko politik.
  • Naungan mengalir ke bawah. Dalam sistem di mana PM mengawal pelantikan kerajaan, kontrak, dan jawatan GLC, ahli parti yang bersekutu dengan keluarga kepimpinan berada dalam kedudukan lebih baik. Ini tekanan struktur, bukan pilihan bebas.
  • Bekas pembantu Ezam Nor menjadi tular menjelaskan bahawa ini kurang tentang demokrasi dan lebih tentang Anwar menyatukan kuasa menjelang cabaran politik dan perundangan yang dijangka.

Penjejak Dinasti

  • Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail (isteri Anwar): bekas Presiden PKR, bekas Timbalan PM
  • Nurul Izzah (anak perempuan): bekas AP, Penasihat Kanan Ekonomi kepada PM, kini Timbalan Presiden PKR
  • Keluarga Anwar telah memegang atau kini memegang dua jawatan parti teratas dan peranan penasihat kanan kerajaan Fakta: Tiada keluarga politik lain dalam gerakan reformis Malaysia memegang penumpuan kuasa ini dalam satu parti.

Hujah Terbaik (Dan Mengapa Ia Gagal)

Nurul Izzah telah berkecimpung dalam politik sejak usia dua puluhan, bertahan melawat penjara, berkempen semasa penahanan bapanya, dan mempunyai kelayakan dasar. Beliau bukan tidak layak. Tetapi persoalannya bukan tentang kelayakan — ia tentang sama ada parti reformis patut mempunyai struktur kepimpinan ayah-anak perempuan sementara bapanya juga Perdana Menteri. Di mana-mana negara demokrasi matang, ini akan dianggap bendera merah tadbir urus.

Apa Yang Patut Berlaku

  1. PKR patut menerima pakai peraturan menghalang ahli keluarga terdekat presiden daripada memegang jawatan timbalan presiden secara serentak.
  2. Anwar patut secara terbuka kekal berkecuali dalam pertandingan tersebut.
  3. Masyarakat sivil Malaysia patut berhenti menerima “ahli yang memilih” sebagai jawapan lengkap apabila bapa yang memilih mengawal parti dan kerajaan.

Keputusan: Gerakan reformasi lahir daripada penentangan terhadap penumpuan kuasa Mahathir. Tiga dekad kemudian, penumpuan baru sedang terbentuk — kali ini dengan lambang keluarga padanya.

*Sumber: Malay Mail · Free Malaysia Today · MalaysiaNow · *SCMP

item.title: Nurul Izzah Wins PKR Deputy President — Anwar Says It's Democracy, Not Nepotism
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister & PKR President

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-24

From Adviser to Deputy President: The Anwar Dynasty Takes Shape

In May 2025, Nurul Izzah Anwar was elected PKR Deputy President — the number two position in the party led by her father, the sitting Prime Minister. Anwar Ibrahim dismissed all criticism with a wave of the hand: she was chosen by members. Case closed. Except it isn’t.

What Happened

At the PKR National Congress in Johor Bahru on 24 May 2025, Nurul Izzah won the contest for Deputy President. The presidency — held by Anwar — was not up for contest. This means the top two positions in PKR are now held by father and daughter.

Anwar’s response was direct:

People will say that my wife and daughter are in the party. That may be true, but when we were fighting in 1998, there wasn’t even a congress.

The Problem With “Chosen by Members”

Yes, party members voted. But context matters:

  • Anwar is the party president. His endorsement — even if unstated — carries enormous weight in internal elections. PKR members know that voting against the president’s daughter carries political risk.
  • Patronage flows downward. In a system where the PM controls government appointments, contracts, and GLC positions, party members who align with the leadership family are better positioned. This is structural pressure, not free choice.
  • Former aide Ezam Nor went viral explaining that this was less about democracy and more about Anwar consolidating power ahead of anticipated political and legal challenges.

The Dynasty Tracker

  • Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail (Anwar’s wife): former PKR President, former Deputy PM
  • Nurul Izzah (daughter): former MP, Senior Economic Adviser to PM, now PKR Deputy President
  • The Anwar family has held or currently holds the top two party positions and a senior government advisory role Fact: No other political family in Malaysia’s reformist movement holds this concentration of power within a single party.

The Steelman (and Why It Fails)

Nurul Izzah has been in politics since her twenties, survived prison visits, campaigned during her father’s incarceration, and has policy credentials. She is not unqualified. But the question is not about qualifications — it is about whether a reformist party should have a father-daughter leadership structure while the father is also Prime Minister. In any mature democracy, this would be considered a governance red flag.

What Should Happen

  1. PKR should adopt rules preventing immediate family members of the president from holding the deputy presidency simultaneously.
  2. Anwar should have publicly stayed neutral in the contest.
  3. Malaysian civil society should stop accepting “the members chose” as a complete answer when the chooser’s father controls the party and the government.

Verdict: The reformasi movement was born out of opposition to Mahathir’s concentration of power. Three decades later, a new concentration is forming — this time with a family crest on it.

*Sources: Malay Mail · Free Malaysia Today · MalaysiaNow · *SCMP

item.title: [BM] Nurul Izzah Tewaskan Rafizi Dengan 6,000 Undi Untuk Timbalan Presiden PKR — Parti Ayah, Peraturan Ayah
item.description:
Said by: PKR Party Elections / Nurul Izzah Anwar

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-23

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada 23 Mei 2025, Nurul Izzah Anwar — anak sulung Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim — menewaskan timbalan presiden penyandang Rafizi Ramli dalam pilihan raya dalaman PKR dengan margin yang meyakinkan: 9,803 undi berbanding 3,866.

Keputusannya tidak pernah diragui. Lebih 200 daripada 222 bahagian PKR telah mengisytiharkan sokongan untuk Nurul Izzah sebelum undian dijalankan. Pilihan raya diadakan di Johor Bahru, dengan presiden parti Anwar Ibrahim mengekalkan jawatan teratas tanpa dicabar.

Beberapa hari kemudian, Rafizi meletak jawatan sebagai Menteri Ekonomi, berkata beliau “tidak lagi memiliki mandat parti.” Sekutunya, Menteri Sumber Asli Nik Nazmi, turut meletak jawatan.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING

Anak perempuan PM kini orang kedua paling berkuasa dalam parti yang mentadbir Malaysia. Ini bukan sekadar pilihan raya parti — ia adalah penyatuan dinasti.

Anwar Ibrahim menghabiskan 25 tahun berkhutbah tentang reformasi. Beliau berjanji untuk memutuskan kitaran naungan, nepotisme, dan tangkapan elit yang mendefinisikan politik Malaysia. Kini anak perempuannya memegang jawatan No. 2 parti, isterinya presiden PKR Wanita, dan penentang politiknya di dalam parti telah dipinggirkan secara sistematik.

SCMP melaporkannya sebagai “kontroversi nepotisme.” Bloomberg menyebutnya “tamparan kepada kerajaan Anwar.” Hanya di PKR ada yang berpura-pura ini pertandingan demokratik biasa.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT

Mari kita jelaskan apa yang berlaku: anak perempuan PM bertanding menentang Menteri Ekonominya sendiri untuk kawalan parti, dan jentera parti menyampaikan kemenangan besar untuk anak bos. Lebih 200 bahagian mengisytiharkan untuk Nurul Izzah sebelum sehelai undi dibuang. Itu bukan demokrasi — itu pertabalan.

Rafizi — orang yang membina kredibiliti dasar PKR, yang mereka bentuk PADU, yang mendorong rasionalisasi subsidi — diganjar dengan penghinaan. Mesej kepada setiap reformis dalam PKR tidak boleh disalah erti: kesetiaan kepada keluarga mengatasi kecekapan.

Nurul Izzah bahkan ditawarkan peluang untuk mewujudkan jawatan “timbalan presiden kedua” untuk Rafizi. Beliau tidak menafikannya. Hadiah saguhati kerana kalah kepada anak perempuan bos adalah gelaran yang dicipta. Itu bukan parti politik — itu firma keluarga.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Anwar sudah melantik Nurul Izzah sebagai Penasihat Kanan Ekonomi pada Januari 2023, walaupun beliau kehilangan kerusi parlimen. Beliau menolak dakwaan nepotisme dengan mendefinisikan semula perkataan itu. Kini corak itu meningkat: daripada penasihat tidak dipilih kepada timbalan presiden parti. Perhentian seterusnya: Timbalan PM?

Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang dinasti politik — Mahathir memupuk anaknya Mukhriz, Najib mewarisi legasi bapanya. Anwar berjanji untuk berbeza. Beliau tidak.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Anda beritahu kami reformasi tentang memutuskan cara lama. Tentang meritokrasi. Tentang menamatkan politik naungan dan keluarga. Anak perempuan anda kini mengawal slot No. 2 parti anda dengan margin 6,000 undi yang direkayasa sebelum undi dicetak. Menteri Ekonomi anda meletak jawatan sebagai protes. Agenda reformasi anda kehilangan juara paling berkredibilitinya. Dan anda memanggilnya demokrasi. Satu-satunya yang direformasi dalam PKR ialah cengkaman keluarga ke atas kuasa.

Sumber: Straits Times | Malay Mail | SCMP | Bloomberg

item.title: Nurul Izzah Defeats Rafizi by 6,000 Votes for PKR Deputy President — Daddy's Party, Daddy's Rules
item.description:
Said by: PKR Party Elections / Nurul Izzah Anwar

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-05-23

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 23 May 2025, Nurul Izzah Anwar — the eldest daughter of Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim — defeated incumbent deputy president Rafizi Ramli in PKR’s internal elections by a commanding margin: 9,803 votes to 3,866.

The result was never in doubt. Over 200 of PKR’s 222 divisions had declared support for Nurul Izzah before the vote even took place. The election was held in Johor Bahru, with party president Anwar Ibrahim retaining the top post unchallenged.

Days later, Rafizi resigned as Economy Minister, saying he “no longer possesses the party’s mandate.” His ally, Natural Resources Minister Nik Nazmi, also quit.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

The PM’s daughter is now the second-most powerful person in the party that runs Malaysia. This isn’t just a party election — it’s a dynastic consolidation.

Anwar Ibrahim spent 25 years preaching reformasi. He promised to break the cycle of patronage, nepotism, and elite capture that defined Malaysian politics. Now his daughter holds the No. 2 party position, his wife is the president of PKR Wanita, and his political opponents within the party have been systematically sidelined.

The SCMP reported it as a “nepotism row.” Bloomberg called it a “blow to Anwar’s government.” Only in PKR would anyone pretend this was a normal democratic contest.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

Let’s be clear about what happened: the PM’s daughter ran against his own Economy Minister for control of the party, and the party apparatus delivered a landslide for the boss’s kid. Over 200 divisions declared for Nurul Izzah before a single ballot was cast. That’s not democracy — that’s a coronation.

Rafizi — the man who built PKR’s policy credibility, who designed PADU, who pushed subsidy rationalisation — was rewarded with humiliation. The message to every reformist in PKR is unmistakable: loyalty to the family trumps competence.

Nurul Izzah was even offered the chance to create a “second deputy president” post for Rafizi. She didn’t deny it. The consolation prize for losing to the boss’s daughter is a made-up title. That’s not a political party — it’s a family firm.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Anwar already appointed Nurul Izzah as Senior Economic Adviser in January 2023, despite her losing her parliamentary seat. He dismissed nepotism accusations by redefining the word. Now the pattern escalates: from unelected adviser to party deputy president. Next stop: Deputy PM?

Malaysia has a long history of political dynasties — Mahathir groomed his son Mukhriz, Najib inherited his father’s legacy. Anwar promised to be different. He isn’t.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You told us reformasi was about breaking the old ways. About meritocracy. About ending the politics of patronage and family. Your daughter now controls your party’s No. 2 slot with a 6,000-vote margin that was engineered before the ballots were printed. Your Economy Minister quit in protest. Your reform agenda lost its most credible champion. And you called it democracy. The only thing reformed in PKR is the family’s grip on power.

Sources: Straits Times | Malay Mail | SCMP | Bloomberg

item.title: [BM] 'Penasihat' Steven Sim Rupanya Suspek Penipuan RM10 Juta — Menteri Dakwa Tak Pernah Lantik Sesiapa
item.description:
Said by: Steven Sim, Human Resources Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-03-31

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU

Pada Mac 2025, seorang ahli perniagaan bergelar “Tan Sri” ditahan oleh pasukan pencegahan pengubahan wang haram Bukit Aman kerana didakwa menipu orang ramai sebanyak RM10 juta. Lelaki berusia 59 tahun itu, yang mempunyai 30 tahun pengalaman dalam industri minyak dan gas, ditahan sebaik pulang ke rumahnya di Jalan U Thant, KL, selepas perjalanan dari Osaka, Jepun.

Sebuah portal berita kemudian melaporkan ahli perniagaan yang ditahan itu mendakwa berkhidmat sebagai penasihat kepada Menteri Sumber Manusia Steven Sim. Setiausaha akhbar Sim, Abdul Hakim Ab Rahman, segera mengeluarkan penafian, menyatakan menteri “tidak pernah melantik mana-mana individu sebagai penasihatnya.”

Kementerian mengarahkan semua pertanyaan tentang jawatan dalam pejabat menteri kepada “saluran rasmi” tetapi tidak memberi penjelasan bagaimana seorang suspek penipuan dikaitkan secara terbuka dengan menteri.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING

Ini adalah kegagalan tadbir urus tanpa mengira sama ada Sim benar-benar melantik lelaki itu atau tidak. Jika lelaki itu secara palsu mendakwa sebagai penasihat menteri, ia menimbulkan persoalan mengapa pejabat Sim tidak tahu lebih awal dan mengapa tindakan tidak diambil sebelum lelaki itu ditahan untuk penipuan RM10 juta yang berasingan.

Jika lelaki itu memang mempunyai hubungan tidak rasmi dengan menteri — walaupun tanpa surat pelantikan rasmi — maka penafian menyeluruh Sim adalah mengelirukan. Dalam politik Malaysia, “penasihat” sering beroperasi dalam zon kelabu tanpa dokumentasi rasmi, tepat untuk mengekalkan penafian yang munasabah.

Walau apa pun, hakikat bahawa suspek penipuan RM10 juta bergerak menggunakan nama menteri PH untuk membina kredibiliti adalah kegagalan tapisan dan tadbir urus yang serius.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT

Kepantasan penafian itu memberi petanda. Dalam beberapa jam berita tersebar, pejabat Sim mengeluarkan kenyataan mengatakan menteri “tidak pernah melantik mana-mana individu sebagai penasihatnya.” Tiada siasatan. Tiada penjelasan. Hanya penafian rata.

Tetapi inilah yang tidak dijawab oleh penafian itu: Bagaimana seorang lelaki yang didakwa menipu orang ramai RM10 juta dikaitkan secara terbuka dengan menteri Kabinet yang sedang berkhidmat? Adakah sebarang hubungan sosial? Sebarang acara dihadiri bersama? Sebarang gambar? Penafian menjawab soalan pelantikan rasmi tetapi mengelak soalan substantif tentang hubungan tersebut.

Menteri PH suka bercakap tentang ketelusan dan akauntabiliti. Ini ujiannya: keluarkan akaun penuh semua orang yang mempunyai akses kepada atau bekerja dengan pejabat menteri, secara rasmi atau tidak rasmi. Sehingga itu, penafian hanyalah pengurusan krisis PR.

📜 KES TERDAHULU

Malaysia mempunyai tradisi lama pemudah cara yang berkait politik, “penasihat,” dan perantara yang menggunakan kedekatan dengan kuasa untuk membina kredibiliti perniagaan. Kontroversi pelantikan “penasihat” Nurul Izzah adalah kes serupa seorang tokoh PH dikaitkan dengan pelantikan yang dipersoalkan. Di bawah BN, amalan ini berleluasa — “setiausaha politik” dan “pegawai khas” beroperasi sebagai penjaga pintu tidak rasmi dengan kuasa tidak rasmi yang besar.

PH berjanji untuk membersihkan ini. Hakikat bahawa suspek penipuan masih mendakwa hubungan menteri menunjukkan pembersihan itu belum berlaku.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Anda berjanji Kerajaan Madani akan berbeza — tiada lagi kronisme, tiada lagi pemudah cara, tiada lagi ahli perniagaan berkait politik menggunakan nama menteri untuk keuntungan peribadi. Kemudian suspek penipuan RM10 juta rupanya secara terbuka mendakwa sebagai penasihat menteri anda, dan yang terbaik anda boleh lakukan adalah siaran akhbar berkata “kami tidak pernah melantik sesiapa.” Itu bukan akauntabiliti — itu kawalan kerosakan. Jika anda tidak boleh mengawal siapa yang menggunakan nama menteri anda, bagaimana anda mengawal siapa yang menggunakan pengaruh mereka?

Sumber: Malay Mail | The Vibes | Bernama | FMT

item.title: Steven Sim's 'Adviser' Turns Out to Be Alleged RM10 Million Fraudster — Minister Claims He Never Appointed Anyone
item.description:
Said by: Steven Sim, Human Resources Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-03-31

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

In March 2025, a businessman with the title “Tan Sri” was arrested by Bukit Aman’s anti-money laundering team for allegedly defrauding the public of RM10 million. The 59-year-old, who had 30 years of experience in the oil and gas industry, was apprehended upon returning to his home at Jalan U Thant, KL, after a trip to Osaka, Japan.

A news portal then reported that the arrested businessman had been claiming to serve as an adviser to Human Resources Minister Steven Sim. Sim’s press secretary Abdul Hakim Ab Rahman quickly issued a denial, stating that the minister “has never appointed any individual as his advisor.”

The ministry directed all inquiries about positions within the minister’s office to “official channels” but offered no explanation for how an alleged fraudster came to be publicly associated with the minister.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This is a governance failure regardless of whether Sim actually appointed the man or not. If the man was falsely claiming to be the minister’s adviser, it raises questions about why Sim’s office didn’t know about it sooner and why action wasn’t taken before the man was arrested for a completely separate RM10 million fraud.

If the man did have some informal relationship with the minister — even without a formal appointment letter — then Sim’s blanket denial is misleading. In Malaysian politics, “advisers” often operate in grey zones without official documentation, precisely to maintain plausible deniability.

Either way, the fact that an alleged RM10 million fraudster was running around using a PH minister’s name to build credibility is a serious vetting and governance failure.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT

The speed of the denial is telling. Within hours of the news breaking, Sim’s office issued a statement saying the minister “has never appointed any individual as his advisor.” No investigation. No explanation. Just a flat denial.

But here’s what the denial doesn’t address: How did a man alleged to have defrauded the public of RM10 million come to be publicly associated with a sitting Cabinet minister? Was there any social contact? Any events attended together? Any photos? The denial addresses the formal appointment question but dodges the substantive question of the relationship.

PH ministers love to talk about transparency and accountability. Here’s a test: release a full accounting of everyone who has access to or works with the minister’s office, formally or informally. Until then, the denial is just PR crisis management.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES

Malaysia has a long tradition of politically connected fixers, “advisers,” and intermediaries who use their proximity to power to build business credibility. The Nurul Izzah “adviser” appointment controversy was a similar case of a PH figure being linked to questionable appointments. Under BN, the practice was endemic — “political secretaries” and “special officers” operated as unofficial gatekeepers with enormous informal power.

PH promised to clean this up. The fact that alleged fraudsters are still claiming ministerial connections suggests the cleanup hasn’t happened.

🎯 PH CALLOUT

You promised Kerajaan Madani would be different — no more cronyism, no more fixers, no more politically connected businessmen using ministerial names for personal gain. Then an alleged RM10 million fraudster turns out to be publicly claiming he’s your minister’s adviser, and the best you can do is a press release saying “we never appointed anyone.” That’s not accountability — that’s damage control. If you can’t even control who’s using your ministers’ names, how are you controlling who’s using their influence?

Sources: Malay Mail | The Vibes | Bernama | FMT

item.title: [BM] Anwar Serang Balik Pengkritik Yang Kata Dia Terlalu Banyak Melancong — 'Mereka Tak Faham Kerja Keras'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-02-27

PM Terbang Yang Tak Boleh Terima Kritikan

Apabila rakyat Malaysia mempersoalkan mengapa PM mereka sentiasa di luar negara sementara krisis domestik meningkat, respons Anwar bukan empati — ia kemarahan. Beliau menyerang pengkritik, berkata mereka “jelas tidak menyedari kerja keras” yang terlibat dalam perjalanan luar negara.

Fakta

Fakta: Anwar mempertahankan jadual perjalanan luar negaranya yang meluas, menolak pengkritik sebagai tidak berpengetahuan.

Fakta: Pada November 2024, pendedahannya sendiri bahawa syarikat swasta membiayai 75% kos perjalanan menimbulkan lebih banyak persoalan.

Fakta: Sementara PM melancong, rakyat Malaysia menghadapi: kos sara hidup meningkat, krisis penjagaan kesihatan, kemerosotan pendidikan, perumahan tak mampu milik, dan kemunduran demokrasi.

Mengapa Ia Penting

  • Setiap PM melancong. Persoalannya sama ada hasilnya membenarkan kekerapan.
  • Menolak persoalan sah tentang keutamaan perjalanan sebagai kejahilan adalah keangkuhan yang sama yang PH berjanji untuk hapuskan.
  • Rakyat Malaysia tidak berkira soal kerja diplomatik. Mereka berkira apabila PM berkuliah tentang kerja keras sementara mereka tak mampu beli barangan runcit. Sumber: Pelbagai laporan berita

item.title: [BM] East Asia Forum: 'Aspirasi Reformasi Bertembung Dengan Realiti Politik Pada 2024'
item.description:
Said by: Kai Ostwald, University of British Columbia

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-02-27

Academic analysis: ‘Anwar pivoted from political survival in 2023 to pursuing an ambitious reform agenda in 2024, with notable gains in economic growth. Yet political turbulence — driven by identity-based populism and an unwieldy coalition — continued to constrain domestic reforms.’ Sources: https://eastasiaforum.org/2025/02/27/malaysias-reform-aspirations-clashed-with-political-realities-in-2024/

item.title: Anwar Hits Back at Critics Who Say He Travels Too Much — 'They Don't Understand Hard Work'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-02-27

The Jet-Setting PM Who Can’t Take Criticism

When Malaysians questioned why their PM was constantly abroad while domestic crises mounted, Anwar’s response was not empathy — it was indignation. He hit out at critics, saying they were “clearly unaware of the hard work” that goes into overseas trips.

The Facts

Fact: Anwar defended his extensive overseas travel schedule, dismissing critics as uninformed.

Fact: In November 2024, his own disclosure that private companies funded 75% of travel costs raised further questions.

Fact: While the PM travels, Malaysians face: rising costs, healthcare crisis, education decline, housing unaffordability, and democratic backsliding.

Why It Matters

  • Every PM travels. The question is whether the results justify the frequency.
  • Dismissing legitimate questions about travel priorities as ignorance is the same arrogance PH promised to end.
  • Malaysians don’t begrudge diplomatic work. They begrudge a PM who lectures them about hard work while they can’t afford groceries. Sources: Multiple news reports

item.title: [BM] Najib Dapat Tahanan Rumah — 'Hukuman' Paling Selesa Dunia Untuk Kleptokrasi Terbesar Dunia
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Pardons Board

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-02-02

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Bekas PM Najib Razak, disabitkan kesalahan rasuah dalam skandal 1MDB yang merompak berbilion daripada rakyat Malaysia, mendapat hukuman dipotong separuh melalui pengampunan diraja dan seterusnya dipindahkan ke tahanan rumah di kediamannya yang mewah di Kuala Lumpur. Orang yang disabitkan mencuri daripada rakyat kini menjalani ‘hukuman’ di rumah agam.

🔥 Kenapa Ia Penting

1MDB adalah kes kleptokrasi terbesar dalam sejarah. DOJ Amerika Syarikat menganggarkan $4.5 bilion telah diselewengkan. Malaysia MASIH membayar hutangnya — RM42 bilion telah dibayar setakat ini, beban keseluruhan RM50 bilion. Rakyat biasa menanggung kos ini melalui perkhidmatan awam yang berkurangan, cukai lebih tinggi, dan kekangan fiskal. Orang yang bertanggungjawab hidup selesa sementara mangsanya membayar hutangnya.

💩 Kenapa Ia Karut

Anwar menghabiskan seluruh kerjaya politiknya mengkritik Najib dan 1MDB. Beliau menggunakan 1MDB sebagai hujah utama mengapa Malaysia perlukan perubahan. Beliau meminta rakyat mengundi PH khusus untuk memastikan akauntabiliti 1MDB. Kemudian, sebaik berkuasa, beliau membenarkan — atau memudahkan — pengurangan hukuman Najib dan tahanan rumah yang selesa. Pejuang antirasuah menjadi pemboleh rasuah. Sebabnya? Sokongan parlimen UMNO memerlukan patron UMNO dijaga dengan selesa.

📜 Kes Terdahulu

Pada 2018, apabila PH mula menang, Najib ditangkap dan didakwa. Proses undang-undang berjalan. Di bawah PH 2.0, proses undang-undang dijejaskan oleh pengiraan politik. Zahid dapat 47 pertuduhan digugurkan. Najib dapat hukuman dipotong separuh. Coraknya jelas: PH tukar keadilan untuk sokongan gabungan UMNO.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Kamu minta kami mengundi untuk keadilan. Kami mengundi. Kamu kemudian menukar keadilan itu untuk kerusi parlimen. Najib tinggal di rumah agam. Zahid bebas. Dan rakyat membayar hutang RM50 bilion 1MDB. Ini bukan keadilan tertangguh — ini keadilan yang dibunuh oleh orang yang berjanji menyampaikannya.

Sumber

  • The Star — Beban Fiskal 1MDB
  • CNA — Anwar Hadapi Krisis Rasuah

item.title: Najib Gets House Arrest — The World's Most Comfortable 'Punishment' for the World's Biggest Kleptocracy
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Pardons Board

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-02-02

📌 What Happened

Former PM Najib Razak, convicted of corruption in the 1MDB scandal that looted billions from the Malaysian public, had his sentence halved by a royal pardon and was subsequently transferred to house arrest at his luxurious Kuala Lumpur residence. The man convicted of stealing from the rakyat now serves ’time’ in a mansion.

🔥 Why It Matters

1MDB was the largest kleptocracy case in history. US DOJ estimated $4.5 billion was misappropriated. Malaysia is STILL paying the debt — RM42 billion paid so far, RM50 billion total burden. Ordinary Malaysians bear this cost through reduced public services, higher taxes, and fiscal constraints. The man responsible lives in comfort while his victims pay his debts.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar spent his entire political career criticising Najib and 1MDB. He used 1MDB as his primary argument for why Malaysia needed change. He asked Malaysians to vote PH specifically to ensure accountability for 1MDB. Then, once in power, he allowed — or facilitated — Najib’s sentence reduction and comfortable house arrest. The anti-corruption crusader became the corruption enabler. The reason? UMNO’s parliamentary support requires keeping UMNO’s patron comfortable.

📜 Previous Cases

In 2018, when PH first won, Najib was arrested and charged. The legal process worked. Under PH 2.0, the legal process was undermined by political calculation. Zahid got 47 charges dropped. Najib got his sentence halved. The pattern is clear: PH trades justice for UMNO’s coalition support.

🎯 PH Callout

You asked us to vote for justice. We voted. You then traded that justice for parliamentary seats. Najib lives in a mansion. Zahid walks free. And the rakyat pays RM50 billion in 1MDB debt. This isn’t justice delayed — it’s justice murdered by the very people who promised to deliver it.

Sources

  • The Star — 1MDB Fiscal Burden
  • CNA — Anwar Faces Graft Crisis

item.title: [BM] Skor CPI Mendatar Di 50 — Sama Seperti Ketika Kemuncak Skandal 1MDB 2015
item.description:
Said by: Transparency International / Rafizi Ramli

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-01-28

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Indeks Persepsi Rasuah Malaysia tersekat di 50 mata dan ke-57 global pada 2023 dan 2024 — sama dengan skor ketika kemuncak skandal 1MDB pada 2015. Rafizi sendiri memberi amaran ini ‘berlaku walaupun pendirian toleransi sifar Anwar terhadap rasuah.’

🔥 Kenapa Ia Penting

CPI adalah penanda aras antarabangsa paling banyak dirujuk untuk rasuah. Selepas dua tahun retorik ’toleransi sifar’ Anwar, skor Malaysia tidak bergerak satu mata pun. Negara ini dilihat sama rasuah seperti ketika Najib menyedut berbilion melalui 1MDB. Ini bukan kesan ketinggalan — CPI mencerminkan persepsi semasa berdasarkan tinjauan pemimpin perniagaan dan pakar.

💩 Kenapa Ia Karut

Mereka memberitahu dunia bahawa walaupun kerajaan bertukar, persekitaran rasuah tidak bertambah baik. Apabila menteri sendiri (Rafizi) secara terbuka memetik skor ini sebagai bukti kegagalan, keputusannya sebulat suara.

🎯 Seruan Kepada PH

Skor CPI kamu sama dengan Najib ketika 1MDB. Bukan serupa — sama. Selepas dua tahun ucapan ’toleransi sifar,’ masyarakat antarabangsa melihat Malaysia sama rasuah seperti di bawah kerajaan paling kleptokratik dalam sejarahnya. Itu bukan ketinggalan — itu keputusan.

Sumber

  • The Vibes — Rafizi mengenai CPI

item.title: CPI Score Flatlines at 50 — Same as During Peak 1MDB Scandal in 2015
item.description:
Said by: Transparency International / Rafizi Ramli

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-01-28

📌 What Happened

Malaysia’s Corruption Perceptions Index stuck at 50 points and 57th globally in both 2023 and 2024 — identical to the score during the peak 1MDB scandal in 2015. Rafizi himself warned this is ‘happening despite Anwar’s zero-tolerance stance on corruption.’

🔥 Why It Matters

The CPI is the most widely cited international benchmark for corruption. After two years of Anwar’s ‘zero tolerance’ rhetoric, Malaysia’s score has not moved a single point. The country is perceived as exactly as corrupt as it was when Najib was siphoning billions through 1MDB. This is not a lag effect — the CPI reflects current perceptions based on surveys of business leaders and experts.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

They are telling the world that despite the change in government, the corruption environment has not improved. When your own minister (Rafizi) publicly cites this score as evidence of failure, the verdict is unanimous.

🎯 PH Callout

Your CPI score is the same as Najib’s during 1MDB. Not similar — identical. After two years of ‘zero tolerance’ speeches, the international community perceives Malaysia as exactly as corrupt as it was under the most kleptocratic government in its history. That’s not a lag — it’s a verdict.

Sources

  • The Vibes — Rafizi on CPI

item.title: [BM] Jho Low MASIH Bebas Selepas 8 Tahun — Buronan Paling Dikehendaki Malaysia Hidup Selesa Entah Di Mana
item.description:
Said by: Interpol / MACC / DOJ

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Sehingga 2025, Low Taek Jho (Jho Low) — tokoh utama skandal 1MDB — masih bebas walaupun notis merah Interpol, waran tangkap antarabangsa, dan pertuduhan di pelbagai bidang kuasa. Tiga kerajaan Malaysia berbeza gagal mendapatkan ekstradisinya. Anwar jadikan akauntabiliti 1MDB janji utamanya, namun tiada kemajuan ketara dalam mengesan atau mengekstradisi Jho Low. Laporan menyebut beliau tinggal di China atau Macau, tetapi kerajaan Malaysia tidak mendedahkan sebarang usaha diplomatik untuk mendapatkan pulangannya.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Jho Low mendalangi kecurian berbilion daripada dana kekayaan negara Malaysia. Beliau buronan paling dikehendaki dalam sejarah Malaysia. Kebebasannya — lapan tahun selepas pertuduhan difailkan — mewakili kegagalan akauntabiliti tunggal terbesar dalam tadbir urus Malaysia. Setiap hari Jho Low kekal bebas adalah hari rakyat membayar hutang yang beliau bantu cipta sementara beliau hidup selesa di luar jangkauan undang-undang Malaysia.

💩 KENAPA IA KARUT: Anwar menghabiskan bertahun-tahun menuntut akauntabiliti 1MDB. “Di mana Jho Low?” adalah seruan perang PH. Tiga tahun dalam kerajaan, jawapannya masih sama: di tempat yang selesa, rupanya di luar jangkauan Anwar. DOJ Amerika Syarikat memulihkan lebih $1 bilion aset berkaitan 1MDB. Singapura memenjarakan jurubank. Switzerland mensanksi institusi. Malaysia — negara yang dirompak — tidak berjaya membawa perancang utama ke mahkamah.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Di bawah Najib, siasatan 1MDB ditindas secara aktif — AG dipecat, pegawai SPRM dipindahkan, dan Jho Low dilindungi. Di bawah Muhyiddin, siasatan diteruskan tetapi Jho Low kekal bebas. Di bawah Anwar, siasatan diselesaikan dengan penyelesaian dan pengurangan hukuman Najib, tetapi ekstradisi Jho Low tidak maju. Coraknya mencadangkan setiap kerajaan Malaysia mendapati mudah dari segi politik untuk bercakap tentang menangkap Jho Low sambil tidak melakukan apa-apa.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: “Di mana Jho Low?” — kamu bertanya soalan itu selama lima tahun. Kini kamu PM. Kamu mengawal jentera diplomatik. Kamu ada notis merah Interpol. Kamu ada rangka kerja kerjasama antarabangsa. Dan Jho Low masih bebas. Dedahkan langkah diplomatik yang kamu ambil. Terbitkan permintaan ekstradisi. Tekan China secara terbuka jika perlu. Atau akui bahawa menangkap Jho Low sentiasa slogan, bukan keutamaan.

Sumber: Reuters | DOJ | SCMP

item.title: [BM] Tuntutan Autonomi Sabah Dan Sarawak Masih Tidak Dipenuhi — Malaysia Timur Kekal Anak Tiri Yang Dilupakan
item.description:
Said by: Sabah & Sarawak Leaders / GPS

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Walaupun pindaan perlembagaan 2021 memulihkan status Sabah dan Sarawak sebagai rakan setara dalam Persekutuan, Malaysia Timur kekal dipinggirkan secara struktur. Sabah adalah negeri termiskin Malaysia. Syor Perjanjian Malaysia 1963 (MA63) berhabuk. Pertikaian royalti minyak dan gas kekal tidak diselesaikan. Perkongsian setara wujud di atas kertas tetapi bukan dalam peruntukan bajet, pelaburan infrastruktur, atau perwakilan politik.

🔥 KENAPA IA PENTING: Sabah dan Sarawak menyumbang sumber — minyak, gas, kayu, tanah — yang membina ekonomi moden Malaysia. Sebagai balasan, mereka menerima infrastruktur, penjagaan kesihatan, pendidikan, dan ketersambungan terburuk di negara ini. Rangka kerja MA63 menjamin layanan setara yang tidak pernah disampaikan. Apabila Sabah kekal negeri termiskin walaupun duduk di atas beberapa sumber asli terkaya Malaysia, sistem persekutuan telah gagal Malaysia Timur.

💩 KENAPA IA KARUT: Anwar berjanji menghormati MA63 dan melayan Sabah dan Sarawak sebagai rakan setara. Tiga tahun kemudian, tuntutan khusus — royalti minyak yang ditingkatkan, kawalan imigresen, autonomi pendidikan, pelaburan infrastruktur — kekal tidak diselesaikan. Pindaan perlembagaan adalah kemenangan simbolik yang tidak mengubah sebarang butiran bajet. Sabah masih mempunyai kadar kemiskinan tertinggi. Sarawak masih mempunyai infrastruktur jalan terburuk.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Di bawah setiap kerajaan, coraknya sama: janjikan autonomi Malaysia Timur, kaji isu, tangguhkan pelaksanaan, dan bergantung pada aritmetik gabungan untuk mengelak akauntabiliti. Enam puluh tahun selepas Perjanjian Malaysia, terma perjanjian kekal tidak ditunaikan.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Kamu meminda Perlembagaan untuk memulihkan status rakan setara. Kemudian kamu gagal menyusuli dengan bajet setara, infrastruktur setara, dan perkhidmatan setara. Sabah masih negeri termiskin. Sarawak masih mempunyai jalan terburuk. Tingkatkan peruntukan persekutuan kepada Malaysia Timur berkadar dengan sumbangan sumber mereka. Selesaikan pertikaian royalti minyak. Sampaikan infrastruktur setaraf Semenanjung Malaysia. Atau terus panggil mereka ‘rakan setara’ sambil melayan mereka seperti anak syarikat.

Sumber: Malaysiakini | Borneo Post | East Asia Forum

item.title: Jho Low STILL Free After 8 Years — Malaysia's Most Wanted Fugitive Living Comfortably Somewhere
item.description:
Said by: Interpol / MACC / DOJ

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: As of 2025, Low Taek Jho (Jho Low) — the central figure in the 1MDB scandal — remains free despite Interpol red notices, international arrest warrants, and charges in multiple jurisdictions. Three different Malaysian governments have failed to secure his extradition. Anwar made 1MDB accountability his signature promise, yet has made no visible progress in locating or extraditing Jho Low. Reports suggest he has been residing in China or Macau, but the Malaysian government has not disclosed any diplomatic efforts to secure his return.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Jho Low orchestrated the theft of billions from a Malaysian sovereign wealth fund. He is the most wanted fugitive in Malaysian history. His freedom — eight years after charges were filed — represents the single largest accountability failure in Malaysian governance. Every day Jho Low remains free is a day the rakyat services debt he helped create while he lives comfortably beyond the reach of Malaysian law. If Malaysia cannot bring its most-wanted kleptocrat to justice, its entire anti-corruption framework is performative.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar spent years demanding 1MDB accountability. “Where is Jho Low?” was a PH rallying cry. Three years into government, the answer remains the same: somewhere comfortable, apparently beyond Anwar’s reach. The US DOJ recovered over $1 billion in 1MDB-linked assets. Singapore jailed bankers. Switzerland sanctioned institutions. Malaysia — the country that was robbed — has not managed to bring the principal orchestrator to trial. If the political will existed, diplomatic pressure on China (where Jho Low is believed to reside) would be a priority. It clearly isn’t.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under Najib, the 1MDB investigation was actively suppressed — the AG was fired, MACC officers were transferred, and Jho Low was protected. Under Muhyiddin, investigations continued but Jho Low remained free. Under Anwar, investigations concluded with settlements and Najib’s reduced sentence, but Jho Low’s extradition has not progressed. The pattern suggests that every Malaysian government has found it politically convenient to talk about catching Jho Low while doing nothing that would actually bring him back.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: “Where is Jho Low?” — you asked that question for five years. Now you’re the PM. You control the diplomatic apparatus. You have Interpol red notices. You have international cooperation frameworks. And Jho Low is still free. Disclose what diplomatic steps you’ve taken. Publish the extradition requests. Pressure China publicly if necessary. Or admit that catching Jho Low was always a slogan, never a priority — and that the biggest kleptocrat in Malaysian history will die free because no government cared enough to bring him home.

Sources: Reuters | DOJ | SCMP

item.title: Sabah and Sarawak Autonomy Demands Still Unmet — East Malaysia Remains the Forgotten Stepchild
item.description:
Said by: Sabah & Sarawak Leaders / GPS

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Despite 2021 constitutional amendments restoring Sabah and Sarawak’s status as equal partners in the Federation, East Malaysia remains structurally marginalised. Sabah is Malaysia’s poorest state. MA63 (Malaysia Agreement 1963) recommendations are gathering dust. Oil and gas royalty disputes remain unresolved. Equal partnership exists on paper but not in budget allocation, infrastructure investment, or political representation. Sarawak’s GPS maintains leverage through coalition politics, but Sabah — where PH won only 1 of 20 seats in the 2025 state election — has almost no bargaining power.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Sabah and Sarawak contributed the resources — oil, gas, timber, land — that built Malaysia’s modern economy. In return, they received the worst infrastructure, healthcare, education, and connectivity in the country. The MA63 framework guarantees equal treatment that has never been delivered. When Sabah remains the poorest state despite sitting on some of Malaysia’s richest natural resources, the federal system has failed East Malaysia. Every year the disparities persist is another year the federation’s promise is broken.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar promised to honour MA63 and treat Sabah and Sarawak as equal partners. Three years later, the specific demands — increased oil royalties, control over immigration, education autonomy, infrastructure investment — remain unresolved. The constitutional amendments were symbolic victories that changed no budget line items. Sabah still has the highest poverty rate. Sarawak still has the worst road infrastructure. Both still have the weakest healthcare coverage. Symbolic equal partnership without fiscal equal partnership is not partnership — it is a rebrand.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, East Malaysian states were coalition partners who received patronage in exchange for parliamentary seats — but never structural reform. Under PH 1.0, a committee was formed to study MA63. Under PH 2.0, the committee’s recommendations remain largely unimplemented. Under every government, the pattern is the same: promise East Malaysian autonomy, study the issue, delay implementation, and rely on coalition arithmetic to avoid accountability. Sixty years after Malaysia Agreement, the agreement’s terms remain unfulfilled.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You amended the Constitution to restore equal partner status. Then you failed to follow through with equal budgets, equal infrastructure, and equal services. Sabah is still the poorest state. Sarawak still has the worst roads. Both still have the worst healthcare access. Constitutional amendments without fiscal commitments are empty gestures. Increase federal allocation to East Malaysia proportional to their resource contribution. Resolve oil royalty disputes. Deliver infrastructure on par with Peninsular Malaysia. Or keep calling them “equal partners” while treating them like subsidiaries.

Sources: Malaysiakini | Borneo Post | East Asia Forum

item.title: [BM] MCA Kepada PH: 'Jangan Salahkan BN Untuk Kegagalan ANDA Mereformasi'
item.description:
Said by: Neow Choo Seong, MCA Youth

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-12-30

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada akhir Disember 2024, jurucakap Pemuda MCA Neow Choo Seong mengeluarkan kenyataan memberitahu PH supaya berhenti menyalahkan BN atas kegagalan PH melaksanakan reformasi yang dijanjikan. Neow berhujah bahawa “kuasa berada sepenuhnya di tangan PH” dan BN tidak patut dijadikan kambing hitam atas keengganan PH sendiri bertindak. Kenyataan ini ketara kerana MCA adalah parti komponen BN dalam gabungan kerajaan perpaduan — bermakna rakan gabungan PH sendiri sudah jemu PH menyalahkan semua orang lain atas kegagalannya menyampaikan reformasi.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Apabila rakan gabungan anda sendiri secara terbuka memberitahu anda untuk berhenti memberi alasan, alasan itu sudah tamat tempoh. Pembelaan lalai PH untuk setiap janji yang dimungkiri ialah “kekangan gabungan” — khususnya, UMNO dan BN menyekat reformasi. Kenyataan MCA meruntuhkan hujah itu dari dalam gabungan: kuasa adalah milik PH. PM adalah PH. Menteri Kewangan adalah PH. Majoriti jawatan kabinet adalah PH. Jika reformasi tidak berlaku, ia kerana PH memilih untuk tidak melaksanakannya — bukan kerana BN menghalangnya.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: PH telah menggunakan “kekangan gabungan” sebagai alasan serba guna selama tiga tahun. Tidak boleh mansuhkan Akta Hasutan? Kekangan gabungan. Tidak boleh mereformasi Akta Mesin Cetak? Kekangan gabungan. Tidak boleh luluskan had tempoh PM? Kekangan gabungan. Kenyataan MCA mendedahkan penipuan: PH mengawal kerajaan. PH mempunyai majoriti parlimen. PH memegang portfolio utama. Reformasi bukan disekat — ia tidak dicuba. Menyalahkan BN bukan strategi politik; ia pengakuan bahawa PH tidak pernah berniat menyampaikan reformasi yang dijanjikan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Menyalahkan rakan gabungan atas kegagalan dasar adalah tradisi politik Malaysia. Di bawah BN, UMNO menyalahkan MCA dan MIC kerana “tidak memahami sensitiviti Melayu.” Di bawah PH 1.0, PH menyalahkan puak Mahathir atas reformasi yang lambat. Di bawah PH 2.0, PH menyalahkan UMNO. Coraknya sama merentas gabungan: parti dominan menggunakan rakan yang lebih kecil sebagai kambing hitam bagi kegagalan dasarnya sendiri. Apabila kambing hitam itu sendiri berkata “berhenti salahkan kami,” strategi itu telah runtuh.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Rakan gabungan anda sendiri memberitahu anda untuk berhenti memberi alasan. Kuasa ada di tangan anda. Anda memegang pejabat PM, Kementerian Kewangan, dan majoriti kerusi kabinet. Jika reformasi tidak berlaku selepas tiga tahun, ia kerana anda memilih untuk tidak melaksanakannya. Berhenti menyalahkan BN. Berhenti menyalahkan “kekangan gabungan.” Akui rekod anda. Sampaikan reformasi yang anda janjikan — atau akui bahawa janji-janji itu tidak pernah ikhlas, dan hadapi pengundi dengan pengakuan itu.

Sumber: Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: MCA to PH: 'Don't Blame BN for YOUR Failure to Reform'
item.description:
Said by: Neow Choo Seong, MCA Youth

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-12-30

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In late December 2024, MCA Youth spokesman Neow Choo Seong issued a statement telling PH to stop blaming BN for PH’s failure to implement promised reforms. Neow argued that “power is firmly in PH’s hands” and BN should not be scapegoated for PH’s own inaction. The statement was notable because MCA is a BN component party within the unity government coalition — meaning even PH’s own coalition partners were tired of PH blaming everyone else for its failure to deliver reform.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When your own coalition partner publicly tells you to stop making excuses, the excuse has expired. PH’s default defence for every broken promise has been “coalition constraints” — specifically, UMNO and BN blocking reform. MCA’s statement demolishes that argument from inside the coalition: power is PH’s. The PM is PH. The Finance Minister is PH. The majority of cabinet positions are PH. If reforms haven’t happened, it’s because PH chose not to implement them — not because BN prevented it.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH has used “coalition constraints” as an all-purpose excuse for three years. Can’t repeal the Sedition Act? Coalition constraints. Can’t reform the Printing Presses Act? Coalition constraints. Can’t deliver PM term limits? Coalition constraints. MCA’s statement exposes the fraud: PH controls the government. PH has a parliamentary majority. PH holds the key portfolios. The reforms aren’t blocked — they’re not attempted. Blaming BN is not a political strategy; it’s a confession that PH never intended to deliver the reforms it promised.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Blaming coalition partners for policy failures is a Malaysian political tradition. Under BN, UMNO blamed MCA and MIC for “not understanding Malay sensitivities.” Under PH 1.0, PH blamed Mahathir’s faction for slow reform. Under PH 2.0, PH blames UMNO. The pattern is identical across coalitions: the dominant party uses smaller partners as scapegoats for its own policy failures. When even the scapegoat says “stop blaming us,” the strategy has collapsed.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your own coalition partner is telling you to stop making excuses. Power is in your hands. You hold the PM’s office, the Finance Ministry, and the majority of cabinet seats. If reforms haven’t happened after three years, it’s because you chose not to implement them. Stop blaming BN. Stop blaming “coalition constraints.” Own your record. Deliver the reforms you promised — or admit that the promises were never sincere, and face the voters with that admission.

Sources: Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: [BM] Rosmah Dibebaskan Dari Semua 17 Tuduhan Pengubahan Wang Haram — Ahli Politik PH Yang Serang Beliau Sedekad Tiba-Tiba Bisu
item.description:
Said by: PH Politicians (Tony Pua, Rafizi Ramli, et al.) / High Court

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-12-19

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 19 Disember 2024, Hakim Mahkamah Tinggi K. Muniandy membebaskan Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor — isteri bekas PM Najib Razak yang dipenjara — daripada kesemua 17 tuduhan dalam kes pengubahan wang haram dan pengelakan cukai melibatkan RM7.09 juta. Hakim memutuskan pihak pendakwaan gagal mengemukakan elemen utama kesalahan. Rosmah didakwa pada Oktober 2018 dengan 12 tuduhan pengubahan wang haram dan 5 tuduhan gagal mengisytiharkan pendapatan. MalaysiaNow menghubungi beberapa pemimpin PH untuk komen — hampir semua enggan. Tony Pua, yang baru dua bulan lalu menyindir Najib, tidak menjawab panggilan. Rafizi Ramli, pejuang antirasuah paling lantang PH, juga membisu.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Rosmah adalah sasaran politik utama PH — simbol hidup ketamakan, rasuah, dan kebebasan hukuman BN. Lebih sedekad, pemimpin PH dari Anwar ke Pua ke Rafizi gunakan nama beliau, beg tangannya, dan dakwaan jenayahnya untuk kumpul undi dan justifikasikan kewujudan mereka sebagai gerakan reformasi. Apabila mahkamah bebaskan beliau, kesunyian itu memekakkan telinga. Tiada seorang pun pemimpin kanan PH keluarkan kenyataan menuntut akauntabiliti, mempersoalkan kegagalan pendakwaan, atau berjanji untuk merayu. Kesunyian ini memberitahu anda segalanya tentang apa sebenarnya perang antirasuah PH: teater pembangkang, bukan reformasi institusi.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

PH habiskan bertahun-tahun beritahu rakyat Malaysia bahawa Rosmah adalah wajah kleptokrasi. Mereka menang pilihan raya atas naratif itu. Kini mereka berkuasa — mengawal AGC, polis, dan strategi pendakwaan — mereka tak mampu pun keluarkan kenyataan akhbar apabila kes runtuh? Kalau pendakwaan lemah, salah siapa? PH berkuasa sejak November 2022. Mereka ada dua tahun untuk pastikan kes kukuh. Sebaliknya, saksi utama dilaporkan meninggal atau hilang, dan AGC kemudian batalkan rayuan sepenuhnya. Ini bukan sekadar berdiam — ini bersubahat dalam keruntuhan akauntabiliti.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Ini mengikut corak tepat pengampunan Najib pada Februari 2024, di mana Lembaga Pengampunan potong separuh hukuman beliau ketika Anwar berkhidmat sebagai PM. Ia mengikut pembatalan 47 tuduhan rasuah terhadap TPM Zahid Hamidi pada September 2023. Setiap kes rasuah utama era BN di bawah pengawasan Anwar sama ada dilemahkan, diselesaikan, atau runtuh — dan setiap kali, bekas anjing penyerang PH membisu. Gerakan antirasuah yang mentakrifkan politik Malaysia sedekad sedang dibongkar oleh orang yang sama yang membinanya.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Kamu bina Pakatan Harapan atas janji bahawa Malaysia layak lebih baik daripada Najib dan Rosmah. Kamu beritahu pengundi bahawa keadilan akan ditegakkan. Kini Rosmah bebas, pendakwa kamu sendiri yang gagalkan kes, dan tiada seorang pun antara kamu yang ada tulang belakang untuk jelaskan apa yang salah. Kalau inilah rupa ‘Reformasi’ dalam amalan — menerima secara senyap apabila musuh politik kamu terlepas atas pengawasan kamu — maka kamu telah menjadi tepat apa yang kamu janji untuk gantikan.

Sumber: Reuters | MalaysiaNow | Malay Mail

item.title: Rosmah Acquitted of All 17 Money Laundering Charges — PH Politicians Who Spent a Decade Attacking Her Go Silent
item.description:
Said by: PH Politicians (Tony Pua, Rafizi Ramli, et al.) / High Court

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-12-19

📌 What Happened

On December 19, 2024, High Court judge K. Muniandy acquitted and discharged Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor — wife of jailed former PM Najib Razak — of all 17 charges against her in a money laundering and tax evasion case involving RM7.09 million. The judge ruled the prosecution failed to present key elements of the offence. Rosmah had been charged in October 2018 with 12 counts of money laundering and 5 counts of failing to declare income. MalaysiaNow contacted multiple PH leaders for comment — almost all refused. Tony Pua, who had sarcastically mocked Najib just two months earlier, did not answer calls. Rafizi Ramli, PH’s most vocal anti-corruption crusader, also stayed silent.

🔥 Why It Matters

Rosmah was PH’s ultimate political piñata — the living symbol of BN excess, corruption, and impunity. For over a decade, PH leaders from Anwar to Pua to Rafizi used her name, her handbags, and her alleged crimes to rally voters and justify their existence as a reform movement. When the court acquitted her, the silence was deafening. Not a single senior PH leader issued a statement demanding accountability, questioning the prosecution’s failure, or committing to appeal. This silence tells you everything about what PH’s anti-corruption crusade was always about: opposition theatre, not institutional reform.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH spent years telling Malaysians that Rosmah was the face of kleptocracy. They won elections on that narrative. Now that they’re in government — with control over the AGC, the police, and prosecution strategy — they can’t even muster a press statement when the case collapses? If the prosecution was weak, whose fault is that? PH has been in power since November 2022. They had two years to ensure cases were airtight. Instead, key witnesses reportedly died or went missing, and the AGC later dropped the appeal entirely. This isn’t just silence — it’s complicity in the collapse of accountability.

📜 Previous Cases

This follows the exact pattern of Najib’s pardon in February 2024, where the Pardons Board halved his sentence while Anwar served as PM. It follows the dropping of 47 corruption charges against DPM Zahid Hamidi in September 2023. Every major BN-era corruption case under Anwar’s watch has either been weakened, settled, or collapsed — and every time, PH’s former attack dogs go quiet. The anti-corruption movement that defined Malaysian politics for a decade is being dismantled by the very people who built it.

🎯 PH Callout

You built Pakatan Harapan on the promise that Malaysia deserved better than Najib and Rosmah. You told voters that justice would be served. Now Rosmah walks free, your own prosecutors fumbled the case, and not one of you has the spine to explain what went wrong. If this is what ‘Reformasi’ looks like in practice — silent acceptance when your political enemies go free on your watch — then you have become exactly what you promised to replace.

Sources: Reuters | MalaysiaNow | Malay Mail

item.title: [BM] Asia Maior: Anwar Meminggirkan 'Perbincangan Terbuka Tentang Pelbagai Garis Peminggiran'
item.description:
Said by: Asia Maior Academic Journal

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-12-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada akhir 2024, jurnal akademik Asia Maior menerbitkan penilaian terhadap tadbir urus Anwar Ibrahim, menyimpulkan bahawa beliau menghadapi cabaran “tanpa mempersoal secara bermakna wacana awam yang secara sejarahnya meminggirkan perbincangan terbuka tentang pelbagai garis peminggiran sosioekonomi.” Analisis itu mendokumentasikan bagaimana retorik reformis Anwar wujud bersama realiti status quo — mengekalkan struktur naungan, rangka kerja etnik dan susunan institusi yang sama yang beliau telah berjanji selama berdekad untuk membongkar.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Apabila jurnal akademik menilai bahawa kerajaan anda mengelak “perbincangan terbuka tentang pelbagai garis peminggiran,” ia mengatakan bahawa anda telah memilih keselamatan politik berbanding reformasi substantif. Garis peminggiran di Malaysia didokumentasikan dengan baik: kuota etnik dalam pendidikan dan pekerjaan sektor awam, ketidaksamaan jantina dalam hak undang-undang, penumpuan kekayaan di kalangan elit yang berhubungan politik, dan peminggiran Malaysia Timur. PH berjanji menangani semua ini. Penilaian Asia Maior mengesyorkan tiada satu pun yang dihadapi secara substantif.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Anwar membina kerjaya politiknya atas mencabar struktur yang tepat yang dikenal pasti Asia Maior sebagai tidak disentuh. Reformasi sepatutnya bermakna mereformasi sistem naungan etnik, membuka wacana awam, dan mewujudkan Malaysia berdasarkan merit bukan identiti. Tiga tahun dalam kerajaan, wacana tidak dibuka — ia diuruskan. Sistem naungan tidak dibongkar — ia dijenamakan semula. Pendekatan PH terhadap peminggiran adalah sama dengan BN: akui dalam ucapan, elak dalam dasar, dan harap ekonomi tumbuh cukup pantas supaya tiada siapa perasan.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Setiap penilaian akademik Malaysia mengikut trajektori yang serupa. Sarjana antarabangsa mencatat jurang antara retorik dan realiti PH dengan ketepatan yang semakin meningkat. Yayasan Rosa Luxemburg mendokumentasikan “rasuah, jurang kaum dan rasa tidak puas hati ekonomi.” East Asia Forum mendokumentasikan aspirasi reformasi bercanggah dengan realiti politik. Asia Maior mendokumentasikan pengelakan wacana terbuka. Setiap penilaian datang dari sudut yang berbeza tetapi mencapai kesimpulan yang sama: PH bercakap reformasi tetapi mengamalkan tadbir urus status quo.
