🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Jurnal akademik mendokumentasikan apa yang rakyat Malaysia sudah tahu: retorik reformasi anda tidak sepadan dengan realiti tadbir urus anda. “Pelbagai garis peminggiran” kekal tidak ditangani kerana menanganinya akan merugikan anda secara politik. Buka wacana yang Anwar janjikan. Arahkan semakan bebas kuota etnik, pengambilan sektor awam dan pengagihan kekayaan. Terbitkan data. Biar rakyat Malaysia berdebat penemuan. Atau terus mengelak perbualan sementara sarjana mendokumentasikan pengelakan anda — dan saksikan mandat reformasi menguap.

Sumber: Asia Maior

item.title: [BM] Yayasan Rosa Luxemburg: 'Rasuah, Jurang Kaum, Rasa Tidak Puas Hati Ekonomi' Di Bawah Anwar
item.description:
Said by: Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-12-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada akhir 2024, Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung (RLS), sebuah yayasan politik Jerman, menerbitkan penilaian terperinci tentang Malaysia di bawah Anwar Ibrahim. Laporan itu menyimpulkan: “Rasuah peringkat tinggi berterusan, ketegangan kaum meningkat dengan ahli politik mengeksploitasi isu sensitif, respons kerajaan tertumpu kepada mengurus insiden melalui undang-undang usang berbanding menggalakkan dialog terbuka.” RLS mendokumentasikan bagaimana kerajaan Anwar mengekalkan struktur naungan dan rangka kerja etnik yang sama yang dijanjikan untuk dibongkar, sambil menggunakan undang-undang penindas untuk mengurus bantahan berbanding menangani punca.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Apabila yayasan politik antarabangsa tanpa kepentingan partisan dalam politik Malaysia menggambarkan kerajaan anda sebagai mengekalkan “rasuah peringkat tinggi” dan mengurus ketegangan kaum melalui “undang-undang usang,” ia adalah diagnosis klinikal kegagalan tadbir urus. RLS bukan corong pembangkang — ia adalah institusi akademik yang menganalisis ekonomi politik secara global. Penilaiannya sejajar dengan HRW, Amnesty International, Asia Maior dan East Asia Forum — konsensus di kalangan pemerhati antarabangsa bahawa agenda reformasi PH telah ditinggalkan.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Pembelaan PH terhadap setiap penilaian antarabangsa adalah sama: “mereka tidak faham konteks Malaysia.” Tetapi apabila lima institusi antarabangsa berbeza — dari benua yang berbeza, dengan metodologi yang berbeza — semua mencapai kesimpulan yang sama, masalahnya bukan pemahaman mereka. Ia tadbir urus anda. RLS secara khusus menyatakan bahawa respons kerajaan adalah mengurus insiden bukan menggalakkan dialog terbuka. Itu bukan reformasi — itu kawalan orang ramai. Mengurus ketegangan kaum melalui Akta Hasutan bukan menangani perkauman. Ia menyekat perbualan sementara masalah yang mendasari membusuk.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Penilaian antarabangsa terhadap tadbir urus Malaysia telah mengikut lengkungan yang konsisten di bawah PH 2.0. HRW: “Agenda Reformasi Terhenti” (2024), “Kemunduran Hak” (2025). Amnesty: “Mengkhianati ikrar reformasi” (2024). RSF: penurunan 34 tempat dalam kebebasan akhbar (2024). Asia Maior: mengelak “perbincangan terbuka tentang pelbagai garis peminggiran.” East Asia Forum: “aspirasi reformasi bercanggah dengan realiti politik.” RLS: “rasuah, jurang kaum, rasa tidak puas hati ekonomi.” Setiap penilaian tiba secara bebas di destinasi yang sama: PH bercakap reformasi tetapi mentadbir seperti BN.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Pemerhati antarabangsa telah mencapai konsensus: agenda reformasi anda telah ditinggalkan. Rasuah berterusan. Ketegangan kaum diurus melalui undang-undang usang, bukan ditangani melalui dialog terbuka. Rasa tidak puas hati ekonomi semakin meningkat. Anda boleh menolak satu laporan kritikal. Anda tidak boleh menolak lima daripada lima institusi di lima benua berbeza. Sama ada komit semula kepada agenda reformasi yang membawa anda berkuasa — atau terima bahawa masyarakat antarabangsa telah mengklasifikasikan kerajaan anda sebagai apa adanya: kesinambungan status quo dengan Bahasa Inggeris yang lebih baik.

Sumber: Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung

item.title: Asia Maior: Anwar Sidelines 'Open Discussion on Multiple Lines of Disenfranchisement'
item.description:
Said by: Asia Maior Academic Journal

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In late 2024, the academic journal Asia Maior published an assessment of Anwar Ibrahim’s governance, concluding that he faced challenges “without substantially interrogating a public discourse that has historically sidelined open discussion on multiple socio-economic lines of disenfranchisement.” The analysis documented how Anwar’s reformist rhetoric coexisted with a status quo reality — maintaining the same patronage structures, ethnic frameworks, and institutional arrangements that he had spent decades promising to dismantle.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When an academic journal assesses that your government avoids “open discussion on multiple lines of disenfranchisement,” it is saying that you have chosen political safety over substantive reform. The lines of disenfranchisement in Malaysia are well-documented: ethnic quotas in education and public sector employment, gender inequality in legal rights, wealth concentration among politically connected elites, and East Malaysian marginalisation. PH promised to address all of these. Asia Maior’s assessment suggests none have been substantively confronted.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar built his political career on challenging the very structures Asia Maior identifies as untouched. Reformasi was supposed to mean reforming the ethnic patronage system, opening public discourse, and creating a Malaysia based on merit rather than identity. Three years into government, the discourse hasn’t opened — it has been managed. The patronage system hasn’t been dismantled — it has been rebranded. PH’s approach to disenfranchisement is identical to BN’s: acknowledge it in speeches, avoid it in policy, and hope the economy grows fast enough that nobody notices.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Every Malaysian academic assessment follows a similar trajectory. International scholars note the gap between PH’s rhetoric and reality with increasing precision. The Rosa Luxemburg Foundation documented “corruption, racial divides, and economic discontent.” East Asia Forum documented reform aspirations clashing with political realities. Asia Maior documented the avoidance of open discourse. Each assessment comes from a different angle but reaches the same conclusion: PH talks reform but practices status quo governance.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Academic journals are documenting what Malaysians already know: your reform rhetoric doesn’t match your governance reality. “Multiple lines of disenfranchisement” remain unaddressed because addressing them would cost you politically. Open the discourse Anwar promised. Commission an independent review of ethnic quotas, public sector hiring, and wealth distribution. Publish the data. Let Malaysians debate the findings. Or keep avoiding the conversation while scholars document your avoidance — and watch the reform mandate evaporate.

Sources: Asia Maior

item.title: Rosa Luxemburg Foundation: 'Corruption, Racial Divides, Economic Discontent' Under Anwar
item.description:
Said by: Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In late 2024, the Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung (RLS), a German political foundation, published a detailed assessment of Malaysia under Anwar Ibrahim. The report concluded: “High-level corruption persists, rising racial tensions with politicians exploiting sensitive issues, government response focused on managing incidents through archaic laws rather than promoting open dialogue.” RLS documented how the Anwar government maintained the same patronage structures and ethnic frameworks it had promised to dismantle, while using repressive laws to manage dissent rather than addressing root causes.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When an international political foundation with no partisan stake in Malaysian politics describes your government as maintaining “high-level corruption” and managing racial tensions through “archaic laws,” it is a clinical diagnosis of governance failure. RLS is not an opposition mouthpiece — it is an academic institution that analyses political economies globally. Its assessment aligns with HRW, Amnesty International, Asia Maior, and East Asia Forum — a consensus among international observers that PH’s reform agenda has been abandoned.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH’s defence against every international assessment is the same: “they don’t understand Malaysian context.” But when five different international institutions — from different continents, with different methodologies — all reach the same conclusion, the problem is not their understanding. It is your governance. RLS specifically noted that the government’s response is to manage incidents rather than promote open dialogue. That’s not reform — it’s crowd control. Managing racial tensions through the Sedition Act is not addressing racism. It is suppressing the conversation while the underlying problems fester.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: International assessments of Malaysian governance have followed a consistent arc under PH 2.0. HRW: “Reform Agenda Stalled” (2024), “Rights Backslide” (2025). Amnesty: “Betrayed reform pledges” (2024). RSF: 34-place drop in press freedom (2024). Asia Maior: avoids “open discussion on multiple lines of disenfranchisement.” East Asia Forum: “reform aspirations clashed with political realities.” RLS: “corruption, racial divides, economic discontent.” Each assessment arrives independently at the same destination: PH talks reform but governs like BN.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: International observers have reached a consensus: your reform agenda has been abandoned. Corruption persists. Racial tensions are managed through archaic laws, not addressed through open dialogue. Economic discontent is growing. You can dismiss one critical report. You cannot dismiss five from five different institutions on five different continents. Either recommit to the reform agenda that got you elected — or accept that the international community has classified your government as what it is: a continuation of the status quo with better English.

Sources: Rosa Luxemburg Stiftung

item.title: [BM] Lawatan Luar Negara Anwar: 75% Dibiayai Syarikat Swasta — Kemudian Kerajaan Kata Sebenarnya Tidak
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister (and Fahmi Fadzil)

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-11-22

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada November 2024, Anwar memberitahu Parlimen bahawa syarikat swasta seperti Petronas dan Proton menanggung kira-kira 75% kos lawatan luar negaranya di bawah strategi perkongsian kos baru. Keesokan harinya, Menteri Komunikasi Fahmi Fadzil “menjelaskan” bahawa eksekutif korporat “membayar sendiri” manakala kerajaan menanggung delegasi Anwar. Kerajaan telah membelanjakan RM1.6 juta untuk mencarter pesawat bagi lawatan empat negara. Apabila disoal lebih lanjut, Anwar memanggilnya “zaman gila” bahawa ketelusan dihukum. Penganalisis CNA memberi amaran amalan itu “boleh mendedahkan kelemahan dalam rangka kerja pembiayaan politik Malaysia.”

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: PM berkata satu perkara di Parlimen — syarikat swasta menanggung 75% kos lawatan. Menteri Komunikasinya berkata sesuatu yang berbeza keesokan harinya — syarikat membayar untuk eksekutif mereka sendiri, bukan delegasi Anwar. Yang mana satu betul? Jika syarikat swasta turut membiayai lawatan PM, terdapat persoalan sah tentang akses dan pengaruh. Syarikat yang terbang bersama PM mendapat masa bersemuka, pembinaan hubungan dan sokongan kerajaan yang dilihat. Akses itu ada nilainya — dan apabila ia dibeli melalui pembiayaan lawatan, ia menimbulkan kebimbangan konflik kepentingan.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Memanggil pengkritik tidak bersyukur kerana mempersoalkan perbelanjaan kerajaan bukan ketelusan — ia pengelakan. Anwar menggayakan perkongsian kos sebagai tanggungjawab fiskal, tetapi keruntuhan pemesejan dalam 24 jam mengesyorkan bahawa pengaturan itu sama ada tidak berstruktur dengan baik atau tidak difahami oleh kabinetnya sendiri. Kerajaan yang tidak mampu memastikan ceritanya konsisten mengenai perbelanjaan lawatan untuk satu kitaran berita tidak menunjukkan ketelusan — ia menunjukkan disfungsi. Dan RM1.6 juta untuk mencarter pesawat untuk empat negara bukanlah mengetatkan tali pinggang, tanpa mengira siapa yang membayar.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Perbelanjaan lawatan PM menjadi pertikaian di bawah setiap pentadbiran. Penggunaan jet kerajaan oleh Najib untuk lawatan peribadi dan pembelian jet mewah adalah skandal yang menjadi tanda dagangan. PH mengkritik setiap ringgit perbelanjaan lawatan Najib. Di bawah PH 2.0, Anwar telah melakukan lebih 100 lawatan luar negara dalam tiga tahun — lebih daripada mana-mana PM Malaysia terdahulu dalam tempoh yang sama. Pengaturan perkongsian kos sepatutnya menjawab kritikan tentang kekerapan lawatan. Sebaliknya, ia mewujudkan kontroversi baru tentang pengaruh korporat.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: PM anda memberitahu Parlimen satu perkara. Menteri Komunikasi anda berkata sesuatu yang berbeza keesokan harinya. Kemudian PM anda mengadu bahawa ketelusan dihukum. Itu bukan ketelusan — itu pemesejan bercanggah diikuti rasa kasihan diri. Terbitkan perakaunan yang jelas dan terperinci bagi setiap lawatan luar negara: kos kerajaan, penyertaan korporat, dan hasil perniagaan apa yang terhasil. Jika perkongsian kos itu sah, buktikan dengan data. Jika tidak, berhenti melakukannya. Tetapi jangan beritahu Parlimen satu angka dan suruh menteri anda bercanggah dalam 24 jam — dan jangkakan rakyat Malaysia memanggil itu tadbir urus.

Sumber: CNA | Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: Anwar's Overseas Trips: 75% Funded by Private Companies — Then Government Says Actually No
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister (and Fahmi Fadzil)

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-11-22

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In November 2024, Anwar told Parliament that private companies like Petronas and Proton covered approximately 75% of his overseas travel costs under a new cost-sharing strategy. The next day, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil “clarified” that corporate executives “paid their own way” while the government covered Anwar’s delegation. The government had spent RM1.6 million to charter a plane for a four-country trip. When questioned further, Anwar called it a “crazy era” that transparency was being punished. CNA analysts warned the practice “could expose vulnerabilities in Malaysia’s political financing framework.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The PM said one thing in Parliament — private companies covered 75% of travel costs. His Communications Minister said something different the next day — companies paid for their own executives, not Anwar’s delegation. Which is it? If private companies are co-funding PM travel, there are legitimate questions about access and influence. Companies that fly with the PM get face time, relationship-building, and perceived government endorsement. That access has value — and when it’s purchased through travel funding, it raises conflict-of-interest concerns.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Calling critics ungrateful for questioning government spending is not transparency — it is deflection. Anwar styled the cost-sharing as fiscal responsibility, but the messaging collapse within 24 hours suggests the arrangement was either poorly structured or poorly understood by his own cabinet. A government that can’t keep its story straight on travel expenses for one news cycle is not demonstrating transparency — it is demonstrating dysfunction. And RM1.6 million to charter a plane for four countries is not exactly belt-tightening, regardless of who pays.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: PM travel expenses have been contentious under every administration. Najib’s use of a government jet for personal trips and the purchase of a luxury jet were signature scandals. PH criticised every ringgit of Najib’s travel spending. Under PH 2.0, Anwar has taken over 100 overseas trips in three years — more than any previous Malaysian PM in the same period. The cost-sharing arrangement was supposed to answer criticism about travel frequency. Instead, it created a new controversy about corporate influence.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Your PM told Parliament one thing. Your Communications Minister said something different the next day. Then your PM complained that transparency is being punished. That’s not transparency — that’s contradictory messaging followed by self-pity. Publish a clear, detailed accounting of every overseas trip: government cost, corporate participation, and what business outcomes resulted. If the cost-sharing is legitimate, prove it with data. If it’s not, stop doing it. But don’t tell Parliament one figure and have your minister contradict it within 24 hours — and expect Malaysians to call that governance.

Sources: CNA | Malay Mail | FMT

item.title: [BM] Kerajaan Sahkan Akta Hasutan TIDAK Akan Dimansuhkan — Janji Tanda Tangan PH Rasmi Mati
item.description:
Said by: Shamsul Anuar Nasarah, Deputy Home Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-11-14

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 14 November 2024, Timbalan Menteri Dalam Negeri Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah memberitahu Parlimen bahawa kerajaan tiada rancangan untuk meminda atau memansuhkan Akta Hasutan 1948. Beliau berkata Akta itu akan dikekalkan untuk ‘memelihara ketenteraman dan keselamatan awam.’ Kenyataan ini dibuat dalam Dewan Rakyat Anwar sendiri, oleh timbalan menteri Anwar sendiri, menamatkan sebarang lakonan bahawa PH akan menunaikan salah satu janji reformasi paling menentukan mereka. Menjelang awal 2026, sekurang-kurangnya 90 tangkapan telah dibuat di bawah Akta Hasutan sejak 2024 — di bawah pengawasan PH.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Pemansuhan Akta Hasutan bukan tuntutan pinggiran — ia adalah teras platform kebebasan sivil PH. Anwar sendiri panggil keputusan Najib mengekalkan Akta pada 2014 ‘regresif’ dan bukti ‘kepimpinan lemah’ yang ’tunduk kepada tuntutan sayap kanan.’ Setiap manifesto PH, setiap pelan hala tuju reformasi, setiap ceramah pilihan raya merujuk Akta Hasutan sebagai simbol tadbir urus autoritarian. Mengekalkannya bukan sekadar janji dimungkiri — ia penyerahan ideologi. PH kini mentadbir dengan alat tepat yang dikatakan hanya digunakan penguasa autoritarian.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Anwar pada 2014: ‘Ini pusingan U. Najib mengingkari janji awalnya. Kepimpinan lemahnya menyebabkan beliau tunduk kepada tuntutan sayap kanan partinya sendiri.’ Anwar pada 2024: kekalkan Akta Hasutan, gunakannya untuk tangkap 90 orang, dan hantar timbalan menterinya sahkan di Parlimen. Kemunafikan ini bukan halus — ia perkataan demi perkataan. Orang yang sama yang panggil Akta Hasutan pulangan kepada ‘zaman ISA’ kini menggenakannya sebagai PM. Sekurang-kurangnya Najib jujur tentang menjadi autoritarian. Anwar melakukannya sambil masih memanggil dirinya reformis.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Akta Hasutan telah menjadi janji dimungkiri kegemaran PH sejak berkuasa. Chegubard didakwa di bawahnya pada April 2024 kerana melaporkan dakwaan kasino Forest City. Wartawan, aktivis, dan pengulas telah disiasat. ARTICLE 19, Amnesty International, dan organisasi masyarakat sivil Malaysia semuanya menyeru pemansuhannya. Setiap undang-undang represif yang PH janji akan mansuhkan — Akta Hasutan, PPPA, SOSMA, Seksyen 233 AKM — kekal dalam buku statut dan dikuatkuasakan secara aktif. Peralatan legislatif penindasan tidak berubah; hanya tangan yang menggenakannya.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Dalam kata-kata kamu sendiri, mengekalkan Akta Hasutan adalah ‘regresif,’ ‘pusingan U,’ dan bukti ‘kepimpinan lemah yang tunduk kepada sayap kanan.’ Itu kata-kata kamu tentang Najib. Setiap satu kini terpakai kepada kamu. Kamu ada dua tahun dan majoriti besar parlimen. Kamu pilih untuk kekalkan undang-undang era kolonial yang kamu bina kerjaya politik untuk menentang. Jangan beritahu rakyat Malaysia kamu berbeza daripada BN. Soal Akta Hasutan, kamu ADALAH BN.

Sumber: NST | The Star | Aliran

item.title: Government Confirms Sedition Act Will NOT Be Repealed — PH's Signature Promise Officially Dead
item.description:
Said by: Shamsul Anuar Nasarah, Deputy Home Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-11-14

📌 What Happened

On November 14, 2024, Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah told Parliament that the government has no plans to amend or repeal the Sedition Act 1948. He said the Act would be maintained to ‘preserve public order and security.’ This statement was made in Anwar’s own Dewan Rakyat, by Anwar’s own deputy minister, ending any pretence that PH would deliver on one of its most defining reform promises. By early 2026, at least 90 arrests had been made under the Sedition Act since 2024 — under PH’s watch.

🔥 Why It Matters

The repeal of the Sedition Act wasn’t some fringe demand — it was the centrepiece of PH’s civil liberties platform. Anwar himself called Najib’s decision to keep the Act in 2014 ‘regressive’ and evidence of ‘weak leadership’ that had ‘caved in to demands of right-wingers.’ Every PH manifesto, every reform roadmap, every election ceramah referenced the Sedition Act as a symbol of authoritarian governance. Keeping it isn’t just a broken promise — it’s an ideological surrender. PH is now governing with the exact tools it said only authoritarians use.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar in 2014: ‘This is a flip-flop. Najib reneged on his earlier promise. His weak leadership has resulted in him caving in to demands of the right-wingers of his own party.’ Anwar in 2024: keeps the Sedition Act, uses it to arrest 90 people, and sends his deputy minister to confirm it in Parliament. The hypocrisy isn’t subtle — it’s word-for-word. The same man who called the Sedition Act a return to ‘ISA days’ now wields it as PM. At least Najib was honest about being authoritarian. Anwar does it while still calling himself a reformist.

📜 Previous Cases

The Sedition Act has been PH’s go-to broken promise since taking power. Chegubard was charged under it in April 2024 for reporting Forest City casino allegations. Reporters, activists, and commentators have been investigated. ARTICLE 19, Amnesty International, and Malaysian civil society organisations have all called for its repeal. Every repressive law PH promised to abolish — the Sedition Act, PPPA, SOSMA, Section 233 CMA — remains on the books and actively enforced. The legislative toolkit of repression hasn’t changed; only the hand that wields it.

🎯 PH Callout

In your own words, keeping the Sedition Act is ‘regressive,’ a ‘flip-flop,’ and evidence of ‘weak leadership caving to right-wingers.’ Those were your words about Najib. Every single one now applies to you. You had two years and a parliamentary supermajority. You chose to keep the colonial-era law that you built a political career opposing. Don’t tell Malaysians you’re different from BN. On the Sedition Act, you are BN.

Sources: NST | The Star | Aliran

item.title: [BM] SPRM Rampas RM6J Dan Bekukan Aset Berkaitan MB Inc Selangor Dalam Siasatan Rasuah Perlombongan Pasir
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari / MACC

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-10-18

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Oktober 2024, Suruhanjaya Pencegahan Rasuah Malaysia (SPRM) merampas RM6 juta dan membekukan aset berkaitan Menteri Besar Incorporated (MBI) Selangor berhubung siasatan rasuah perlombongan pasir. Ketua Pesuruhjaya SPRM Azam Baki mengesahkan bahawa lima firma berkaitan seorang ahli politik Selangor sedang disiasat atas dakwaan rasuah melibatkan konsesi perlombongan pasir. Siasatan mendedahkan rangkaian kontrak dan pembayaran yang menimbulkan persoalan tentang integriti kelulusan perlombongan pasir Selangor.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: MBI Selangor adalah badan pelaburan negeri — secara langsung di bawah bidang kuasa Menteri Besar. Apabila SPRM merampas berjuta-juta berkaitan kenderaan korporat MB sendiri, ia melibatkan tadbir urus pejabat tertinggi negeri. Perlombongan pasir adalah salah satu sektor yang paling terdedah kepada rasuah di Selangor, melibatkan kemusnahan alam sekitar, pencemaran sungai dan berbilion dalam hasil. Hakikat bahawa SPRM terpaksa campur tangan bermakna semakan dalaman gagal.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Amirudin telah menjadi MB sejak 2018. Rasuah perlombongan pasir di Selangor telah didokumentasikan selama bertahun-tahun. Kumpulan alam sekitar telah memberi amaran tentang perlombongan haram yang memusnahkan tebing sungai dan meracuni sumber air. Di bawah pengawasan Amirudin, masalah itu membesar sehingga SPRM terpaksa campur tangan. Itu bukan tadbir urus — itu kecuaian. MB sama ada tidak tahu (ketidakcekapan) atau tidak bertindak (bersubahat). Kedua-duanya tidak boleh diterima.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Pada September 2025, SPRM mengesahkan perkembangan baharu dalam siasatan rasuah MBI. Bekas pegawai GLC Selangor didakwa atas rasuah RM1.7 juta. Sebelum itu, aktivis Chegubard membuat laporan SPRM mendakwa ketidakteraturan RM10 bilion berkaitan projek Gerbang Maritim Selangor. Coraknya konsisten: Selangor di bawah PH adalah sarang rasuah yang kerajaan enggan mengakui.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: SPRM sedang menyiasat badan pelaburan negeri anda untuk rasuah. Pegawai GLC anda sendiri telah didakwa. Sektor perlombongan pasir anda huru-hara. Dan respons anda adalah senyap. Jika ini berlaku di bawah menteri besar BN, PKR akan mengadakan ceramah malam menuntut perletakan jawatan. Di bawah Amirudin? Senyap sepi.

Sumber: Malay Mail | Yahoo News

item.title: MACC Seizes RM6M and Freezes Assets Linked to Selangor Menteri Besar Inc in Sand Mining Corruption Probe
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari / MACC

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-10-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In October 2024, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) seized RM6 million and froze assets linked to Selangor Menteri Besar Incorporated (MBI) in connection with a sand mining corruption probe. MACC Chief Commissioner Azam Baki confirmed that five firms linked to a Selangor politician were under investigation for alleged corruption involving a sand mining concession. The probe uncovered a network of contracts and payments that raised questions about the integrity of Selangor’s sand mining approvals.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Selangor MBI is the state’s investment arm — directly under the Menteri Besar’s purview. When MACC seizes millions linked to the MB’s own corporate vehicle, it implicates the governance of the state’s highest office. Sand mining is one of Selangor’s most corruption-prone sectors, involving environmental destruction, river pollution, and billions in revenue. The fact that MACC had to intervene means internal checks failed.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Amirudin has been MB since 2018. Sand mining corruption in Selangor has been documented for years. Environmental groups have warned about illegal mining destroying riverbanks and poisoning water sources. Under Amirudin’s watch, the problem grew until MACC had to step in. That’s not governance — that’s negligence. The MB either didn’t know (incompetence) or didn’t act (complicity). Neither is acceptable.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: In September 2025, MACC confirmed new developments in the MBI corruption probe. A former Selangor GLC officer was charged over RM1.7 million in graft. Earlier, activist Chegubard lodged a MACC report alleging RM10 billion in irregularities linked to the Selangor Maritime Gateway project. The pattern is consistent: Selangor under PH is a corruption hotspot that the government refuses to acknowledge.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: MACC is investigating your state investment arm for corruption. Your own GLC officers have been charged. Your sand mining sector is a mess. And your response is silence. If this happened under a BN menteri besar, PKR would be holding nightly ceramah demanding resignation. Under Amirudin? Crickets.

Sources: Malay Mail | Yahoo News

item.title: [BM] AP DAP Dipaksa Sokong Pindaan Kewarganegaraan 'Regresif' — Majlis Peguam Kecam Kerajaan 'Sekeras-Kerasnya'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / DAP Leadership

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-10-17

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 17 Oktober 2024, Parlimen meluluskan Rang Undang-Undang Pindaan Perlembagaan 2024 dengan 206 undi — jauh melebihi majoriti dua pertiga yang diperlukan. Rang undang-undang ini meminda peruntukan kewarganegaraan, termasuk perubahan yang diberi amaran oleh pertubuhan masyarakat sivil, organisasi hak asasi, dan Majlis Peguam Malaysia akan menghapuskan perlindungan untuk kanak-kanak terbiar dan yatim. Apabila rang undang-undang ini pertama kali dibentangkan pada Mac, perpecahan timbul dalam DAP, dengan beberapa AP tidak berpuas hati kerana dipaksa menyokong apa yang Majlis Peguam panggil pindaan ‘regresif.’ AP-AP tersebut diarahkan akur dengan pendirian kerajaan. Majlis Peguam meluluskan resolusi sebulat suara di AGM mengecam kerajaan Anwar ‘sekeras-kerasnya’ atas peruntukan regresif tersebut.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Ini kerajaan yang berkempen untuk memperluaskan hak, bukan mengecilkannya. Pindaan kewarganegaraan mendedahkan ketegangan asas dalam identiti PH: apabila dipaksa memilih antara prinsip reformasi dan disiplin gabungan, DAP — yang dikatakan hati nurani PH — memilih disiplin. Majlis Peguam, Lawyers for Liberty, dan badan hak antarabangsa semuanya beri amaran yang sama: kanak-kanak tanpa kerakyatan dan yatim akan hilang perlindungan. PH paksa AP sendiri mengundi menyokong rang undang-undang yang profesion undang-undang dan masyarakat sivil secara meluas menentang.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

PH habiskan bertahun-tahun dalam pembangkang menyerang BN kerana meluluskan pindaan perlembagaan tanpa perundingan memadai atau mengambil kira hak minoriti. Kini PH luluskan pindaan perlembagaan sendiri — dengan bantahan dalaman disenyapkan dan kritikan luaran diabaikan — dan panggil ia ‘reformasi bipartisan’ kerana AP pembangkang juga mengundi menyokong. Jumlah 206 undi disambut sebagai konsensus. Ia bukan konsensus — ia arahan whip. Apabila ahli parti sendiri membantah secara peribadi dan profesion undang-undang sendiri mengecam secara terbuka, jumlah undi besar bukan bermaksud anda betul. Ia bermaksud anda dapat kepatuhan.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Ini mengikut corak PH meninggalkan komitmen hak asasi apabila tidak selesa secara politik. Parti yang janji ratifikasi ICCPR belum buat lagi. Parti yang janji mansuh Akta Hasutan sahkan pada November 2024 ia tidak akan berbuat demikian. Parti yang memperjuangkan hak kanak-kanak terbiar dalam pembangkang mengundi untuk melemahkan perlindungan tersebut dalam kerajaan. Setiap reformasi perlembagaan di bawah PH ada rasa yang sama: bahagian yang perluas kuasa kerajaan lulus mudah; bahagian yang lindungi hak individu ditangguh, dicairkan, atau ditinggalkan.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Kamu beritahu pengundi kamu akan berbeza daripada BN soal hak dan perlembagaan. Kamu beritahu penyokong DAP khususnya bahawa menyertai kerajaan perpaduan bukan bermaksud meninggalkan prinsip. Kemudian kamu paksa AP sendiri menyokong pindaan yang Majlis Peguam — bukan PAS, bukan PN, tetapi profesion undang-undang — panggil regresif. Kalau DAP tak boleh pun mengundi hati nurani sendiri atas hak kewarganegaraan kanak-kanak tanpa diarah diam dan patuh, apa sebenarnya gunanya DAP dalam kerajaan?

Sumber: MalaysiaNow | Majlis Peguam | BFM

item.title: DAP MPs Whipped Into Supporting 'Regressive' Citizenship Amendments — Malaysian Bar Condemns Government 'in the Strongest Possible Terms'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / DAP Leadership

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-10-17

📌 What Happened

On October 17, 2024, Parliament passed the Constitution (Amendment) Bill 2024 with 206 votes — well above the two-thirds majority required. The bill amended citizenship provisions, including changes that civil society groups, rights organisations, and the Malaysian Bar warned would strip protections from abandoned children and foundlings. When the bill was first tabled in March, a split emerged within DAP, with several MPs unhappy about supporting what the Malaysian Bar called ‘regressive’ amendments. Those MPs were warned to toe the government line. The Malaysian Bar passed a unanimous resolution at its AGM condemning the Anwar government ‘in the strongest possible terms’ for the regressive provisions.

🔥 Why It Matters

This is a government that campaigned on expanding rights, not shrinking them. The citizenship amendment exposed a fundamental tension in PH’s identity: when forced to choose between their reform principles and coalition discipline, DAP — PH’s supposed conscience — chose discipline. The Malaysian Bar, Lawyers for Liberty, and international rights bodies all flagged the same concern: that stateless children and foundlings would lose protections. PH whipped its own MPs into voting for a bill that the legal profession and civil society overwhelmingly opposed. This is what reform looks like when it’s subordinated to political convenience.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH spent years in opposition attacking BN for ramming through constitutional amendments without adequate consultation or regard for minority rights. Now PH rams through its own constitutional changes — with internal dissent silenced and external criticism ignored — and calls it ‘bipartisan reform’ because opposition MPs also voted for it. The 206-vote count is being celebrated as consensus. It’s not consensus — it’s whipping. When your own party members are privately objecting and your own legal profession is publicly condemning you, a big vote count doesn’t mean you got it right. It means you got compliance.

📜 Previous Cases

This follows PH’s pattern of abandoning rights commitments when politically inconvenient. The party that promised to ratify ICCPR hasn’t done so. The party that promised to repeal the Sedition Act confirmed in November 2024 it won’t. The party that championed foundling rights in opposition voted to weaken those protections in government. Every constitutional reform under PH has the same flavour: the parts that expand government power pass easily; the parts that protect individual rights get delayed, watered down, or abandoned.

🎯 PH Callout

You told voters you would be different from BN on rights and the constitution. You told DAP supporters specifically that joining a unity government wouldn’t mean abandoning principles. Then you whipped your own MPs into supporting amendments that the Malaysian Bar — not PAS, not PN, but the legal profession — called regressive. If DAP can’t even vote its conscience on children’s citizenship rights without being told to shut up and comply, what exactly is the point of having DAP in government?

Sources: MalaysiaNow | Malaysian Bar | BFM

item.title: [BM] Saga Kasino Forest City — Anwar Ugut PENJARA Bagi Mereka Yang Melaporkannya
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-05-01

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Mei 2024, Anwar Ibrahim menyifatkan dakwaan mengenai kasino di Forest City di Johor sebagai “berniat jahat” dan mencadangkan mereka yang menyebarkan khabar angin boleh dipenjarakan. SCMP melaporkan bahawa Anwar dan hartawan yang dikaitkan dengan Zon Kewangan Khas Forest City “didesak supaya menarik balik ugutan” selepas tindak balas agresif itu mendapat kecaman. Chegubard — aktivis yang melaporkan rancangan kasino tersebut — sebenarnya telah didakwa di bawah Akta Hasutan kerana hantaran media sosialnya. Tindak balas kerajaan terhadap kewartawanan mengenai Forest City bukanlah ketelusan atau penafian dengan bukti — ia adalah ugutan penjara.

🔥 MENGAPA IA PENTING: Apabila seorang Perdana Menteri mengugut penjara kepada mereka yang melaporkan projek pembangunan, ia menghantar isyarat yang memusnahkan tentang kebebasan akhbar dan akauntabiliti awam. Forest City adalah projek sensitif dari segi politik — pembangunan bersandarkan China di Johor yang kerajaan promosikan sebagai Zon Kewangan Khas dengan insentif cukai 0%. Soalan tentang aktiviti yang dibenarkan di sana adalah kewartawanan kepentingan awam yang sah, bukan hasutan. Ugutan penjara untuk pelaporan bukan tindak balas terhadap maklumat salah — ia adalah penindasan terhadap penelitian.

💩 MENGAPA IA KARUT: Jika dakwaan kasino itu palsu, kerajaan mempunyai setiap alat untuk menyangkalnya: keluarkan penafian rasmi, terbitkan peraturan SFZ, tunjukkan bahawa perjudian dikecualikan. Sebaliknya, Anwar memilih ugutan. Itu bukan tindak balas kerajaan yang tiada apa untuk disembunyikan — ia adalah tindak balas kerajaan yang mahu soalan berhenti. Laporan SCMP — bahawa Anwar dan hartawan “didesak menarik balik ugutan” — menunjukkan bahawa sekutu pun menyedari tindak balas itu tidak setimpal. Apabila reaksi anda terhadap kewartawanan lebih agresif daripada reaksi anda terhadap rasuah, keutamaan anda jelas terbukti.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: PM Malaysia mempunyai sejarah panjang mengugut pengkritik berbanding menangani dakwaan. Mahathir mengugut wartawan yang mempersoalkan dasarnya. Kerajaan Najib menggunakan Akta Rahsia Rasmi, Akta Hasutan, dan SPRM terhadap pengkritik 1MDB. Muhyiddin menggunakan ordinan darurat untuk menindas perbezaan pendapat semasa COVID. PH berjanji memutuskan corak ini. Sebaliknya, tindak balas Anwar terhadap laporan Forest City tidak dapat dibezakan daripada pendahulunya: ugut penjara, dakwa pengkritik, dan harap cerita itu mati.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Tindak balas yang betul terhadap dakwaan palsu adalah bukti, bukan ugutan. Jika tiada kasino dirancang di Forest City, terbitkan peraturan SFZ dan buktikan. Jika ada, akui dan pertahankan dasar tersebut. Tetapi mengugut penjara kepada wartawan yang bertanya soalan tentang zon khas yang dibiayai awam adalah autoritarian — dan anda tahu itu, kerana anda menghabiskan 25 tahun menerima taktik yang sama. Berhenti mengugut wartawan. Mula menjawab soalan.

Sumber: SCMP | Malaysiakini

item.title: Forest City Casino Saga — Anwar Threatens JAIL for Those Who Report on It
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-05-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In May 2024, Anwar Ibrahim called allegations about a casino at Forest City in Johor “malicious” and suggested those spreading the rumours could face jail. SCMP reported that Anwar and tycoons connected to the Forest City Special Financial Zone were “urged to walk back threats” after the aggressive response drew backlash. Chegubard — the activist who reported on the casino plan — was actually charged with sedition for his social media post. The government’s response to journalism about Forest City was not transparency or denial with evidence — it was threats of imprisonment.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When a Prime Minister threatens jail for people who report on a development project, it sends a devastating signal about press freedom and public accountability. Forest City is a politically sensitive project — a Chinese-backed development in Johor that the government is promoting as a Special Financial Zone with 0% tax incentives. Questions about what activities will be permitted there are legitimate public interest journalism, not sedition. The threat of jail for reporting is not a response to misinformation — it is a suppression of scrutiny.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: If the casino allegations were false, the government had every tool available to debunk them: issue a formal denial, publish the SFZ regulations, show that gaming is excluded. Instead, Anwar chose threats. That’s not the response of a government with nothing to hide — it’s the response of a government that wants questions to stop. SCMP’s reporting — that Anwar and tycoons were “urged to walk back threats” — suggests even allies recognised the response was disproportionate. When your reaction to journalism is more aggressive than your reaction to corruption, your priorities are transparent.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysian PMs have a long history of threatening critics instead of addressing allegations. Mahathir threatened journalists who questioned his policies. Najib’s government used the Official Secrets Act, Sedition Act, and MACC against critics of 1MDB. Muhyiddin used emergency ordinances to suppress dissent during COVID. PH promised to break this pattern. Instead, Anwar’s response to Forest City reporting was indistinguishable from his predecessors: threaten jail, charge the critic, and hope the story dies.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: The correct response to false allegations is evidence, not threats. If there’s no casino planned at Forest City, publish the SFZ regulations and prove it. If there is, own it and defend the policy. But threatening jail for reporters asking questions about a publicly funded special zone is authoritarian — and you know it, because you spent 25 years on the receiving end of exactly this tactic. Stop threatening journalists. Start answering questions.

Sources: SCMP | Malaysiakini

item.title: [BM] Polis Tindas Perhimpunan Bersih Di Bawah Kerajaan PH — Golongan Reformis Menjadi Penindas
item.description:
Said by: PH Government; Police (PDRM)

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-02-26

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 26-27 Februari 2024, Bersih mengadakan perhimpunan menggesa kerajaan PH melaksanakan reformasi politik yang dijanjikan semasa kempen pilihan raya 2022 — termasuk kebebasan institusi, langkah antirasuah, dan pengawasan parlimen. Polis merespons dengan tindakan keras yang mendapat kecaman segera daripada masyarakat sivil. Lawyers for Liberty mengeluarkan kenyataan mengecam respons kerajaan, mengkritik kekurangan pemahaman polis terhadap Akta Perhimpunan Aman dan Perlembagaan Persekutuan. Bersih juga menyerahkan memorandum kepada kerajaan menggesa pelaksanaan reformasi yang lama tertangguh.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Perhimpunan Bersih adalah oksigen kebangkitan PH kepada kuasa. Perhimpunan Bersih 2.0, 3.0, 4.0, dan 5.0 antara 2011 dan 2016 membina gerakan massa yang akhirnya menjatuhkan BN. Pemimpin PH bukan sahaja menyokong perhimpunan ini — mereka berpawai di dalamnya. Anwar sendiri adalah penerima manfaat advokasi Bersih. Kini, kerajaan yang sama yang menunggang gelombang Bersih ke kuasa menggunakan polis untuk menindas tuntutan Bersih bagi reformasi yang PH janjikan. Kebebasan berhimpun hanya diterima apabila ia melayani kepentingan PH.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Pemimpin PH menghabiskan sedekad menuntut hak berhimpun secara aman. Nik Nazmi — yang secara literal didakwa di bawah Akta Perhimpunan Aman kerana menganjurkan protes pada 2013 — adalah menteri PH apabila polis menindas Bersih. Kecaman LFL secara khusus memetik keputusan Mahkamah Rayuan 2014 daripada kes Nik Nazmi sendiri yang membatalkan peruntukan sekatan dalam Akta Perhimpunan Aman. Kemenangan undang-undang PH sendiri terhadap penindasan protes kini diabaikan oleh kerajaan PH sendiri. Ini bukan mengenai ketenteraman awam — ini mengenai kerajaan yang menjanjikan reformasi dan kini menggunakan alat penindasan yang sama seperti pendahulunya untuk mengelak dipertanggungjawabkan atas janji yang dimungkiri.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Di bawah BN, tindakan keras polis terhadap perhimpunan Bersih menjadi skandal muka depan. Gas pemedih mata, meriam air, dan tangkapan beramai-ramai di Bersih 2.0 dan 3.0 dipetik oleh pertubuhan hak asasi manusia antarabangsa sebagai bukti kemunduran demokrasi Malaysia. PH menggunakan tindakan keras ini untuk membina naratif mereka sebagai pembela demokrasi. Laporan Dunia 2024 Human Rights Watch menyatakan bahawa kerajaan baharu Malaysia “sebahagian besarnya gagal menunaikan janjinya untuk menangani penindasan” dalam tahun pertamanya — secara khusus mencatatkan jurang antara janji reformasi PH dan rekod pentadbiran sebenarnya.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda berpawai dengan Bersih. Anda ditembak gas pemedih mata dengan Bersih. Anda menggunakan perhimpunan Bersih untuk membina gerakan yang membawa anda ke kuasa. Kini anda menghantar polis mengejar Bersih kerana mereka menuntut anda menunaikan janji. Golongan reformis telah menjadi penindas. Jika anda tidak tahan kritikan aman daripada orang yang meletakkan anda berkuasa, anda tidak layak memegang kuasa itu.

Sumber: FMT | The Star | HRW

item.title: Police Crack Down on Bersih Rally Under PH Government — The Reformists Become the Repressors
item.description:
Said by: PH Government; Police (PDRM)

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-02-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 26-27 February 2024, Bersih held a rally calling on the PH government to implement the political reforms it had promised during the 2022 election campaign — including institutional independence, anti-corruption measures, and parliamentary oversight. Police responded with a crackdown that drew immediate condemnation from civil society. Lawyers for Liberty issued a statement condemning the government’s response, criticising the police’s lack of understanding of the Peaceful Assembly Act and the Federal Constitution. Bersih also handed a memorandum to the government urging implementation of long-delayed reforms.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Bersih’s rallies were the oxygen of PH’s rise to power. The Bersih 2.0, 3.0, 4.0, and 5.0 rallies between 2011 and 2016 built the mass movement that eventually toppled BN. PH leaders didn’t just support these rallies — they marched in them. Anwar himself was a beneficiary of Bersih’s advocacy. Now, the same government that rode Bersih’s wave to power is using police to suppress Bersih’s demands for the reforms PH promised. Freedom of assembly is only acceptable when it serves PH’s interests.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH leaders spent a decade demanding the right to peaceful assembly. Nik Nazmi — who was literally charged under the PAA for organising a protest in 2013 — was a PH minister when police cracked down on Bersih. LFL’s condemnation specifically cited a 2014 Court of Appeal ruling from Nik Nazmi’s own case that struck down restrictive provisions of the PAA. PH’s own legal victory against protest suppression is being ignored by PH’s own government. This isn’t about public order — it’s about a government that promised reform and is now using the same repressive tools as its predecessors to avoid being held accountable for broken promises.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, police crackdowns on Bersih rallies were front-page scandals. Tear gas, water cannons, and mass arrests at Bersih 2.0 and 3.0 were cited by international human rights organisations as evidence of Malaysia’s democratic backsliding. PH used these crackdowns to build their narrative as defenders of democracy. Human Rights Watch’s 2024 World Report stated that Malaysia’s new government “largely failed to uphold its pledges to address repression” in its first year — specifically noting the gap between PH’s reform promises and its actual governance record.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You marched with Bersih. You were tear-gassed with Bersih. You used Bersih’s rallies to build the movement that brought you to power. Now you’re sending police after Bersih because they’re demanding you keep the promises you made. The reformists have become the repressors. If you can’t handle peaceful criticism from the very people who put you in power, you don’t deserve that power.

Sources: FMT | The Star | HRW

item.title: [BM] Hukuman Penjara Najib Dipotong Separuh — Lembaga Pengampunan Di Bawah Pengawasan Anwar
item.description:
Said by: Pardons Board / King of Malaysia

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-02-02

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada 2 Februari 2024, Lembaga Pengampunan — yang beroperasi di bawah kuasa perlembagaan Yang di-Pertuan Agong, dengan PM sebagai ahli — memotong separuh hukuman penjara 12 tahun Najib Razak kepada 6 tahun atas peranannya dalam skandal 1MDB. Denda RM210 juta Najib juga dikurangkan kepada RM50 juta. Keputusan itu datang di tengah kontroversi “adendum diraja,” dengan pihak Najib mendakwa dokumen tambahan wujud yang memberikan tahanan rumah. Reuters melaporkan pengampunan itu datang “di tengah tuduhan kemunduran reformasi.” Pengkritik memanggilnya urusan bilik belakang untuk mengekalkan UMNO dalam gabungan — UMNO telah menjadikan pembebasan Najib sebagai prasyarat untuk sokongan berterusan kepada kerajaan Anwar.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Najib disabitkan dengan skandal kewangan terbesar dalam sejarah Malaysia — berbilion dicuri dari dana kekayaan negara, dicuci melalui bank global, dibelanjakan untuk kapal layar, barang kemas, dan filem Hollywood. Sabitan itu adalah pencapaian utama PH di bawah PH 1.0. Memotong separuh hukuman di bawah PH 2.0 menghantar mesej yang dahsyat: di Malaysia, penalti rasuah boleh dirunding jika anda mempunyai cukup leveraj politik. Pengampunan itu bukan berdasarkan bukti baharu atau merit undang-undang — ia berdasarkan aritmetik gabungan. Sokongan UMNO kepada PH datang dengan harga tersirat, dan rakyat menyaksikan harga itu dibayar.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Anwar menghabiskan seluruh kerjaya politiknya menuntut akauntabiliti bagi Najib. “Malu Apa Bossku” adalah slogan yang diejek PH tanpa henti. Kini hukuman Najib dipotong separuh, dendanya dikurangkan 76%, dan penyokongnya mendesak untuk tahanan rumah — semua di bawah pengawasan Anwar. Lembaga Pengampunan termasuk PM. Keputusan itu tidak boleh dipisahkan daripada konteks politik: UMNO mahu hukuman Najib dikurangkan, UMNO penting kepada majoriti PH, dan hukuman itu dikurangkan. Memanggil ini apa-apa selain urusan politik memerlukan tahap kenaifan yang tiada rakyat Malaysia miliki.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Malaysia mempunyai sejarah pengampunan yang mudah secara politik. Anwar sendiri diampunkan oleh Yang di-Pertuan Agong selepas kemenangan PH 1.0 pada 2018 — pengampunan yang disambut sebagai keadilan. Perbezaannya: sabitan Anwar dilihat secara meluas sebagai bermotif politik. Sabitan Najib ditegakkan oleh setiap peringkat badan kehakiman berdasarkan bukti yang sangat kuat. Mengampunkan banduan politik dan mengurangkan hukuman kleptokrat yang disabitkan adalah dua tindakan yang secara asas berbeza — tetapi kesediaan PH memudahkan kedua-duanya mencadangkan bahawa proses pengampunan adalah alat politik, bukan mekanisme keadilan.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Anda membina gerakan anda atas menuntut akauntabiliti Najib. Kemudian anda potong separuh hukumannya untuk menyenangkan UMNO. Rakyat berpawai dalam perhimpunan Bersih, mengundi dalam PRU14 dan PRU15, dan menunggu bertahun untuk keadilan — dan anda menukarnya dengan kestabilan gabungan. Jika jenayah Najib bernilai 12 tahun apabila PH dalam pembangkang, ia tidak menjadi bernilai 6 tahun hanya kerana UMNO dalam gabungan anda. Setiap ringgit yang dicuri Najib masih hilang. Setiap tahun yang dibuang daripada hukumannya adalah diskaun atas akauntabiliti. Rakyat akan ingat.

Sumber: Reuters | Straits Times | Al Jazeera

item.title: Najib's Jail Sentence Halved — Pardons Board Under Anwar's Watch
item.description:
Said by: Pardons Board / King of Malaysia

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-02-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 2 February 2024, the Pardons Board — which operates under the constitutional authority of the King, with the PM as a member — halved Najib Razak’s 12-year jail sentence to 6 years for his role in the 1MDB scandal. Najib’s RM210 million fine was also reduced to RM50 million. The decision came amid a “royal addendum” controversy, with Najib’s camp claiming an additional document existed granting house arrest. Reuters reported the pardon came “amid accusations of backsliding on reforms.” Critics called it a backroom deal to keep UMNO in the coalition — UMNO had made Najib’s release a precondition for continued support of Anwar’s government.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Najib was convicted of the largest financial scandal in Malaysian history — billions stolen from a sovereign wealth fund, laundered through global banks, spent on yachts, jewellery, and Hollywood films. The conviction was PH’s signature achievement under PH 1.0. Halving the sentence under PH 2.0 sent a devastating message: in Malaysia, corruption penalties are negotiable if you have enough political leverage. The pardon was not based on new evidence or legal merit — it was based on coalition arithmetic. UMNO’s support for PH came with an implicit price, and the rakyat is watching that price get paid.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar spent his entire political career demanding accountability for Najib. “Malu Apa Bossku” was the slogan PH mocked relentlessly. Now Najib’s sentence is halved, his fine reduced by 76%, and his supporters are pushing for house arrest — all under Anwar’s watch. The Pardons Board includes the PM. The decision cannot be separated from the political context: UMNO wanted Najib’s sentence reduced, UMNO is essential to PH’s majority, and the sentence was reduced. Calling this anything other than a political deal requires a level of naivety that no Malaysian possesses.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a history of politically convenient pardons. Anwar himself was pardoned by the King after PH 1.0’s victory in 2018 — a pardon that was celebrated as justice. The difference: Anwar’s conviction was widely seen as politically motivated. Najib’s conviction was upheld by every level of the judiciary based on overwhelming evidence. Pardoning a political prisoner and reducing the sentence of a convicted kleptocrat are fundamentally different acts — but PH’s willingness to facilitate both suggests that the pardons process is a political tool, not a justice mechanism.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You built your movement on holding Najib accountable. Then you halved his sentence to keep UMNO happy. The rakyat marched in Bersih rallies, voted in GE14 and GE15, and waited years for justice — and you traded it for coalition stability. If Najib’s crime was worth 12 years when PH was in opposition, it doesn’t become worth 6 years just because UMNO is in your coalition. Every ringgit Najib stole is still missing. Every year removed from his sentence is a discount on accountability. The rakyat will remember.

Sources: Reuters | Straits Times | Al Jazeera

item.title: [BM] Amnesty International 2024: Anwar 'Mengkhianati Janji Reformasi Yang Dibuat Kepada Pengundi Malaysia'
item.description:
Said by: Amnesty International / Shayna Bauchner

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-01-11

📌 APA YANG BERLAKU: Pada Januari 2024, laporan tahunan Amnesty International menyatakan bahawa Anwar Ibrahim telah “mengkhianati janji reformasi yang dibuatnya kepada pengundi Malaysia supaya beliau boleh mengekalkan gabungannya.” Laporan itu, ditulis oleh penyelidik Shayna Bauchner, mengenal pasti tujuh bidang di mana Malaysia gagal memenuhi tanggungjawab hak asasi manusianya, termasuk kebebasan bersuara, kebebasan berhimpun, kebebasan media, hak pendatang, hak LGBTQ+, dan hukuman mati. Amnesty menyatakan bahawa kerajaan bukan sahaja gagal memajukan reformasi tetapi secara aktif menggunakan undang-undang menindas yang dijanjikan untuk dimansuhkan.

🔥 MENGAPA INI PENTING: Apabila Amnesty International — pertubuhan hak asasi manusia terbesar dunia — menggunakan perkataan “mengkhianati,” ia bukan membuat kritikan biasa. Ia mendokumentasikan kerajaan yang secara eksplisit menjanjikan reformasi tertentu, menerima undi atas dasar itu, dan kemudian berpatah balik. Pilihan perkataan itu penting: “terbantut” membawa makna kelewatan; “mengkhianati” membawa makna niat. Amnesty mengatakan bahawa peninggalan reformasi oleh PH bukan disebabkan kekangan atau kerumitan — ia adalah pilihan sengaja untuk mengutamakan kestabilan gabungan berbanding komitmen hak asasi manusia.

💩 MENGAPA INI KARUT: Pembelaan PH — “kekangan gabungan memerlukan kompromi” — adalah tepat-tepat hujah yang digunakan BN selama berdekad. UMNO berkata ia tidak boleh mereformasi kerana rakan gabungan. PAS berkata ia tidak boleh bersederhana kerana asasnya. Kini PH berkata ia tidak boleh menyampaikan hak kerana UMNO ada dalam gabungan. Alasan itu serupa; hanya nama parti yang bertukar. Jika anda tahu UMNO akan menyekat reformasi apabila anda membentuk gabungan, maka janji manifesto anda adalah palsu dengan sengaja. Jika anda tidak menjangkakannya, anda tidak cekap. Walau bagaimanapun, penggunaan “mengkhianati” oleh Amnesty adalah tepat.

📜 KES TERDAHULU: Amnesty telah mendokumentasikan kegagalan hak asasi manusia Malaysia merentas setiap pentadbiran. Di bawah Mahathir 1.0, tumpuan adalah penahanan ISA dan tindakan keras terhadap perbezaan pendapat. Di bawah Najib, ia adalah penindasan berkaitan 1MDB dan Akta Hasutan. Di bawah PH 1.0, Amnesty mencatatkan kemajuan separa sebelum Langkah Sheraton. Di bawah PH 2.0, keputusan adalah yang paling keras — kerana jurang antara janji dan penyampaian tidak pernah lebih lebar. PH membuat komitmen hak asasi manusia paling bercita-cita tinggi dalam sejarah Malaysia, dan memungkirinya secara lebih menyeluruh daripada mana-mana pendahulu.

🎯 SERUAN KEPADA PH: Amnesty International mengatakan anda “mengkhianati” pengundi anda. Bukan “mengecewakan.” Bukan “tidak memenuhi.” Mengkhianati. Perkataan itu akan mengikut kerajaan ini ke setiap forum antarabangsa, setiap pertemuan diplomatik, dan setiap pilihan raya masa depan. Anda masih boleh mengubah trajektori: mansuhkan Akta Hasutan, lindungi hak berhimpun, jamin kebebasan akhbar, dan berhenti menggunakan undang-undang menindas terhadap pengkritik. Atau biarkan “mengkhianati” menjadi ringkasan satu perkataan era Anwar — kerana sekarang, Amnesty dan HRW bersetuju bahawa ia memang begitu.

Sumber: Amnesty International | Malaysiakini

item.title: Amnesty International 2024: Anwar 'Betrayed the Reform Pledges He Made to Malaysian Voters'
item.description:
Said by: Amnesty International / Shayna Bauchner

Category: Politics

Date: 2024-01-11

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2024, Amnesty International’s annual report stated that Anwar Ibrahim had “betrayed the reform pledges he made to Malaysian voters so he could hold his coalition together.” The report, authored by researcher Shayna Bauchner, identified seven areas where Malaysia fell short of its human rights obligations, including freedom of expression, freedom of assembly, media freedom, migrant rights, LGBTQ+ rights, and the death penalty. Amnesty noted that the government had not only failed to advance reforms but had actively used repressive laws it had promised to repeal.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When Amnesty International — the world’s largest human rights organisation — uses the word “betrayed,” it is not making a casual criticism. It is documenting a government that explicitly promised specific reforms, received votes on that basis, and then reversed course. The word choice matters: “stalled” implies delay; “betrayed” implies intent. Amnesty is saying that PH’s abandonment of reform was not due to constraints or complexity — it was a deliberate choice to prioritise coalition stability over human rights commitments.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH’s defence — “coalition constraints require compromise” — is exactly the argument BN used for decades. UMNO said it couldn’t reform because of coalition partners. PAS said it couldn’t moderate because of its base. Now PH says it can’t deliver rights because UMNO is in the coalition. The excuse is identical; only the party name has changed. If you knew UMNO would block reforms when you formed the coalition, then your manifesto promises were knowingly false. If you didn’t anticipate it, you’re incompetent. Either way, Amnesty’s use of “betrayed” is accurate.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Amnesty has documented Malaysian human rights failures across every administration. Under Mahathir 1.0, the focus was ISA detentions and crackdowns on dissent. Under Najib, it was 1MDB-related repression and the Sedition Act. Under PH 1.0, Amnesty noted partial progress before the Sheraton Move. Under PH 2.0, the verdict is the harshest yet — because the gap between promise and delivery has never been wider. PH made the most ambitious human rights commitments in Malaysian history, and broke them more comprehensively than any predecessor.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Amnesty International says you “betrayed” your voters. Not “disappointed.” Not “fell short.” Betrayed. That word will follow this government into every international forum, every diplomatic meeting, and every future election. You can still change the trajectory: repeal the Sedition Act, protect assembly rights, guarantee press freedom, and stop using repressive laws against critics. Or let “betrayed” become the one-word summary of the Anwar era — because right now, Amnesty and HRW agree that it is.

Sources: Amnesty International | Malaysiakini

item.title: [BM] SPRM Rampas Ilham Tower Daim Dalam Siasatan Pengubahan Wang Haram — Pendakwaan Terpilih Atau Akauntabiliti Tulen?
item.description:
Said by: Azam Baki, MACC Chief Commissioner / Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2023-12-21

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 21 Disember 2023, SPRM merampas Ilham Tower 60 tingkat di Kuala Lumpur — bernilai anggaran RM1 hingga RM1.5 bilion — di bawah Seksyen 51 Akta Pencegahan Pengubahan Wang Haram (AMLA). Rampasan ini sebahagian daripada siasatan yang semakin meluas terhadap bekas menteri kewangan Tun Daim Zainuddin berhubung urus niaga korporat 1997 bernilai RM2.3 bilion melibatkan Renong Bhd dan United Engineers Malaysia (UEM), kedua-duanya dikaitkan dengan UMNO. Ketua Pesuruhjaya SPRM Tan Sri Azam Baki mengesahkan rampasan itu. Daim menafikan sebarang salah laku dan mengekalkan pendirian bahawa siasatan bermotif politik. SPRM juga mendakwa Daim dan isterinya Naimah Abdul Khalid kerana gagal mengisytiharkan harta, dan mengeluarkan notis untuk mengesan empat orang anak Daim.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Di atas kertas, menyiasat kekayaan tersembunyi bekas menteri kewangan adalah tepat apa yang kerajaan antirasuah patut buat. Tetapi konteksnya penting: Daim adalah sekutu utama Mahathir, yang kini menentang Anwar secara politik. Sekutu Mahathir — Daim, pelbagai tokoh pembangkang — menghadapi penelitian SPRM di bawah Anwar, manakala rakan gabungan Anwar sendiri Zahid mempunyai 47 pertuduhan digugurkan beberapa bulan sebelum itu. Coraknya tidak boleh disangkal: SPRM keras terhadap lawan politik dan lembut terhadap sekutu politik.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Ketua SPRM yang sama, Azam Baki, yang agresif mengejar Daim adalah orang yang sama yang menghadapi skandal pegangan saham sendiri dan dilindungi kerajaan Anwar. Pejabat AG yang sama yang meluluskan siasatan Daim juga menandatangani penggugaran 47 pertuduhan Zahid. Kalau SPRM benar-benar bebas, rakyat mungkin meraikan siasatan Daim sebagai kemenangan. Tetapi apabila ketua agensi antirasuah sendiri dibelenggu kontroversi, dan kerajaan memilih rasuah mana untuk didakwa berdasarkan penjajaran politik, seluruh latihan ini kelihatan seperti kuasa negara berpakaian akauntabiliti.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Pendakwaan terpilih mempunyai sejarah panjang di Malaysia. Di bawah Najib, SPRM dituduh menyasar tokoh pembangkang sambil mengabaikan rasuah berkaitan BN. Di bawah Mahathir 2.0, pemimpin UMNO didakwa mengikut kepentingan politik. Di bawah Anwar, senarai sasaran berubah lagi: sekutu Mahathir diserbu, sekutu Anwar dapat DNAA. Siasatan ICIJ Pandora Papers mengaitkan Daim dengan amanah luar pesisir $52 juta — jadi mungkin ada substansi dalam siasatan. Tetapi aplikasi keadilan yang terpilih memastikan siasatan yang sah pun dicemari syak politik.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Korang nak kredit kejar Daim? Boleh. Tapi jelaskan kenapa korang gugurkan 47 pertuduhan Zahid tiga bulan sebelum tu. Jelaskan kenapa Azam Baki — orang yang ada skandal aset sendiri — yang ketuai siasatan ini. Korang tak boleh dakwa lawan dan lindungi kawan dan panggil ia Reformasi. Rakyat nampak coraknya. Sama ada semua orang tertakluk kepada undang-undang, atau tiada siapa. Sekarang ni, nampak macam undang-undang hanya terpakai kepada orang yang menyusahkan politik Anwar Ibrahim.

Sumber: Al Jazeera | The Edge | The Star | ICIJ

item.title: MACC Seizes Daim's Ilham Tower in Anti-Money Laundering Probe — Selective Prosecution or Genuine Accountability?
item.description:
Said by: Azam Baki, MACC Chief Commissioner / Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2023-12-21

📌 What Happened

On December 21, 2023, MACC seized the 60-storey Ilham Tower in Kuala Lumpur — worth an estimated RM1 to RM1.5 billion — under Section 51 of the Anti-Money Laundering Act (AMLA). The seizure was part of a widening probe into former finance minister Tun Daim Zainuddin over a 1997 corporate transaction worth RM2.3 billion involving Renong Bhd and United Engineers Malaysia (UEM), both linked to UMNO. MACC Chief Azam Baki confirmed the seizure. Daim denied any wrongdoing and maintained that the investigations were politically motivated. MACC also charged Daim and his wife Naimah Abdul Khalid for failing to declare assets, and issued notices to locate four of Daim’s children.

🔥 Why It Matters

On paper, investigating a former finance minister’s hidden wealth is exactly what an anti-corruption government should do. But the context matters: Daim was a key ally of Mahathir, who is now politically opposed to Anwar. Mahathir’s allies — Daim, various opposition figures — faced MACC scrutiny under Anwar’s watch, while Anwar’s own coalition partner Zahid had 47 charges dropped months earlier. The pattern is unmistakable: MACC goes hard on political opponents and soft on political allies. This isn’t anti-corruption — it’s the weaponization of prosecution.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The same MACC chief, Azam Baki, who aggressively pursued Daim is the same man who faced his own shareholding scandal and was protected by Anwar’s government. The same AG’s office that signed off on the Daim probe also signed off on dropping Zahid’s 47 charges. If MACC were genuinely independent, the public might celebrate the Daim investigation as a win. But when the anti-corruption agency’s chief is himself mired in controversy, and the government picks and chooses which corruption to prosecute based on political alignment, the whole exercise looks like state power dressed up as accountability.

📜 Previous Cases

Selective prosecution has a long pedigree in Malaysia. Under Najib, MACC was accused of targeting opposition figures while ignoring BN-linked corruption. Under Mahathir 2.0, UMNO leaders faced charges that conveniently served political goals. Under Anwar, the target list shifted again: Mahathir’s allies get raided, Anwar’s allies get DNAA. The ICIJ’s Pandora Papers investigation linked Daim to $52 million in offshore trusts — so there may be genuine substance to the probe. But the selective application of justice ensures that even legitimate investigations are tainted by political suspicion.

🎯 PH Callout

You want credit for going after Daim? Fine. Then explain why you dropped Zahid’s 47 charges three months earlier. Explain why Azam Baki — a man with his own asset scandals — is leading this probe. You cannot prosecute your opponents and protect your allies and call it Reformasi. The rakyat sees the pattern. Either everyone is subject to the law, or nobody is. Right now, it looks like the law only applies to people who are politically inconvenient to Anwar Ibrahim.

Sources: Al Jazeera | The Edge | The Star | ICIJ

item.title: [BM] AG Gugurkan Semua 47 Pertuduhan Terhadap Zahid Hamidi Tengah Perbicaraan — Anwar Kata 'Tidak Campur Tangan,' Tiada Siapa Percaya
item.description:
Said by: Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Deputy Prime Minister / Attorney General’s Chambers

Category: Politics

Date: 2023-09-04

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada 4 September 2023, Jabatan Peguam Negara memohon untuk menghentikan semua 47 pertuduhan rasuah terhadap Timbalan Perdana Menteri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi — 12 pertuduhan pecah amanah jenayah, 27 pengubahan wang haram, dan 8 rasuah melibatkan puluhan juta ringgit daripada Yayasan Akalbudi. Mahkamah Tinggi memberikan Pelepasan Tanpa Pembebasan (DNAA). Ini berlaku selepas Hakim Collin Lawrence Sequerah sudah memutuskan bahawa pihak pendakwaan berjaya membuktikan kes prima facie terhadap semua pertuduhan, dan perbicaraan sudah di peringkat pembelaan. Pendakwaan memetik 11 alasan termasuk keperluan ‘siasatan lebih menyeluruh.’ Perdana Menteri Anwar Ibrahim menafikan sebarang campur tangan, berkata ‘AG mempunyai kuasa untuk mengendalikan kes tersebut.’

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

Ini adalah pukulan paling merosakkan terhadap kredibiliti antirasuah PH sejak berkuasa. Zahid adalah presiden UMNO dan pengerusi BN — rakan gabungan paling penting Anwar. Tanpa kerusi parlimen UMNO, kerajaan perpaduan runtuh. Masa — tengah perbicaraan, selepas keputusan prima facie — tidak pernah berlaku dan luar biasa dari segi undang-undang. Majlis Peguam Malaysia mengambil langkah luar biasa memfailkan semakan kehakiman terhadap keputusan AG. Seluruh jenama politik Anwar dibina atas perjuangan menentang rasuah. Membiarkan rakan gabungannya terlepas dari 47 pertuduhan memusnahkan jenama itu dalam sehari.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Pertahanan Anwar ‘saya tidak campur tangan’ adalah helah paling lama dalam buku panduan naungan: anda tidak perlu angkat telefon apabila seluruh sistem faham bahawa TPM terlepas adalah syarat kelangsungan kerajaan anda. AG melapor kepada PM. PM perlukan Zahid. Pertuduhan digugurkan. Matematinya bercakap sendiri. PH menghabiskan sedekad menjerit tentang campur tangan AG di bawah Najib dan Muhyiddin — kini mereka mahu kita percaya pejabat yang sama bertindak secara bebas untuk membebaskan sekutu terpenting PM pada masa paling sesuai dari segi politik. Majlis Peguam tidak percaya. Rakyat tidak percaya. Satu-satunya yang percaya adalah mereka yang kelangsungan politik bergantung pada berpura-pura.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Ini mengikut corak tepat tradisi ‘ahli politik tidak boleh disentuh’ Malaysia. Pertuduhan 1MDB Najib difailkan selepas BN hilang kuasa; pertuduhan Zahid digugurkan selepas UMNO sertai PH. Di bawah Mahathir 2.0, tiada pemimpin UMNO didakwa dengan serius sehingga angin politik berubah. Peranan dwi AG — penasihat undang-undang kerajaan DAN pendakwa raya — telah menjadi mekanisme campur tangan politik dalam pendakwaan sejak merdeka. PH janji untuk memisahkan peranan ini. Pada 2023, mereka belum buat. Menjelang 2026, masih belum.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Korang kata korang berbeza. Korang kata Reformasi bermakna tiada lagi urus niaga politik, tiada lagi pemimpin kebal, tiada lagi pendakwaan terpilih. Lepas tu kerajaan korang bebaskan seorang lelaki yang hadapi 47 pertuduhan rasuah sebab korang perlukan kerusi partinya. Setiap ceramah, setiap sepanduk, setiap slogan tentang tadbir urus bersih — inilah hari ia mati. Korang tak boleh berkempen atas platform antirasuah dan kemudian berdagang pendakwaan untuk aritmetik parlimen. Rakyat ingat.

Sumber: Reuters | Al Jazeera | Malay Mail | CNA

item.title: AG Drops All 47 Charges Against Zahid Hamidi Mid-Trial — Anwar Says He 'Did Not Interfere,' Nobody Believes Him
item.description:
Said by: Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Deputy Prime Minister / Attorney General’s Chambers

Category: Politics

Date: 2023-09-04

📌 What Happened

On September 4, 2023, the Attorney General’s Chambers applied to discontinue all 47 corruption charges against Deputy Prime Minister Ahmad Zahid Hamidi — 12 counts of criminal breach of trust, 27 of money laundering, and 8 of bribery involving tens of millions from his Yayasan Akalbudi charitable foundation. The High Court granted a Discharge Not Amounting to Acquittal (DNAA). This was after presiding judge Justice Collin Lawrence Sequerah had already ruled that the prosecution had established a prima facie case on all charges, and the trial was already at the defence stage. The prosecution cited 11 reasons including the need for ‘more comprehensive investigations.’ Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim denied any involvement, saying ’the AG had the powers to handle the case.’

🔥 Why It Matters

This was the single most damaging blow to PH’s anti-corruption credibility since taking power. Zahid is the president of UMNO and chairman of Barisan Nasional — Anwar’s essential coalition partner. Without UMNO’s parliamentary seats, the unity government collapses. The timing — mid-trial, after a prima facie ruling — was unprecedented and legally extraordinary. The Malaysian Bar took the extraordinary step of filing a judicial review against the AG’s decision, calling it unjustifiable. Anwar’s entire political brand was built on fighting corruption. Letting his coalition partner walk free from 47 charges shattered that brand in a single day.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar’s ‘I did not interfere’ defence is the oldest trick in the patronage playbook: you don’t need to pick up the phone when the entire system understands that the deputy PM walking free is a condition of your government surviving. The AG reports to the PM. The PM needs Zahid. The charges were dropped. The math does the talking. PH spent a decade screaming about AG interference under Najib and Muhyiddin — now they want us to believe the same office acted independently to free the PM’s most important ally at the most politically convenient moment. The Malaysian Bar didn’t buy it. The public didn’t buy it. The only people who bought it are the ones whose political survival depends on pretending.

📜 Previous Cases

This follows the exact pattern of Malaysia’s ‘untouchable politician’ tradition. Najib’s 1MDB charges were filed after BN lost power; Zahid’s charges were dropped after UMNO joined PH. Under Mahathir 2.0, no UMNO leader faced serious prosecution until the political winds changed. The AG’s dual role — government legal adviser AND public prosecutor — has been the mechanism for political interference in prosecution since independence. PH promised to separate these roles. In 2023, they hadn’t done it. By 2026, they still haven’t.

🎯 PH Callout

You told us you were different. You told us Reformasi meant no more political deals, no more untouchable leaders, no more selective prosecution. Then your government freed a man facing 47 corruption charges because you needed his party’s seats. Every ceramah, every banner, every slogan about clean governance — this is the day it died. You don’t get to campaign on anti-corruption and then trade prosecutions for parliamentary arithmetic. The rakyat remembers.

Sources: Reuters | Al Jazeera | Malay Mail | CNA

item.title: [BM] SKMM Sekat Laman Media Pembangkang Sebelum Pilihan Raya Negeri — Fahmi Kata 'Saya Tak Beri Arahan,' RSF Panggil Ia Penapisan
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister / MCMC

Category: Politics

Date: 2023-08-07

📌 Apa Yang Berlaku

Pada Ogos 2023, menjelang enam pilihan raya negeri, SKMM Malaysia menyekat akses kepada sekurang-kurangnya empat laman berita pro-pembangkang — MalaysiaNow, UtusanTV, Malaysia Today, dan TV Pertiwi — serta blog Wee Choo Keong. Pengguna dialihkan ke alamat IP SKMM. Tiada satu pun media tersebut menerima amaran atau penjelasan tentang artikel mana yang mencetuskan sekatan. Menteri Komunikasi Fahmi Fadzil menafikan memberi sebarang arahan kepada SKMM, berkata ‘Saya tidak memberi arahan kepada SKMM untuk menyekat sesiapa.’ Wartawan Tanpa Sempadan (RSF) mengeluarkan kenyataan mengecam tindakan itu dan menggesa Fahmi menghentikan penapisan. Sekatan terhadap MalaysiaNow dan UtusanTV ditarik dalam beberapa hari selepas pilihan raya, tetapi Malaysia Today dan laman lain kekal disekat.

🔥 Kenapa Ini Penting

PH berkuasa pada PRU14 2018 sebahagiannya atas janji mengakhiri penindasan media. The Economist meletakkan Malaysia sebagai negara paling demokratik di Asia Tenggara tahun itu. Lima tahun kemudian, PH menyekat media pembangkang tepat sebelum pilihan raya — buku panduan tepat rejim BN yang mereka gantikan. Masanya tidak halus: sekat suara kritikal sebelum hari mengundi, buka semula sesetengah selepas keputusan keluar. Ini menandakan komitmen PH terhadap kebebasan akhbar adalah bersyarat — bebas apabila sesuai, disekat apabila tinjauan ketat.

💩 Kenapa Ini Karut

Pertahanan Fahmi ‘saya tak beri arahan’ adalah buku panduan penafian munasabah. SKMM berada di bawah kementeriannya. Sekatan berlaku semasa tempoh paling sensitif politik 2023. Jika Menteri Komunikasi tidak tahu siapa mengarahkan agensinya sendiri menyekat laman berita beberapa hari sebelum pilihan raya, dia sama ada tidak cekap atau berbohong — dan kedua-dua jawapan tidak boleh diterima. PH dalam pembangkang menjerit bila BN sekat media. PH dalam kerajaan kata ‘bukan salah saya, SKMM bertindak sendiri.’ Kemunafikan ini mengejutkan.

📜 Kes-Kes Sebelum Ini

Penindasan media adalah tradisi dwipartisan Malaysia. BN sekat The Malaysian Insider dan Sarawak Report. Kerajaan Muhyiddin menekan Al Jazeera dan menahan wartawan. PH janji mansuhkan Akta Mesin Cetak, Akta Hasutan, dan Seksyen 233 CMA. Tiada satu pun dimansuhkan. Sebaliknya, PH menggunakan undang-undang yang sama — kadang-kadang lebih agresif — daripada pendahulu mereka.

🎯 Panggilan Kepada PH

Korang janji kebebasan media. Korang janji hentikan penapisan SKMM. Korang janji kepada rakyat bahawa kerajaan PH tidak akan sekat laman berita untuk kepentingan politik. Lepas tu korang sekat empat laman tepat sebelum pilihan raya dan bagitahu kita bukan korang. Kalau kerajaan ‘reformasi’ korang tak boleh jamin wartawan boleh terbit tanpa disekat negara, korang bukan reformis — korang naluri autoritarian yang sama pakai jersi berbeza.

Sumber: The Diplomat | MalaysiaNow | Reuters | Civicus

item.title: MCMC Blocks Opposition Media Sites Before State Elections — Fahmi Says 'I Gave No Instruction,' RSF Calls It Censorship
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister / MCMC

Category: Politics

Date: 2023-08-07

📌 What Happened

In August 2023, in the lead-up to six state elections, Malaysia’s MCMC blocked access to at least four pro-opposition news sites — MalaysiaNow, UtusanTV, Malaysia Today, and TV Pertiwi — as well as blogger Wee Choo Keong’s site. Users were redirected to an MCMC IP address. None of the outlets received any warning or explanation of which articles triggered the blocks. Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil denied giving any instruction to MCMC, saying ‘I have given no instruction to MCMC to block anyone.’ Reporters Without Borders (RSF) issued a statement slamming the crackdowns and urging Fahmi to end such censorship. While blocks on MalaysiaNow and UtusanTV were lifted within days after the elections, Malaysia Today and other sites remained blocked long after.

🔥 Why It Matters

PH came to power in GE14 in 2018 partly on a promise to end media repression. The Economist ranked Malaysia the most democratic country in Southeast Asia that year. Five years later, PH is blocking opposition media right before elections — the exact playbook of the BN regimes they replaced. The timing was not subtle: block critical voices before voting day, unblock some after results are in. This signalled that PH’s commitment to press freedom was conditional — free when it suits them, restricted when the polls are tight.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Fahmi’s ‘I gave no instruction’ defence is the plausible deniability playbook. MCMC falls under his ministry. The blocks happened during the most politically sensitive period of 2023. If the Communications Minister doesn’t know who ordered his own agency to block news sites days before an election, he is either incompetent or lying — and neither answer is acceptable. PH in opposition screamed when BN blocked media. PH in government says ’not my fault, MCMC acted independently.’ The hypocrisy is breathtaking. RSF saw right through it. The international community saw right through it. Only Fahmi pretends there’s nothing to see.

📜 Previous Cases

Media suppression is a bipartisan Malaysian tradition. BN blocked The Malaysian Insider and Sarawak Report. Muhyiddin’s government pressured Al Jazeera and detained journalists. PH promised to repeal the Printing Presses and Publications Act, the Sedition Act, and Section 233 of the CMA. None have been repealed. Instead, PH has been using the same laws — sometimes more aggressively — than their predecessors, as documented by the US State Department’s 2023 human rights report on Malaysia.

🎯 PH Callout
